En: Oh god... (Try to breathe normally.) "This really wasn't the best idea, was it..."
Zh: “天啦…”（试着正常呼吸。）“这真心不是最好的主意，对吧…”

En: I deserve this. My body deserves to suffer for being this *weak* and *disappointing*.
Zh: “我自找的。我的身体就应该遭受这种*虚弱*和*失望*。”

En: Is that... Is that *blood* I'm seeing? (Rub your forehead.)
Zh: “那是…我看见的是*血*吗？”（摸摸自己的前额。）

En: What if... you just break it down?
Zh: 如果…你把它砸碎呢？

En: Walk it off, son! Stop wriggling like a maggot, it's nothing to worry about.
Zh: 走开，小子！别像只蛆虫一样扭扭捏捏了，根本没什么好担心的。

En: Maybe I *should* break it down? But not before I strategize...
Zh: 也许我*应该*把它砸开？但是我得先制定战略…

En: That's right, take in your surroundings. You need to have a solid ground and a proper posture if you want to succeed.
Zh: 没错，好好考虑你周围的环境。如果想要成功，你需要一个坚实的地面，还有一个合适的支点。

En: Smash into the door and shout: "Freeze, dirtbags!"
Zh: 用力撞门，然后大声吼叫：“不许动，你们这些坏人！”

En: Smash into the door and shout: "Hands up, now!"
Zh: 用力撞门，然后大声吼叫：“举起手来，现在！”

En: Smash into the door and shout: "Fuck, it hurts!"
Zh: 用力撞门，然后大声吼叫：“操，疼死了！”

En: A hollowed out, dark echo...
Zh: 一个空洞、黑暗的回音…

En: Touch the back of your neck: What is this feeling?
Zh: 摸摸自己的后颈：这是什么感觉？

En: Outside, the wind howls in from across the bay. The building at Rue de Saint-Ghislaine stands like a matchbox on its side, with men inside like little wooden sticks ready to catch fire.
Zh: 外面，风从海岸边呼啸而过。一栋火柴盒般的建筑矗立在圣吉莱纳路的旁边，里面的男人仿佛一根随时准备点燃自己的小火柴。

En: You smash into the wood and see a small crack appear on the door frame. It's going to take one more try to break through to the other side, but you've done it...
Zh: 你撞向木门，看见门框上出现了一个小小的裂缝。再试一次应该就能突破到另一边，但是你已经做到了…

En: Ow, shoot! Whose moronic idea was it to just *run through the door*? Don't you know that things like that *hurt*?!
Zh: 哎哟，该死！这是哪个白痴提的主意，说要*破门而入*？难道你不知道这样会*很疼*吗？！

En: Smash into the door again -- wordless.
Zh: 再撞一次门——什么话也不说。

En: Smash into the door and shout: "Welcome to Revachol!"
Zh: 用力撞门，然后大喊：“欢迎来到瑞瓦肖！”

En: You're not thinking of trying again, are you? The lieutenant stares at you as you stand there, silent and brooding.
Zh: 警督盯着站在那里的你，一言不发地沉思着。“你不会想再试一次吧？”

En: What are you doing, officer?! A voice rings from the other room. It sounds almost worried -- worried about your health.
Zh: 一个声音从另一个房间里传了出来：“你在做什么，警官？！”听起来好像很担心——担心你的健康。

En: Please don't cry, please don't cry... (Slap your face.) "I don't want anyone to see me cry!"
Zh: “不要哭，千万别哭…”（扇自己的脸。）“我不想被人看到我掉眼泪！”

En: Break down the door.
Zh: 把门撞开。

En: Preparation Phase Complete.
Zh: 准备阶段完毕。

En: The Destroyer!
Zh: 毁灭者！

En: One More Door.
Zh: 又是一扇门。

En: Broke into Cuno's parents' apartment.
Zh: 闯入坤诺父母的公寓。

En: Broke into the communard apartment.
Zh: 闯入公社成员的公寓。

En: After exerting some force you manage to turn the key. It's eerily silent. The door slides slightly open, letting a draft of cold air into the room.
Zh: 略施小力之后，你终于拧动了钥匙。里面安静地可怕。轻轻推开门，一股冷空气溜了进去。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The door shudders with your impact. Hinges snap, panels crack, splinters fly by as you smash through, tumbling into the darkness ahead.
Zh: 在你的撞击下，门不断震动着。突然，铰链折断，门板破裂，碎片飞溅，你猛的一下摔倒在眼前的黑暗之中。

En: Unlock the door with the key.
Zh: 用钥匙打开门。

En: [Open the door and enter.]
Zh: [打开门进去。]

En: Detective, you're the one in charge. The lieutenant motions toward the door.
Zh: “警探，你是负责人。”警督朝着门比了个手势。

En: Knock on the door.
Zh: 敲敲门。

En: You feel the tiny hairs on the back of your neck rise up, as if someone's standing right behind you.
Zh: 你感觉脖子后面的汗毛竖了起来，就好像此刻有人正站在你身后。

En: Uh, Kim? Maybe you should go first...
Zh: “呃，金？也许你应该先进去…”

En: Only an echo... No one is there.
Zh: 只有一声回音…里面没有人。

En: Are you alright? The lieutenant steps closer, his eyes soft and worried. "This looked... pretty intense and painful, I must admit."
Zh: “你没事吧？”警督靠近了几步，眼神柔和，略显担忧。“我必须承认，这个看起来…很猛烈，而且很痛苦。”

En: What is going *on* there?! an upset, high-pitched voice calls out from the bookstore. "Are you really trying to break down the back door?"
Zh: “到底*出什么事*了？！”书店里响起一个不安又尖锐的声音。“你真打算砸破后门吗？”

En: I warn you, don't tempt the spirits, officer! You can hear her take a frightened step backwards. "Don't tempt the spirits or you'll damage the holy *wards* on the door!"
Zh: “我警告你，别去招惹那些幽灵，警官！”你可以听见她惊恐地后退了一步。“别去招惹那些幽灵，不然你会破坏门上的神圣*守护*的！”

En: It barely looks like you've done any damage to the door, however -- it's still locked and closed, covered in dozens of little charms and trinkets.
Zh: 看起来你基本没对门造成什么伤害，不过——它仍然紧闭着，上面挂着许多小符咒和装饰品。

En: Smash into the door and shout: "Fuck the system!"
Zh: 用力撞门，然后大声吼叫：“去他妈的系统！”

En: First -- check your posture.
Zh: 首先——检查一下自己的姿势。

En: Check your surroundings.
Zh: 检查一下周围的环境。

En: Steady breathing, solid core -- you've got this. With one shoulder forward you're ready to smash into the door like a battering ram.
Zh: 稳住呼吸，沉住核心——你可以的。一只肩膀向前，你已经准备好像攻城槌一样撞击大门。

En: The room is dimly lit and littered with old barber shop rubbish, but the path to the door is clear.
Zh: 房间里光线昏暗，杂乱地堆放着旧理发店的垃圾，但是通往大门的道路十分干净。

En: And what about the door?
Zh: 那扇门怎么样？

En: It's made of a solid block of wood, but it has stood there for ages -- the hinges are old and coated with a carmine layer of rust. It should be doable.
Zh: 门是用一块坚实的木头做成的，不过已经上了年月——铰链十分破旧，上面还覆盖着一层红色的锈迹。这个方案应该是可行的。

En: An uneasy knot forms in the pit of your stomach...
Zh: 一种不舒服的感觉在你的胃部打了个结…

En: A panicking woman squeezes the pendant around her neck until it leaves a mark inside her palm. A man, a few metres away, stands frozen with a key in his hand. Somewhere inside a spider is spinning its web.
Zh: 一个惊慌失措的女人用力捏紧了脖子上的吊坠，直到吊坠在她掌心留下一个深深的印记。几米开外，一个男人手拿钥匙，僵硬地站在原地。里面的某个地方，蜘蛛正在勤劳的织网。

En: A heavy door with a missing handle stands before you, covered in dozens (if not hundreds) of little oddly-shaped trinkets and charms. It appears to be locked.
Zh: 你面前是一堵缺了个把手的沉重大门，上面挂着几十个（甚至上百个）形状古怪的小装饰品和符咒。看起来像是锁着的。

En: A heavy door with a missing handle stands before you, covered in dozens (if not hundreds) of Semenese trinkets and charms. It appears to be locked.
Zh: 你面前是一堵缺了个把手的沉重大门，上面挂着几十个（甚至上百个）形状古怪的塞美尼小装饰品和符咒。看起来像是锁着的。

En: You feel like you should get this one. Definitely. It's *important* somehow. There is something personal inside...
Zh: 你觉得自己应该买这本。绝对如此。不知道为什么，它就是*很重要*。里面有些很私人的东西…

En: What's it about?
Zh: 讲什么的？

En: High Speed Love chronicles the romance between two of the finest TipTop Tournée racers in history. One of them is the madcap driver Jacob Irw. His blond mane graces the cover.
Zh: “全速恋爱”讲的是历史上两名顶尖巡回赛車手之间的罗曼史。其中之一是狂妄車手雅各布·欧文。他的金色鬓毛为封面增色不少。

En: Next to Irw's life story, you see a slim biography of an Occidental rock star called "The Antistar". He's famous for shooting morphine into one of his eyeballs, and cocaine into the other!
Zh: 在欧文的传记旁边，你看见一本薄薄的奥西登摇滚明星传记，名叫“叛逆巨星”。他因为在一只眼睛里注射吗啡，另一只眼睛里注射可卡因而出名。

En: Next to that, Revacholian radio-personality Guillaume Bevy stands in front of a run down drug den. He's a permanent fixture on Channel 8, reporting on real life crime and ruining cops' days...
Zh: 在那旁边是瑞瓦肖的广播名人纪尧姆·贝维，站在一个贩毒窝点前面。他是8频道的不变招牌，专门报道现实生活中的犯罪，还有破坏警察的生活…

En: I really *must* insist you buy one of the books. You're interrupted by the shopkeep. "Reading them is not for free. Do still browse though. But not too *long*."
Zh: “我*必须*坚持你买一本书。”店主打断了你。“读书可不是免费的。不过请继续浏览。但是别*太久*就好。”

En: She understands she has erred against the customer and immediately corrects course.
Zh: 她明白自己得罪了顾客，立马纠正了自己的行为。

En: I'm sorry, I did not mean to rush you. You *are* browsing. Go ahead. Take your time. Time is commerce.
Zh: “很抱歉，我也不想催你。你*的确*是在浏览。请吧。别着急。时间就是生意。”

En: Storekeep, anything of note in this shelf?
Zh: “店主，这个书架上有什么值得注意的吗？”

En: I would say... The woman hums to herself. "'The Greatest Innocence.' Yes, most certainly. It's an important educational tool, delving into the depths of history, religion, and their relation to *innocentic power*."
Zh: “要我说的话…”女人低语到。“‘最伟大的无罪者’。没错，我很肯定。这是一本很重要的教学工具，对历史、宗教信仰，以及它们与*无罪政权*之间的联系进行了深入探究。”

En: A true cultural touchstone. She nods. "Enjoy the read."
Zh: “一个真正的文化试金石。”她点点头。“祝你阅读愉快。”

En: A very influential historical figure, but surely I don't have to tell you that. She waves her hand, as if casting aside the thought. "You're a law officer and law officers have at least *some* education."
Zh: “一个很有影响力的历史人物，这一点应该就不用我告诉你了吧。”她摆摆手，像是要把这个想法抛在一旁。“你是一个警官，执法人员至少都受过*一些*教育吧。”

En: Who or what is an innocence?
Zh: “无罪者是谁，或者是什么？”

En: The book is also very daring. The author aims to re-examine the universal understandings of the innocentic system, creating a fresh vantage point and a shift in the tired order of things.
Zh: “这本书也非常大胆。作者想要重新审视大众对无罪体系的普遍理解，创造一个全新的有利位置，改变让人厌倦的旧秩序。”

En: So you recommend it?
Zh: “所以你很推荐了？”

En: Certainly. It's prudent for a person to have at least an elementary understanding of history and society. Imagine the chaos we'd be in otherwise.
Zh: “当然。对于一个人来说，对历史和社会至少有一个初级的理解是很明智的。想象一下，如果不是这样，我们将会陷入怎样的混乱。”

En: I thought it was about which of these... innocences... is the coolest and greatest?
Zh: “我以为这是关于这些…无罪者当中…谁是最酷最伟大的？”

En: Perhaps for a layman! She scoffs. "Deep analysis is necessary to peel back the multi-layered meanings."
Zh: “对于一个外行来说也许是吧！”她嘲笑着。“要想层层剥开多层含义，深入分析是很有必要的。”

En: Do her words seem vague and abstract to you?
Zh: 在你看来，她的话语是不是很模糊，而且很抽象？

En: Suddenly, a particularly odd title catches your eye. It reads: "High Speed Love: the Tragic True Love Story of Jacob Irw and Alfie Deletraz" by one Cecilia Averbrook.
Zh: 突然间，一个特别古怪的标题吸引了你的注意。上面写着：“全速恋爱：雅各布·欧文与阿尔菲·德拉查斯的真实爱情悲剧”，作者是塞西莉亚·艾维布鲁克。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Look through the display of books.
Zh: 浏览陈列的书籍。

En: I'm interested in that 'Greatest Innocence' book.
Zh: “我对那本‘最伟大的无罪者’有点兴趣。”

En: The plaque on the shelf reads: "Biographies of Famous People." You see a large variety of names, none of which ring a bell.
Zh: 书架的饰板上写着：“名人传记。”你看见各种各样的名字，但是没有一个能勾起你的回忆。

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: Browsing through all the books with all their names makes your head spin. None of these seem important or relevant. It's all just vapid egoism!
Zh: 浏览所有这些书还有它们的书名，让你感到头晕目眩。没有一本看起来很重要，或者有什么关联。全都是些毫无趣味的利己主义！

En: Great. I don't need to know any more.
Zh: “很好。我也不用知道更多了。”

En: Shelves full of biographies of *famous people*, whoever they are.
Zh: 书架上堆满了*名人*传记，不管他们到底是谁。

En: You will attain a place on this shelf one day... or *die trying*!
Zh: 总有一天，你也会在那个书架上拥有一席之地…或者要努力到*至死方休*！

En: For playing with friends I'd recommend 'Suzerainty'. It's a civilization-building game where you build a civilization, then set off to brutally colonize and repress other civilizations. It'll cost 12 reál.
Zh: “如果跟朋友一起玩的话，我推荐‘大领主’。这是一个文明建设类游戏，你需要创建一种文明，然后进行野蛮殖民，镇压其他文明。价格是12雷亚尔。”

En: I don't want to think about this.
Zh: 我不想思考这个问题。

En: Then don't.
Zh: 那就不要。

En: Yes. Kids, friends, chicks. I have all those.
Zh: “是的。孩子，朋友，小妞。我都有。”

En: Then you're a lucky man, officer. Children are the greatest of treasures... She fiddles with her pendant, thinking.
Zh: “那你可真是个幸运的男人，警官。孩子是最大的财富…”她把玩着自己的吊坠，若有所思。

En: So what about all these 'Wirrâl' things?
Zh: “那一堆‘威勒尔’是什么？”

En: That price *is* steep, but then it's a Third Edition Mega-Setting Supplement, so it makes sense.
Zh: 这个价格*有点*不合理，不过毕竟是豪华配置增量套装第三版，所以也能说通。

En: A small mountain of colourful board game boxes. There are numerous types of games for all ages. A lot of shelf space seems to be taken up by Wirrâl-related merchandise.
Zh: 彩色的桌游盒堆成了一座小山，里面有各种各样为不同年龄段准备的游戏。书架的很多空间似乎都被威勒尔相关的商品占据了。

En: Look through the pile of Wirrâl-related items.
Zh: 浏览那堆跟威勒尔有关的商品。

En: Storekeep, what board games do you have here?
Zh: “店主，你这里都有什么桌游？”

En: I want to buy the *Suzerainty* game.
Zh: “我想买*大领主*游戏。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: I want to buy the *Wirrâl* game.
Zh: “我想买*威勒尔*游戏。”

En: Wonderful board games, sir. 'The Viticulturist' is a classic for sure, or perhaps you'd like 'Archipelagos of Insulinde', a very educational game for those interested in geography.
Zh: “很棒的桌游，先生。*葡萄种植者*肯定很经典，或者你也许会喜欢*伊苏林迪群岛*，一款为地理爱好者准备的教育游戏。”

En: 'Raubritter' is a fun game of economic competition, but can get quite intense after a while. We have games for the whole family. You can play with your children!
Zh: “*强盗贵族*是一款有趣的经济竞争游戏，不过玩着玩着就会变得很紧张。我们还有适合全家人一起玩的游戏。你可以跟自己的孩子一起玩耍！”

En: Lousy auras there! She shudders. "No, *role-playing games* are popular among those types... you know, who're into those kinds of things. Personally, I don't like it. Not at all!"
Zh: “那里有污秽的光环！”她颤抖着。“不，*角色扮演游戏*是其中最流行的…你知道的，大家喜欢这类东西。就我个人而言，我不喜欢。一点也不喜欢！”

En: Wow, I have children? A family?
Zh: 哇哦，我有孩子？有自己的家庭？

En: Do I have friends? (Look at the lieutenant.)
Zh: 我有朋友吗？（看着警督。）

En: Wonderful choice, sir. She smiles at you. "A wholesome *family* game."
Zh: “很棒的选择，先生。”她对你笑了笑。“一个有益身心健康的*家庭*游戏。”

En: If you say so. She gives you a curious glance. "But you better stay away from those immoral occult rituals."
Zh: “如果你这么说的话。”她好奇地看了你一言。“不过你最好离那些邪恶的神秘仪式远一点。”

En: Who are you going to play board games with? Do you have friends or family?
Zh: 你打算跟谁一起玩桌游呢？你有朋友或者家人吗？

En: It certainly feels like whatever you are will die with you.
Zh: 感觉就像是：不管你是谁，都会跟着你一起死去。

En: Are you actually friends? Or just colleagues thrown together by circumstance?
Zh: 你真的有朋友吗？或者只是在特定环境下偶尔聚集在一起的同事？

En: I don't feel as if I have any kids.
Zh: “我觉得自己好像没孩子。”

En: Friends are technically like family. She fiddles with her pendant, thinking.
Zh: “从严格意义上讲，朋友就像家人一样。”她把玩着吊坠，若有所思。

En: Look at me, who'd want to have children with me?
Zh: “看看我，你觉得谁愿意跟我生孩子？”

En: Don't be so hard on yourself, sir. You just need to clean up a bit. And technically, friends are a bit like family. She fiddles her pendant.
Zh: “别对自己这么苛刻，先生。你需要好好梳洗一下。而且从严格意义上讲，朋友也有点像家人。”她摆弄着自己的吊坠。

En: A towering mountain of colourful board game boxes and Wirrâl codices.
Zh: 堆得像山一样高的桌游盒，还有威勒尔法典。

En: I've heard they turn people into occult-enthusiasts. That they have rituals, where they try to summon entities. Highly immoral stuff... You can still buy them, though. She looks at the table, crossing her arms.
Zh: “我听说他们会把人变成神秘主义爱好者。他们还有某种仪式，用来召唤特殊的*存在*。都是特别邪恶的东西…不过，你要买还是可以的。”她看着桌子，抱紧双臂。

En: An endless variety of sourcebooks, lorebooks, and codices litter the table. The top-most book is titled "Welkin Compendium, Second Edition."
Zh: 桌子上散落着不同种类的资料书，攻略书和法典。最上面那本的标题是“精灵纲要，第二版。”

En: Hm, maybe I'm not into all this binoclard stuff.
Zh: 嗯，也许我对这些东西不太感兴趣。

En: There's a box that says: "Wirrâl. Third Edition Mega-Setting Supplements Module". The side panel notes: "A *fantastique* adventure board game. New maps and miniatures!" A sticker on it displays -- 25 reál.
Zh: 有一个盒子上面写着：“威勒尔。豪华配置增量套装第三版”。侧面注释着：“一部*幻想*冒险桌游。新地图，新模型！上面贴着一个标签——25雷亚尔。”

En: Anything that really catches my eye?
Zh: 有什么特别吸引我目光的吗？

En: There's also a large hardbound tome with intricate cover art: "The Hunters of Katuak: Boreal Creature Compendium," and a pick-your-path adventure gamebook titled "Tales of Wirrâl: Cavern of Velkraag."
Zh: 还有一本大型精装巨著，封面图片特别复杂：“卡塔克猎人：北方生物纲要”，还有一本*自由路线选择*冒险游戏书，名字是：“威勒尔传说：沃克拉格山洞”。

En: Nonsense for anemic binoclards!
Zh: 对于贫血四眼仔来说，这都是废话！

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: Books in a *board game* section? Who wants to read books!?
Zh: 把书放在*桌游*区域？谁愿意看书啊！？

En: Steal the guide to cockatoos.
Zh: 偷走凤头鹦鹉指南。

En: Angry storekeeper stare-down.
Zh: 愤怒的店主盯的你不敢看她。

En: You don't have behavioural problems. That's garbage. You're cool.
Zh: 你没有行为问题。那些都是废话。你没什么事。

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: A sulphur-crested cockatoo sits on the cover, its beak slightly open. It looks as if the bird is calling out the book title: "From A to Zrieek! A Guide To A Well-Behaved Cockatoo."
Zh: 一只硫磺色羽冠的凤头鹦鹉坐在封面上，嘴部微张。似乎这只鸟正在呼唤着书名：“从0到1！凤头鹦鹉驯养指南。”

En: Flip through the pages.
Zh: 翻阅书页。

En: What about this guide to cockatoos, storekeeper?
Zh: “这本凤头鹦鹉指南怎么样，店主？”

En: It's a must-have if you own a cockatoo. I've heard they're quite capricious.
Zh: “如果你有凤头鹦鹉的话，那这本就是必需品了。我听说它们挺反复无常的。”

Actor: From A to Zrieek! A Guide To a Well-Behaved Cockatoo
En: Turns out that there are so many different cockatoo species, and they all have behavioural problems.
Zh: 结果居然有这么多不同种类的凤头鹦鹉，而且都有行为问题。

En: I want to buy the guide to cockatoos.
Zh: “我想买这本凤头鹦鹉指南。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Oh, a nature enthusiast! Good choice. I *knew* it was a good idea to keep that around...
Zh: “哦，一个自然爱好者！选的好。我就*知道*把它留在附近是个好主意…”

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: You wait for the storekeeper to be distracted. When she's not looking, you haul the tome of cockatoos into your pocket.
Zh: 你在等待店主分心。当她没看到的时候，你把凤头鹦鹉指南塞进了自己的口袋。

En: Slowly, you move your hand toward the tome of cockatoos. Before you reach the book, you're interrupted:
Zh: 你慢慢把手伸向凤头鹦鹉指南。还没碰到书，你就被打断了：

En: It's quite a challenge, but eventually the guide to cockatoos is yours. Splendid effort!
Zh: 这是个不小的挑战，不过最终凤头鹦鹉指南是你的了。多么辉煌的成就！

En: Sir, no touching the merchandise. People like their books pristine, not handled by god knows who.
Zh: “先生，请不要乱碰商品。大家都喜欢崭新的书，而不是被天知道什么样的人碰过的书。”

En: The lieutenant coughs sharply, then whispers: "Do we really need to do this? I think not."
Zh: 警督用力咳了几声，然后低语到：“我们真要这么做吗？我觉得还是不要了吧。”

En: Sex crimes *did* pique your interest, but still -- this Dick Mullen person only gets your pleasure centres *down*.
Zh: 性犯罪*确实*勾起了你的兴趣，不过——这个叫迪克·马伦的人只会*降低*你的快感中心。

En: NOTE! Reading about Dick Mullen will not actually make you a better detective.
Zh: 注意！看迪克·马伦的书不能真让你变成一个更好的警探。

En: Furthermore -- they have NO IDEA how hard it is to simply remove a body from a tree.
Zh: 而且——他们根本不知道把一具尸体从树上弄下来到底有多艰难。

En: Relax. It's not that bad. Also, this means your bloodflow is returning to normal. Good news, really.
Zh: 放松点。其实也没那么糟。而且，这就意味着你的血流正在恢复正常。这是好消息，真的。

En: These shelves are overburdened with books from the same series. You see the name "Dick Mullen" over and over.
Zh: 这个书架被同一系列的书压得喘不过气来。你一遍又一遍地看见“迪克·马伦”这个名字。

En: Storekeep, what's all this crime fiction?
Zh: “店主，这是什么犯罪小说？”

En: Look through the display of books.
Zh: 浏览陈列的书籍。

En: Who is Dick Mullen?
Zh: 迪克·马伦是谁？

En: Dresscode: Modus Mullen.
Zh: 着装要求：模式马伦。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: What do we have here? Mistaken identity...
Zh: 看看这里有些什么？错误的身份…

En: Shelves filled to the brim with crime novels featuring the supposedly stalwart Vespertine detective, Dick Mullen.
Zh: 这个书架堆满了犯罪小说，主角是忠诚的维斯珀裔警探——迪克·马伦。

En: Oh, crime, robberies, murders... She lowers her voice. "Even *sexual* crimes. We're fortunate to have Dick Mullen and his stories to sort all that out."
Zh: “哦，犯罪，抢劫，凶杀…”她压低声音。“甚至还有*性犯罪*。我们很幸运，还能有迪克·马伦和他的故事来解决这些问题。”

En: You see: "Dick Mullen on the Job", "Get Me Mullen!", "The Stalwart Adventures of Richard P. Mullen", "Dick Mullen and the Murder in the Orchard", "The Sordid Affair of Dick Mullen"...
Zh: 你看见：“出击：迪克·马伦”，“马伦登场！”，“理查德·P·马伦的华丽冒险”，“迪克·马伦与奥查德杀手”，“迪克·马伦之肮脏勾当”…

En: A Killing Is Declared, "Dick Mullen in the Murderhouse", "The Final Case of Dick Mullen" (an obvious lie), "Dick Mullen in the Clock Tower", "The Ordeals of Dick Mullen", "Dauntless Dick", "Dick Mullen's Funeral Pyre", "The Murder of Dick Mullen"...
Zh: “杀人宣告”，“迪克·马伦之凶宅惊魂”，“迪克·马伦——最后一案”（明显就是骗人的），“迪克·马伦之钟楼怪谈”，“迪克·马伦之残酷考验”，“无畏迪克”，“迪克·马伦之火葬”，“迪克·马伦谋杀案”…

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: Gosh, why? One civilian looks on, amazed. The bald man bellows a reply:
Zh: “天啊，为什么？”一位目睹市民惊叹地说。秃头男子低头吼道：

En: You're a... a police officer... apparently. You should buy *all* of these. They really do teach a person how to be a proper detective.
Zh: “很明显，你是一个…警官——所以你应该*全部*买下来。它们真的能教会你如何做个合格的警官。”

En: Crime fiction... is a disgrace. An asinine misrepresentation of the physical attributes and the arduous everyday work of actual police officers.
Zh: 犯罪小说…真是丢人现眼。这是对现实警官的身体素质以及艰苦日常工作的愚蠢扭曲和误传。

En: Sure thing.
Zh: 当然了。

En: Oh god, no.
Zh: 天啦，不。

En: Dick Mullen *dies*?!
Zh: 迪克·马伦*死了*吗？！

En: Oh no, turns out he faked it to solve a case.
Zh: 哦，不是的，他只是为了破案伪装的。

En: Are there any more?
Zh: 还有更多吗？

En: Okay, let's stop now.
Zh: 好吧，现在就打住。

En: Yes. There's also: "The Dame Who Did It", "Farewell, My Mullen" (faking death seems to be a common trope in the Mullen series), "The Morbid Tales of Dick Mullen", "A Dark Tide Turns"...
Zh: 是的。还有：“罪恶女爵士”，“再见了，我的马伦”（在马伦系列里，假死好像挺常见的），“迪克·马伦之恐怖传说”，“暗潮汹涌”…

En: Tragedy Calls for Dick Mullen (another one with fake death), and, of course, "Dick Mullen: The Murderer", in order to catch a murderer, Dick Mullen must *become* the murderer.
Zh: “迪克·马伦之悲剧的召唤”（他又装死了），当然还有，“迪克·马伦：谋杀犯”（为了抓住凶手，迪克·马伦必须*变成*一个杀人犯。）

En: Come on, this is *not* the way real police solve crimes. The *real* police are some twenty kilometres away, sitting in an armoured motor carriage.
Zh: 拜托，警察根本*不是*这样破案的好吗。*真正*的警察大概还在20公里开外的地方，坐在装甲汽車里呢。

En: Your quick eye notices a small caterpillar crawling across the spine of a book. The title reads: "Dick Mullen and the Mistaken Identity."
Zh: 你的目光敏锐地捕捉到一只小毛毛虫，它正在一本书脊上爬行。书的标题是：“迪克·马伦与错误身份”。

En: Your attempt to grasp at the answer fails... it seems very close by, pulsating just out of reach.
Zh: 你试着抓住答案，但却徒劳无功…它好像非常接近，就在你刚好够不着的地方脉动着。

En: After all this, you still haven't found the answer to the one question that matters: Who *is* Dick Mullen?
Zh: 经历了这一切，你还是没有找到那个最关键问题的答案：迪克·马伦*是*谁？

En: These books *greatly* overstate the excitement of police work, glossing over how long it takes to actually follow up on leads and eliminate dead-ends.
Zh: 这些书*严重*夸大了警察工作的乐趣，掩盖了警察到底要花多少时间跟踪线索、打破案件僵局的事实。

En: Not a single mention of all the stress this work creates upon the officer's *family*. Detective fiction just doesn't tell the truth at all. Now, would you like a list of the books found on the shelf?
Zh: 而且根本没有提到这份工作给警察的*家人*带来的压力。侦探小说根本没有陈述事实。现在，你想要一份在书架上发现的所有书籍的清单吗？

En: What's more, they completely ignore the psychological hardships of, year after year, coming into contact with people during the worst days of their lives.
Zh: 还有，年复一年，警察要在人们生命中最糟糕的日子与他们接触，那种心理上的苦楚，都被小说完全忽略了。

En: A couple spook novels hide amidst all the detective books. Thrilling tales of spycraft and derring-do.
Zh: 几本谍战小说隐藏在一大堆侦探小说中间，讲的是惊心动魄的间谍活动和惊险大胆的冒险故事。

En: Come on, Chester, tell the story again. A bald man turns toward a lean man and pats him on the back.
Zh: 一个秃顶的男人转向一个高瘦的男人，拍拍他的背。“来吧，切斯特，再说一遍那个故事。”

En: Again? Man, I tell that one at least once a month. It's not that interesting, Chester replies.
Zh: “又来？伙计，我每个月至少要讲一次。其实真没什么意思。”切斯特回应到。

En: The *fuck* it is, the bald man replies. "And these guys haven't heard it." He motions to the civilian sitting in the back seat. "You see, Chester here," he pokes his finger at the lean man. "Chester *faked his own death* once!"
Zh: “少*他妈*废话，”秃顶男人回应到。“这些家伙还没听过呢。”他向坐在后面的市民示意。“你知道吗，这位切斯特，”他用手指戳了戳高瘦的男人。“切斯特曾经*假死*过呢！”

En: Yeah, sure came close, Chester mutters in return, then turns to the rapt listener. "Alright, so I was tailing this guy called 'Francis the Shoe'..." The inside of the motor carriage is thick with cigarette smoke. Outside, it starts to rain...
Zh: “是啊，其实很接近了，”切斯特嘟囔着作为回应，然后转向全神贯注的听众。“好吧，所以我在跟踪一个叫‘快脚弗朗西斯’的人…”汽車里面充斥着香烟的烟雾。汽車外面，雨滴开始坠落…

En: A very fuckin' dangerous case, ain't that right, Chester? They almost got you that time.
Zh: “天啦，为什么？”一个听众惊讶地睁大双眼——秃顶男人回复到：“真他妈是个危险的案子，是吧，切斯特？他们那次差点就抓住你了。”

En: Hrm, of course. She shudders. "Such violence and immorality... uh, I mean, enjoy!"
Zh: “呃，当然。”她耸耸肩。“特别暴力，而且有伤风化…呃，我是说，请慢慢享受！”

En: I want to buy this Dick Mullen novel.
Zh: “我想买迪克·马伦的小说。”

En: A worn paperback from Dick Mullen's classic *hard-boiled* phase. The premise seems to be that Dick Mullen is framed for the murder of his best friend, and has just a few days to prove his innocence.
Zh: 一本迪克·马伦*硬汉*时期的经典平装书。前情好像是迪克·马伦被诬陷谋杀了自己最好的朋友，只剩下几天可以证明自己的清白。

En: Hmm. Why does this speak to me?
Zh: 嗯。我为什么对这个有种奇怪的感觉？

En: Could it be the motifs of unstable identities and shocking betrayals?
Zh: 也许因为主题是不停变幻的身份还有令人震惊的背叛吗？

En: That's probably right, I'm a complicated guy, full of contradictions.
Zh: 有这个可能，我本身就是个很复杂的人，充满各种矛盾。

En: No, it's gotta be something *else*.
Zh: 不，一定有什么*别的*原因。

En: Perhaps you're drawn to the dark and *noirish* atmosphere?
Zh: 也许是你被黑暗和*危险*的气氛吸引了？

En: Yeah, I'm pretty intense. I'm all about that dark stuff.
Zh: 是啊，还蛮强烈的。我一向喜欢黑暗的东西。

En: No, that's not it either.
Zh: 不，也不是这样的。

En: Is it because the smoking dame in the slinky red dress on the cover is giving you a hard-on?
Zh: 是因为封面上那个穿着紧身红裙抽烟的女人让你兴奋了吗？

En: Oh, right. That's definitely it.
Zh: 哦，没错。绝对是这个原因。

En: See the way she's suggestively holding that pistol? That's *symbolism*.
Zh: 看见她那很有暗示性的握枪方式了吗？那就是*象征*。

En: Yeah, this is the book for you.
Zh: 是啊，这就是属于你的那本书。

En: Then this is the book for you.
Zh: 那这本书就是你的菜了。

En: What? No. That's not what's happening.
Zh: 什么？不，不可能的。

En: Yes it is. The bulge in your pants is embarrassingly obvious.
Zh: 就是这样的。你裤子中间的凸起显眼的让人尴尬。

En: Quit staring -- You're in public! There's plenty of time for that later... in the privacy of your own room.
Zh: 别盯着看了——你这是在公众场合！之后时间还多着呢…等你回到自己的房间之后再说。

En: Something the matter, detective?
Zh: “出什么事了吗，警探？”

En: Nope, all good here. (Casually adjust your pants.)
Zh: “没，一切顺利。”（随便调整一下自己的裤子。）

En: Kim, would you describe this woman as a *smoking dame*?
Zh: “金，你会形容她是一个*吸烟女郎*吗？”

En: The lieutenant nods. You think you detect the faintest trace of a grin on his face, but you can't say for certain.
Zh: 警督点点头。你觉得自己隐约察觉到他脸上那丝稍纵即逝的笑容，不过你也不太确定。

En: The lieutenant narrows his eyes and contemplates the book for a moment.
Zh: 警督眯起眼睛，盯着那本书沉思了一会儿。

En: I would certainly say she fits that description, providing that's your type.
Zh: “我确实觉得她很符合那个描述，如果她是你中意的类型的话。”

En: Wait a minute, all this told us *nothing* about who Dick Mullen actually is...
Zh: 等等，对于迪克·马伦到底是谁这个问题，这些没有透露给我们*任何*信息…

En: Sssssire, you could learn much from thisss Mullen, if you underssstood who he issss....
Zh: 大人，您可以从这位马伦身上学到很多东西，如果您能明白他是谁的话…

En: Yeah, actually... this is bullshit.
Zh: 是啊，其实…这都是废话。

En: Okay... you're one of the initiated. Then go ahead and fiddle with the curtains. But do so at your own PERIL.
Zh: 好吧…你是先行者之一。那就别管了，尽情玩弄那扇窗帘吧。不过，后果自负。

En: Oddly enough, the more she tries to draw you away from the curtains, the more alluring they become.
Zh: 说来也怪，她越是想让你不靠近窗帘，它们就变得越来越诱人。

En: Okay. So what's in there has a will and it *wants* you to enter? This is bad.
Zh: 好吧。所以那里面有一种意志力，它*想要*你进去？这怕是不太妙啊。

En: The curtains do seem *frail* suddenly. Not robust enough to contain a slippery darkness.
Zh: 突然间，窗帘变得非常*脆弱*。不够强壮的它根本无法控制住狡猾的黑暗。

En: Lies. Rip them open, we say!
Zh: 谎言。吾辈会说，把它们撕开！

En: Aside from poking at it -- suspiciously -- there is nothing else to do with the fish-head charm at this time. The curtains remain shut before you.
Zh: 此时此刻，除了怀疑地戳一戳之外，已经没什么可以对鱼头符咒做的了。你面前的窗帘还是紧紧闭合着。

En: There *is* something mysterious about the curtains... be careful!
Zh: 这幅窗帘*的确*有些神秘的地方…小心点！

En: You see some kind of charm -- an irregular polyhedron, assembled from bones, sticks, and straw. Inside, a disturbing fish-head with empty eye sockets stares at you.
Zh: 你看见某种符咒——那是一个不规则的多面体，由骨头、树枝和稻草组成。在那里面，一个令人不安的鱼头正用空洞的眼眶盯着你。

En: From the looks of it this is a traditional Semenese ward, meant to provide protection against ill luck, bad dreams, curses, and other supra-natural scourges.
Zh: 从外观来看，这是一种传统的塞美尼守护，专门用来抵御厄运，噩梦，诅咒，还有其他超自然灾祸。

En: (Pull open the curtains.)
Zh: （拉开窗帘。）

En: What do you see? the lieutenant asks, whispering conspiratorially.
Zh: “你看见什么了？”警督神秘兮兮地问到。

En: Examine the strange cage-like trinket.
Zh: 检查那个像笼子一样的奇怪装饰。

En: Ignore the curtains for now. [Leave.]
Zh: 暂时不管那个窗帘。[离开。]

En: You see a set of tattered curtains blocking the way to another room. A strange cage-like trinket dangles from the curtains.
Zh: 你看见一面破破烂烂的窗帘挡住了通往另一个房间的路，上面还挂着些像笼子一样奇怪的装饰品。

En: She's right -- those curtains give off some *eerie vibes*. Better not mess with it.
Zh: 她是对的——那些窗帘发散出*诡异的波动*，最好不要乱动。

En: I warned you! You are unleashing forces beyond your understanding!
Zh: “我警告你！你这样做会释放超出你理解能力之外的力量！”

En: We must be dealing with seriously malign forces here if there are *dozens* of wards.
Zh: 如果这里有*很多*守护的话，我们对付的一定是极端邪恶的力量。

En: Just as you're about to pull apart the curtains, the petrified voice of the shop owner cries out once more:
Zh: 你正要拉开窗帘，店主那惊讶的声音再次响起：

En: What are you, a coward? Think about the marvellous adventures waiting for us on the other side!
Zh: 怎么回事，你是胆小鬼吗？想想另一边，有多么不可思议的冒险正在等待着我们。

En: The room behind them is dim... You can only discern shadowy silhouettes of *things* there, crouching in the dark like monsters in a child's bedroom.
Zh: 窗帘后的房间很昏暗……你只能隐约辨认出某种*东西*的模糊阴影，就像蜷缩在孩童卧室里那些黑暗角落的野兽。

En: Honestly, I can't really see anything. It looks like a regular back room to me.
Zh: “老实说，我什么也看不清。不过在我看来，就是个普通的房间而已。”

En: I see, the lieutenant nods, before looking at the shopkeeper: "Quick, she's turning around..."
Zh: “我明白了，”警督点点头，然后看向店主：“快点，她转过去了…”

En: Excuse me, officer... Plaisance calls out from the counter. "The back room is strictly for employees only."
Zh: “不好意思，警官…”柜台附近的普莱桑斯大声呼喊。“后面的房间只允许雇员出入。”

En: Excuse me, officer... She calls out from the counter. "The back room is strictly for employees only."
Zh: “不好意思，警官…”柜台后的她大声呼喊着。“后面的房间只允许雇员出入。”

En: Dark isles? What's going on?
Zh: 黑暗岛屿？怎么回事？

En: Do you realize what's out there? Upon the mist-shrouded islands of Ile du Fantôme, in Semenine? Eerie pygmies and wild shamans practising unnatural mysticism?
Zh: 你有没有意识到外面有什么？塞美尼那迷雾环绕的幽灵岛上到底有什么？古怪的侏儒族，还有使用超自然秘术的疯狂萨满？

En: Yes, I've *sensed* that. It's terrifying.
Zh: 是的，我能*感觉*到。真的很恐怖。

En: They lurk in the tropical pale of Semenine, casting strange magicks on everyone! Ignore the curtains -- or suffer the consequences...
Zh: 它们潜伏在塞美尼的热带灰域区，对所有人施展奇怪的法术！不要管那个窗帘——否则你将自食其果…

En: The curtains, tattered with age and covered in dust, hang before you. As if taunting you...
Zh: 你面前挂着一幅被岁月摧残过的破烂窗帘，上面满是灰尘，仿佛在嘲弄你…

En: You see a tattered set of curtains and a polyhedron-shaped cage-like trinket.
Zh: 你看见一面破破烂烂的窗帘，还有个像笼子一样的多面体装饰品。

En: You're out of line.
Zh: 你有些出格了。

En: Not so! You sense their strange magicks, cast from the dark forests they writhe around in. Don't believe it? Go ahead, straight through the curtains. Suffer the consequences.
Zh: 不是这样的！你能感觉到它们奇异的魔力，那些在黑暗森林周围蠕动的不祥魂灵。你不相信吗？那就去吧，直直地穿过窗帘，承受一切后果。

En: I prefer to just not think about it...
Zh: 我宁愿不去想它…

En: What's so eerie about those curtains?
Zh: 这些窗帘有什么可怕的？

En: Be wary of those mysterious ornaments from the *dark isles*.
Zh: 小心那些来自*黑暗岛屿*的神秘装饰。

En: What's the hang-up? Mystical spirits, unnatural emanations -- nothing a para-detective can't handle. Let's go!
Zh: 有什么好烦恼的？神秘幽灵，超自然波动——没有什么是超能警探对付不了的。我们走吧！

En: Wait... What?
Zh: 等等…什么？

En: So the Remote Viewers Division... is real? I am it. And I am up to the task!
Zh: 所以遥视者部队…是真的了？我就是其中一员，而且我能胜任这个任务！

En: Shopkeeper, what's behind the curtains?
Zh: “店主，窗帘背后是什么？”

En: And who are the Semenese?
Zh: 塞美尼人是谁？

En: Inhabitants of Ile du Fantôme, the Semenine Islands down south...
Zh: 南部的塞美尼群岛，幽灵岛的居民。

En: (Rip them apart, this time for real.)
Zh: （把它们掀开，这次是动真格的了。）

En: Sir, don't touch that! I told you it's off-limits for the customers! Her hand has closed around her pendant, her fingers nervously playing with the talisman.
Zh: “先生，别碰它！我告诉过你顾客是禁止进入的！”她紧握吊坠，手指紧张地摆弄着护身符。

En: I've heard there's a fridge there that I need.
Zh: “我听说那里有台我需要的冰箱。”

En: Everybody suddenly *needs* something from there.... She waves her hands angrily. "Leave the curtains be. It's what it wants..."
Zh: “所有人都会突然*需要*一些东西…”她愤怒地挥挥手。“别碰那个窗帘。它希望如此…”

En: You *do*?! She grabs her pendant again, visibly shaken. "My god, even more reasons to not mess with the curtains... Just step away, dear sir!"
Zh: “你*有吗*？！”她又抓紧了吊坠，明显有些动摇。“天啦，那就更不能去碰窗帘了…先生，麻烦你离开那里！”

En: But I sense this place calling for me. I must investigate *beyond* the threshold!
Zh: “但是我感觉到这个地方在呼唤我。我必须*跨越*这道门槛，彻底调查清楚。”

En: Ma'am, this is different -- I'm a police officer, I *need* to get in there.
Zh: “夫人，这不太一样——我是个警察，我*需要*进去。”

En: You see a dimly lit room full of dusty furniture and trash. A doorway stands in the back, covered in dozens of scary little cage-like trinkets, your shadow looming over it like an omen.
Zh: 你看见一个昏暗的房间，里面堆满灰尘扑扑的家具和垃圾。后面有一个出入口，上面挂了许多笼子形状的可怕装饰品——你的阴影笼罩在那上面，像是某种预兆。

En: Why? It's not like anyone was *killed* there! She stops abruptly as her hand flies over her mouth, baffled by her own bluntness.
Zh: “为什么？又不是说那里有人*被杀*了！”她突然停了下来，手指飞快掠过自己的嘴唇，像是在为自己的率直感到迷惑。

En: Nothing! Now please go back to browsing books. She fiddles with her pendant. "Don't you feel *compelled* to look at the books? The books are all you care about!"
Zh: “没什么！你还是继续回去看书吧。”她把玩着吊坠。“难道你没感觉到*必须*去看那些书吗？书才是你唯一需要关心的东西！”

En: Nothing! Why aren't you browsing the books? She fiddles with her pendant. "Don't you feel *compelled* to look at the books? You ought to."
Zh: “没什么！你为什么不看书呢？”她把玩着吊坠。“难道你没感觉到*必须*去看那些书吗？你本来应该如此啊。”

En: A faint slice of light from the curtains reveals a doorway in the back, draped in cage-like polyhedrons. They are all made of fish heads and tiny animal bones; it looks like a small graveyard...
Zh: 窗帘后透出一丝微弱的光，揭露出后面的一个门道，上面挂着笼子形状的多面体。多面体是用鱼头和小动物骨头做成的，看起来很像一个小型坟场…

En: Inhabitants of Ile du Fantôme or the Semenine Islands down south. Don't listen to what Inland Empire told you -- it's a beautiful place with a rich and unique culture...
Zh: 幽灵岛和塞美尼南部岛屿的居民。别听本心的鬼话——那是个美丽的地方，拥有丰富而独特的文化………

En: Do you know what takes place upon mist-shrouded Semenese islands? Don't listen to Encyclopedia -- they're inhabited by shamans and wildmen practising unnatural mysticism.
Zh: 你知道烟雾缭绕的塞美尼岛上发生了什么吗？别听博物的——上面住满了萨满，还有使用超自然秘法的疯子。

En: Para-psychologically speaking -- we're *done* if you decide to open them. I won't be held responsible for the consequences! It's too dangerous!
Zh: “从超心理的角度来说——如果你决定打开它，那我们就*完蛋*了。我是不会对那个后果负责的！实在是太危险了！”

En: She looks away, mumbling: "Why is everyone *always* messing with the curtains, why can't they just buy books like normal people..."
Zh: 她移开目光，嘟囔着：“为什么每个人*总是*喜欢摆弄窗帘，为什么就不能像普通人一样买些书呢…”

En: I see *monsters* crouching in the dark.
Zh: “我看见*野兽*蜷缩在黑暗里。”

En: I see more of those little trinkets. It looks pretty insane.
Zh: “我看见了更多小装饰品。看起来简直太疯狂了。”

En: There's a door in the back. Wonder where it leads...
Zh: “后面有一扇门。不知道会通往什么地方…”

En: No, what do you *really* see?
Zh: “不，你*到底*看见什么了？”

En: Spirits, from the darkest realms, rising to greet us...
Zh: “幽灵，来自至暗国度，前来迎接我们的…”

En: You're hopeless, he sighs. "Quick, she's turning around..."
Zh: “你没救了，”他叹了一口气。“快点，她要转过来了…”

En: What are you doing there, officers? The shopkeeper turns around right as you manage to close the curtains. "I told you not to play with the curtains... Please browse the books, sir!"
Zh: “你们在做什么呢，警官们？”你刚把窗帘一拉上，书店主人就转过身来。“我告诉过你，别碰窗帘…麻烦看书去好吗，先生！”

En: Spooky, the lieutenant comments. "Quick, she's turning around..."
Zh: “可怕，”警督评论到。“快点，她要转过来了…”

En: To the cellar? the lieutenant suggests. "Quick, she's turning around..."
Zh: “通往地窖吗？”警督暗示到。“快点，她要转过来了…”

En: I am sorry, I don't mean to be so impolite, just please don't go there! I can't allow that. You'll only make things worse and unleash *the powers*.
Zh: “很抱歉，我也不想这么没礼貌，请别进去！这一点我绝不允许。你只会让事情变得更糟糕，会把*力量*释放出来的。”

En: I don't care, you can't stop me! I'll open them. (Continue.)
Zh: “我不在乎，你阻止不了我！我偏要打开。”（继续。）

En: No! She raises her hand to try and stop you. "Please just talk to me, officer... Come here and let's talk this through before you decide to do anything *extreme*..."
Zh: “不！”她举起手，想要阻拦你。“请跟我聊聊，警官…过来吧，在你决定做出什么*极端的*事情之前，我们先好好谈谈……”

En: This is about *the curse*? That's why you're afraid?
Zh: “是关于*诅咒*的事吗？这就是你害怕的原因？”

En: No, it's just a store room for the employees, I told you! Now *please* step away from the curtains... She's almost begging you.
Zh: “不，那里只是雇员使用的储藏室，我已经告诉过你了！现在，*请*离窗帘远一点…”她几乎是在求你了。

En: Talking is always good. Go see what she has to say.
Zh: 她唯一想要的就是你远离窗帘，即便只是一小会就好。也许可以先跟她聊聊？

En: Alright, I'll think about it for a while... (Continue.)
Zh: “好吧，我再稍微考虑下…”（继续。）

En: Thank you! The shopkeeper lets out a loud sigh. "Let's just talk about this first, alright? There's no reason for you to venture into the unknown..."
Zh: “谢谢你！”店主大声地叹了口气。“我们先好好聊聊，行吗？你没有理由冒险进入未知的…”

En: She speaks almost as if she's trying to put a *spell* on you, urging you to buy more books...
Zh: 她说话的方式几乎像是要对你*下咒*一样，催促你多买些书…

En: You see a set of tattered curtains blocking the way to another room. A strange cage-like trinket dangles from the curtains.
Zh: 你看见一面破破烂烂的窗帘，挡住了通往另一个房间的路。窗帘上还挂了些笼子形状的装饰品。

En: Pull the curtains open.
Zh: 把窗帘拉开。

En: [Ignore the curtains.]
Zh: [忽略窗帘。]

En: You see a dimly lit room full of dusty furniture and trash. A doorway stands in the back, covered in dozens of scary little cage-like trinkets, your shadow looming over it like an omen.
Zh: 你看见一个昏暗的房间，里面堆满灰尘扑扑的家具和垃圾。后面有一个出入口，上面挂了许多笼子形状的可怕装饰品——你的阴影笼罩在那上面，像是某种预兆。

En: A small terrified "Oh!" escapes from Plaisance, as she tries her best to look away, her round face buried in her hands.
Zh: 一个惊恐的“哦！”字不经意地从普莱桑斯的口中逃了出来，她拼命想转移目光，把自己那张圆脸埋进手中。

En: Take a peek behind the curtains.
Zh: 偷偷看一眼窗帘后面。

En: You see a dimly lit room full of dusty furniture and trash. A doorway stands in the back, covered in dozens of scary little Semenese wards, your shadow looming over it like an omen.
Zh: 你看见一个昏暗的房间，里面堆满灰尘扑扑的家具和垃圾。后面有一个出入口，上面挂了许多笼子形状的可怕装饰品——你的阴影笼罩在那上面，像是某种预兆。

En: You see a dimly lit room full of dusty furniture and trash. A doorway stands in the back, covered in dozens of scary little Semenese wards, your shadow looming over it like an omen.
Zh: 你看见一个昏暗的房间，里面堆满灰尘扑扑的家具和垃圾。后面有一个出入口，上面挂着可怕的小塞美尼守护——你的阴影笼罩在那上面，像是某种预兆。

En: Those snow crabs are worse than they sound.
Zh: 那些雪蟹比听起来的更糟糕。

En: Wow, more Hjelmdall...
Zh: “哇哦，还有这么多赫姆达尔…”

En: Mountains of it. Heroic quantities of Hjelmdall. Roy's puny shirt is nothing compared to the *real deal*.
Zh: 赫姆达尔的英雄小说，堆得像山一样。跟*本尊*相比，罗伊那件衬衫根本微不足道。

En: This is entirely, *completely* you. You have found the *right* books.
Zh: 这彻彻底底，*完完全全*就是你本人嘛。你找到了*对的*书。

En: It's pulp adventure. Takes place in a faux-mythological non-place called Hjelmdall. There is no such region on the Katla isola.
Zh: 这是纸上冒险。发生在一个名叫赫姆达尔的虚拟神话世界。卡特拉洲上根本没有这种地方。

En: The display rack is brimming with worn paperbacks featuring an extremely muscular, sword-wielding barbarian on the cover. Nearly all the titles contain the word *Hjelmdall* somewhere.
Zh: 展示架堆满了破旧的平装书，封面上有一个很显眼的野蛮人——肌肉极度发达，手持长剑。几乎所有标题都包含*赫姆达尔*这个词。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Storekeep, tell me about the muscleman books.
Zh: “店主，跟我说说那本肌肉男的书。”

En: I want to buy the 'Hjelmdallermann' book.
Zh: “我想买那个叫‘赫姆达尔英雄传’的书。”

En: Look through the display of books.
Zh: 浏览陈列的书籍。

En: I want to buy 'Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman'.
Zh: “我想买这本‘来自赫姆达尔的男人与魔女’。”

En: Oh, *Man from Hjelmdall*. A very popular series of adventure novels. She looks at the book with some disdain. "They're awfully immoral and violent books."
Zh: “哦，*来自赫姆达尔的男人*。一本非常出名的系列冒险小说。”她略为不屑地看着那本书。“那些书非常有伤风化，而且还很暴力。”

En: Blood and violence, scantily clad women, epic narratives, all those mystical things he encounters. They're bound to grab those with little imagination and nothing to do.
Zh: “鲜血和暴力，衣不遮体的女人，史诗故事，还有他遇到的所有神秘的东西。一定会吸引那些缺乏想象力，而且无所事事的人。”

En: Why are they so popular?
Zh: “为什么会流行呢？”

En: Who's this Man from Hjelmdall?
Zh: “来自赫姆达尔的男人是谁？”

En: At last, someone sensible... She fiddles with her pendant. "However, I still urge you to buy one. Can't judge a book by the cover, they say!"
Zh: “总算遇到通情达理的人了…”她摆弄着自己的吊坠。“不过，我还是劝你买一本。毕竟他们都说，不能只通过封面来评判一本书的好坏。”

En: He's a 'hero' from the frozen northlands. He travels to all sorts of lands and mainly kills people. There are hundreds of books in the series... and they sell incredibly well.
Zh: “他是来自冰封北国的一个‘英雄’，周游各地，主要是到处杀人。这个系列有几百本书…而且卖得相当不错。”

En: She nudges her glasses. "It *is* a best-seller for a reason."
Zh: 她推了推眼镜。“它*成为*畅销书是有理由的。”

En: Oh, sirs, I know. The storekeep apologizes immediately. "This really isn't representative of our values. But the store has to cater to its customers."\n
Zh: “哦，先生，我知道的。”店主立即道歉。“它真的不能代表我们的价值观。但是这家店还是要迎合顾客的。”

En: Oh, sir, I know. The storekeep apologizes immediately. "This really isn't representative of our values. But the store has to cater to its customers."
Zh: “哦，先生，我知道的。”店主立即道歉。“它真的不能代表我们的价值观。但是这家店还是要迎合顾客的。”

En: The entire series is also *endearingly* racist, if I may add.
Zh: “如果我可以补充的话，这整个系列还很*讨喜的*种族歧视呢。”

En: Storekeep, this book is misogynistic. How can you peddle such filth?
Zh: “店主，这本书很歧视女性啊。你怎么能卖这种肮脏的东西？”

En: Do any of the books call out to me?
Zh: 有没有哪本书在呼唤我？

En: Dresscode: Rummager.
Zh: 着装要求：翻查者。

En: Hjelmdallerfan.
Zh: 赫姆达尔迷。

En: A twinge at the back of your head makes you flinch. Your eye starts twitching.
Zh: 后脑勺感到一阵刺痛，让你有些退却。你的眼睛也开始抽搐。

En: Nothing of interest. Only silence and the cosmic background pain-radiation.
Zh: 没什么感兴趣的。只有沉默和宇宙背景的痛苦辐射。

En: Oh yes, certainly. She rubs her hands together. "Another good sale."
Zh: “没错，当然了。”她搓着双手。“又是个好买卖。”

En: Rows and rows of Hjelmdallermenn blur your vision. You make out some titles: "Man from Hjelmdall and the Mammoth-Riders", "Man from Hjelmdall: Return to Hjelmdall", and the solipsistic "Man From Hjelmdall and the Hjelmdall Man."
Zh: 一排排的赫姆达尔英雄传模糊了你的视线。你依稀辨认出一些标题：“来自赫姆达尔的男人与猛犸象骑手”，“来自赫姆达尔的男人：回到赫姆达尔”，还有最一本唯我论的“来自赫姆达尔的男人与赫姆达尔的男人”。

En: Good god, how many are there?
Zh: 我的天，怎么这么多？

En: Enough.
Zh: 够了。

En: The display rack before you is burdened under piles of Man from Hjelmdall novels.
Zh: 你眼前的展示架被*来自赫姆达尔的男人*的小说压得喘不过气来。

En: Maybe a hundred? "Man from Hjelmdall and the Sages at the End of the World", "Man from Hjelmdall and the False-God", "Man from Hjelmdall and the Scorched Earth", "Man from Hjelmdall: The Hjelmdall Colonies", "Man from Hjelmdall and the Swamp Beast", "Man from Hjelmdall and the Snow Crabs."
Zh: 也许有一百本？“来自赫姆达尔的男人与末日先哲”，“来自赫姆达尔的男人与虚神”，“来自赫姆达尔的男人与焦土策略”，“来自赫姆达尔的男人：赫姆达尔殖民地”，“来自赫姆达尔的男人与沼泽怪兽”，来自赫姆达尔的男人与雪蟹”。

En: Is that all?
Zh: 就这些吗？

En: Not even close! "Man from Hjelmdall in Hell", "Man from Hjelmdall and the Forest of Slaves", "Man from Hjelmdall Under the Lake", "Man from Hjelmdall: Hjelmdall Burning". There's even "The Trial of Death", a Pasternal combat game book set in the world of Hjelmdallermann, and so much more.
Zh: 还够没有呢！“来自赫姆达尔的男人之坠入地狱”，“来自赫姆达尔的男人与森林奴隶”，“来自赫姆达尔的男人之湖水深处”，“来自赫姆达尔的男人：燃烧的赫姆达尔”。甚至还有“死亡宣判”，以赫姆达尔世界观为背景的田园风战斗游戏书。还有很多很多。

En: It's symbolic of vice and sin.
Zh: 它是恶行与罪孽的象征。

En: Especially those leering types who seem to wear nothing but an armoured bikini. There's also some sort of a snake-lizard beast slithering around her abdomen-chest-shoulder region.
Zh: 特别是那些搔首弄姿、眼含秋波的家伙，好像只穿了身装甲比基尼。而且还有蛇蜥样的野兽盘绕在她的‘胸腹肩’区域。

En: Hmm, aren't all women devil women?
Zh: 嗯，那些不都是魔女吗？

En: Your hand reaches toward a book with glossy cover art of the very muscular Man from Hjelmdall -- in *chains* -- kneeling in front of a staircase leading to a throne. A woman sits on the throne, leering at the man.
Zh: 你的手伸向那本书，光滑的封面上印着肌肉发达的*来自赫姆达尔的男人*——被*链条束缚*的男人——他正跪在通往王座的楼梯前。一个女人坐在王座上，正朝着那个男人抛媚眼。

En: She's laughing at him. Belittling him.
Zh: 她在嘲笑他，轻视他。

En: Between the throne and the Hjelmdallermann lies a bonfire, casting shadows on the wall. The shadow of the woman's head-dress looks like a pair of devil horns. The title reads: "Man from Hjelmdall and the Devil Woman".
Zh: 在王座和来自赫姆达尔男人中间是一团篝火，在墙壁上投射出黑色的影子。女人头饰的阴影看起来像是一对恶魔角。标题是：“来自赫姆达尔的男人与魔女。”

En: Ugh, what is it?
Zh: 呃，这是什么？

En: Ow!
Zh: 啊！

En: Interesting.
Zh: 有意思。

En: That doesn't sound like something I'd be interested in.
Zh: “听起来不像是我感兴趣的东西。”

En: Sounds good. Which one should I start with?
Zh: “听起来不错。那我应该从哪本开始？”

En: What does it matter? They're all the same. She rolls her eyes, then fiddles with her pendant. "However, the customer is always right, they say."
Zh: “有什么关系吗？它们都一样。”她翻了翻白眼，然后摆弄起脖子上的吊坠。“不过，大家都说，顾客永远是对的。”

En: If you're a novice of the series, I'd recommend 'Hjelmdallermann: the Man from Hjelmdall.' It's supposed to be a good introduction to the series.
Zh: “如果你是第一次看系列小说的话，那我推荐从‘赫姆达尔传奇：来自赫姆达尔的男人’开始。对于这个系列来说，这本入门应该最合适。”

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: The lieutenant nods toward it and says: "Map of Martinaise. This could work maybe?"
Zh: 警督朝着它点点头，然后说到：“马丁内斯地图。也许能派上用场？”

En: Martinaise!
Zh: 马丁内斯！

En: This one *could* work for seeing where the shot came from.
Zh: 这个*也许*可以用来确认子弹的来源。

En: What else?
Zh: 还有什么？

En: You can. On Caillou -- Revachol, a single black star; on Ozonne -- Fond de l'Air and Virmandeux; on Archipelagos -- Croyant-Morain, Villiers; on Semenine -- Olduvai. And on Laurentide -- Deora Of The Seven Seas...
Zh: 你可以的。在卡尤——瑞瓦肖，一颗黑色的星星；在欧佐纳尔——丰德艾尔和维曼迪克斯；在阿基佩戈斯——是克里杨特-莫兰，维利耶；在塞美尼——奥杜瓦。还有劳伦冰盖——是七海德奥拉…

En: In the northeast a dust mite stands on the north coast of Caillou. In a bookstore. It's you.
Zh: 在东北方，卡尤的北部海岸上站着一粒尘螨。那是站在书店里的你。

En: Radiating outwards from you -- the Suzerain Revachol. With a radius of 80 kilometres. Still, the crown jewel of this isola would be barely visible.
Zh: 从你的位置向外辐射——是瑞瓦肖宗主国。半径大约80公里。不过，这个洲的宝珠还是很难看见。

En: Disintegrating into mathematics.
Zh: 分解成数学。

En: Squint first -- can you see *cities* on the islands?
Zh: 先斜着看——你能看见岛上的*城市*吗？

En: Look at the edges.
Zh: 看看边缘。

En: Lost little pearls of light. Tiny fires in the dark.
Zh: 光芒中遗漏的小珍珠。黑暗中的点点火光。

En: 850 million people live on these little dots. An oceanic world of culture and commerce, torn apart by history.
Zh: 一共有8.5亿人住在这些小点上。一个被历史撕裂的、文化和商业的海洋世界。

En: Connections to other worlds. Worlds past the Insulindian, unknown to you. You only know you've never been there.
Zh: 与其他世界的联系。比伊苏林迪更遥远，对于你来说尚是未知的世界。你只知道自己从未到过那个地方。

En: 850 million people live on these tiny dots. An oceanic world of culture and commerce, torn apart by history.
Zh: 8.5亿人住在这些小点上。一个被历史撕裂的、文化和商业的海洋世界。

En: You have little idea what they are -- distant stars? gods? -- but looking at them makes you feel almost non-existent. Whatever they are, the *isolas* are immeasurably large compared to you. And very, very far away.
Zh: 你根本不知道它们是什么——遥远的星星？神明？——但是看着它们的时候，让你几乎感觉自己并不存在。不管它们是什么，这些*洲*跟你相比，实在是大的无边无际。而且还很遥远，遥不可及。

En: Perhaps they *are* gods. Gods of distance and outer dust.
Zh: 也许它们*是*神明。远方和外尘的神。

En: The north coast of a verdant island is shattered by the delta of a river. It is the River Esperance. Countless bridges put the shards back together, connecting city blocks to river islands. *La Delta*, says a great, artificial heart in the centre, teeming with lifeforms and construction.
Zh: 一座青翠的岛屿北岸被一条河流的三角洲弄的支离破碎。那是艾斯普兰河。数不清的桥梁把碎片拼凑在一起，连接着城区和河心滩。*德尔塔*，就是中央那颗巨大的人造心脏，充满各种生命体和建筑物。

En: To the east, rolling hillsides: Le Jardin, Stella Maris, the suburbs of Saint-Batiste, swallowed up into the megacity. They sound *rich* to you. This is Revachol East.
Zh: 东边起伏的山坡上：勒雅尔丹，斯特拉里斯，圣巴蒂斯特郊区，被吞入了这座巨型都市。在你听来，它们非常*富有*。这是瑞瓦肖东部。

En: And west of the river?
Zh: 那河流西边呢？

En: And Martinaise?
Zh: 那马丁内斯呢？

En: It's so small you can't even see it on the map. No... wait. There it is! North of Jamrock, the strip of coast next to the Greater Revachol Industrial Harbour. It looks downright despondent. It's almost Coal City, to be honest.
Zh: 它实在太小，在地图上几乎很难看见。不…等等。有了！加姆洛克北边，就在瑞瓦肖大工业港旁边的海岸地带。看起来十分沮丧。老实说，跟煤城不相上下。

En: You feel you're *just* west of Coal City. Somewhere above Jamrock and close to Coal City.
Zh: 你感觉到自己*就在*煤城西边。就在加姆洛克上方，靠近煤城的某个地方。

En: No. Coal City is worse. A charred limb. Rain falls on its slick black streets. And then there's the Burnt-Out Quarter in the heart of Jamrock... is it cold in this bookstore, or is it just *you*?
Zh: 不。煤城要糟糕得多。那是一具焦黑的残躯。雨水坠落在它粘腻的黑色街道上。然后是加姆洛克正中央的燃尽区……是书店突然变冷了，还是只是*你*一个人的感觉？

En: No. This is somewhere to be. This is all you have, but it's still something. Streets and sodium lights. The sky, the world. You're still alive.
Zh: 不。这里就是属于你的某个地方。虽然是你拥有的全部，不过仍然不是空无一物。那里有街道，有钠光灯，有天空，有世界。还有你——还在呼吸。

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: Several maps have been attached to a bulletin board hidden inside the alcove. They're held up by small pins. The board has come loose from one corner.
Zh: 小阁子里藏着一张布告牌，上面还有几份地图——是被小钉子固定住的。布告牌有一个角落已经松动了。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Look at the map of Insulinde.
Zh: 查看伊苏林迪地图。

En: Look at the map of Revachol.
Zh: 查看瑞瓦肖地图。

En: Steal it instead.
Zh: 把它偷走。

En: Angry storekeeper stare-down.
Zh: 愤怒的店主盯的你不敢看她。

En: I want to buy the map of Martinaise.
Zh: “我想买一份马丁内斯的地图。”

En: The maps look old and faded. Your eye catches a map of Insulinde, a map of Revachol, and a map of Martinaise.
Zh: 地图看上去破旧而褪色。你的眼睛注意到一份伊苏林迪地图，一份瑞瓦肖地图，还有一份马丁内斯地图。

En: She nods. "Always good to be informed of your surroundings."
Zh: 她点点头。“了解自己周围的环境总是不错的。”

En: You peek at the storekeep, waiting for her to be distracted. When she's not looking, you deftly rip the map off the bulletin board and pocket it.
Zh: 你偷偷盯着店主，等待她分心。当她没看到时，你熟练地从布告牌上撕下地图，然后装进口袋。

En: Slowly, you move your hand towards the map attached to the board. Before you can even reach it, you're interrupted...
Zh: 你慢慢把手伸向布告牌上的地图。不过还没碰到，你就被打断了：

En: Look at the map of Martinaise.
Zh: 查看马丁内斯地图。

En: Storekeep, can I buy these maps?
Zh: “店主，我能买这些地图吗？”

En: I'm sorry, officer, the map of Martinaise is the only one available. The other two are not for sale anymore... and besides, you could scarcely afford them.
Zh: “抱歉，警官，马丁内斯地图只有这一份了。其他两份也是非卖品…再说了，你基本上是买不起的。”

En: It's not really a map. It's a tourist thing -- a picture postcard with buildings on it, drawn from an isometric perspective. A date in the upper right corner says: '48.
Zh: 它不是一份真正的地图。好像是旅游相关的东西——一张美术明信片，上面还有些建筑，是以等角透视的方法画的。右上角有一个日期：48年。

En: This large map displays archipelagos. You see a constellation of small dots on the light blue emptiness of the Insulindic ocean. The largest, in the northeast, is "Le Caillou" (you are here). Another, far away in the southwest -- "Semenese Islands (Ile du Fantôme)".
Zh: 这份大地图上展示了所有群岛。你在伊苏林迪大洋浅蓝色的空白处看到一群小点。最大的在东北边，名叫“卡尤”（你在这里）。还有一个在遥远的西南方——“塞美尼群岛（幽灵岛）。”

En: Ozonne, Laurentide, Face-a-la-Mer, Archipelagos, North Arcade Islands... all just specks of dust on the vastness of the Insulindic. On the edges of the map, the colour fades into a blur of dotted lines. Black and white.
Zh: 欧佐纳尔，劳伦冰盖，法斯拉美，阿基佩戈斯，北廊群岛……在广阔无垠的伊苏林迪里，它们都不过是粒粒斑驳的尘埃。地图边缘的颜色已经褪变成一些模糊的虚线，黑白相间。

En: Couron. It's somewhere to live. Not bad. Then there's Jamrock -- it's *bad*. People shouldn't live there, but they do. Then Faubourg -- it's almost *as* bad and much larger. Then Coal City. It's the worst.
Zh: 科戎。一个适合居住的地方。不算太糟糕。然后是加姆洛克——那里很*糟糕*。人们不应该住在那里，但事实并非如此。然后是市郊——几乎是*同样的*糟糕，而且要大上许多。最后是煤城，最糟糕的地方。

En: Still, it's detailed. Could be pretty useful for scouting ahead. You see the jagged boxes of an industrial harbour, even the Whirling-in-Rags there...
Zh: 不过还是挺详尽的，对之后的搜查可能会很有用。你看见一个锯齿状的盒子——代表工业港口，甚至连褴褛飞旋都有……

En: You're now the proud owner of a map of Martinaise, which, to be honest, did not even cost that much.
Zh: 现在你是马丁内斯地图骄傲的所有者，说实话，甚至都没花多少钱。

En: The lieutenant coughs sharply, then whispers: "Perhaps not. It's against RCM policy to defraud small business owners."
Zh: 警督用力咳了几声，然后低语到：“还是别了吧。诈骗小商家是违反RCM政策的。”

En: Sir, please, no touching the merchandise. These old maps are... relics of the past.
Zh: “先生，请不要乱碰商品。这些旧地图…是过去的纪念品。”

En: They're quite valuable, though they might not look it. The map of Martinaise is 90 cents, though.
Zh: “看起来可能不怎么样，但它们其实很贵重。不过，马丁内斯地图只要90分。”

En: Why is the one of Martinaise so cheap?
Zh: “为什么马丁内斯的这么便宜？”

En: You seem to underestimate my resources, but sure, okay.
Zh: “你好像低估了我的财力，不过没事，无所谓。”

En: Yes-yes. Are you interested or not?
Zh: “是啊是啊。你到底有没有兴趣？”

En: That old thing? It's an out-of-date map of a tourist location that never was nor came to be...
Zh: “那个旧玩意？那是一份过时的地图，上面标的都是些从来都不是、也从来没实现的旅游景点。”

En: From when some *design-studio people* tried to spruce the place up, four or five years ago. They also renovated the horse-statue, set up those coin-operated viewers and designed the new street lamps.
Zh: “从那些*设计工作室的人*想把这里收拾干净开始，大概是4、5年前的事吧。他们还翻新了那座骏马雕像，装了投币望远镜，还设计了新街灯。”

En: The place does not look like a successful tourist trap, does it?
Zh: 这里看起来不像是个成功的旅游陷阱，不是吗？

En: What happened then?
Zh: “后来发生什么事了？”

En: They didn't get far... for some reason. A shame the project never got going. Would be nice if someone fixed Martinaise up. All these ruins are bad for business.
Zh: “出于某种原因…他们没能取得太大进展。很遗憾，那个项目根本没能展开。如果有人能修整马丁内斯就好了。这些废墟可不利于做生意啊。”

En: Officer, discard the thought. For your brave efforts against those thugs you may have it for free.
Zh: “警官，你还是放弃这个想法吧。为了感谢你对抗恶徒的英勇行为，我可以把它免费送给你。”

En: I want to buy the map of Martinaise.
Zh: “我想买这份马丁内斯地图。”

En: The ocean breaks apart into a tangle of cosines and azimuths, all pointing into pale nothingness. *Mundi* is the north azimuth; *Graad* is the northeast azimuth; *Samara* is the east azimuth; *Seol* is the west azimuth. *Isolas*, they're called.
Zh: 海洋分裂成一团余弦和方位角，全都指向苍白的虚无。*蒙迪*位于北角；*格拉德*位于东北角；*萨马拉*位于东角；*西奥*位于西角。它们都叫做*洲*。

En: I don't know... I don't want to be the party-pooper, but this *pale territory* sounds sort of dangerous. Maybe you *shouldn't* walk in it naked.
Zh: 我不知道…我不想扫你的兴，但是这个*灰域的疆域*听起来好像挺危险的。也许你*不应该*光着身子走进去。

En: This is exactly what you need!
Zh: 你需要的就是这个！

En: Entroponetic nudism? That's insane. That's beyond dangerous.
Zh: 灰域裸体主义？太疯狂了吧。而且已经远超危险的范围了。

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: Look through the shelf.
Zh: 浏览书架。

En: Storekeep, what books are these?
Zh: “店主，这些是什么书？”

En: This bookstore is not *strictly* about crime, romance, and biographies of famous people -- there's also a wide range of paranatural literature.
Zh: 这个书店从*严格的*意义上讲，不只有犯罪小说，浪漫小说和名人传记——还有各种各样的超自然读物。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Find something truly otherworldly.
Zh: 寻找一些真正超凡脱俗的东西。

En: What is the pale?
Zh: 灰域是什么？

En: Remote Viewers Division.
Zh: 遥视者部队。

En: Heard about the curse.
Zh: 听闻诅咒。

En: Don't believe in curses.
Zh: 不相信诅咒。

En: All right. I want to buy *Medicinal Purposes of the Pale*.
Zh: “好吧。我想买这本*灰域的医学用途*。”

En: Hum... sir, please, no browsing in that shelf. She narrows her eyes. "That wisdom is not for free."
Zh: “呃…先生，请别看那个书架。”她眯起眼睛。“那种智慧可不是免费的。”

En: I can't have you end up... like... opening a police store next door and stealing my customers, oh no.
Zh: “我不能让你…怎么说呢…在我隔壁开一间警察商店，然后抢走我的生意，哦不。”

En: Amidst the various books you find one written by someone named Matthias W. Dundas. It's about "Wholeness, Unity, Balance".
Zh: 在各种各样的书籍之间，你发现一本马提亚·W·邓达斯写的书。它讲述的是“完整，统一与平衡”。

En: The point of the book, and many others on this shelf, is to give people medicinal advice in situations where they don't have access to paid health services.
Zh: 这本书、还有这个书架上其他很多书的重点，是为那些无法负担医疗服务费用的人提供医学上的建议。

En: It serves platitudes, while also telling everyone that traditional medicine (the kind people don't have access to, and which costs more than this book) is garbage, and would only give you cancer anyway, without even curing your cold or anything.
Zh: 里面都是些陈词滥调，同时还告诉所有人——传统医学（人们得不到的，而且比书贵上很多的那种）都是垃圾，连感冒或者其他小病都治不好，反而只会让你患上癌症。

En: Wholeness, Unity, Balance on the other hand, can basically take care of anything. Though it is important to note...
Zh: “完整，统一与平衡”，换句话说，基本可以解决所有问题。尽管值得注意的是…

En: When it's up to your mind to heal yourself, then it's because of your mind that you're *ill* in the first place.
Zh: “当你决定用心灵治愈自身疾病的时候，首先就说明了一个问题：你的心灵已经*生病*了。”

En: These three things are very important to the working-class mind.
Zh: 这三件事对于工人阶级的思想来说尤其重要。

En: How does that work?
Zh: 那是怎么运作的呢？

En: Uh huh, interesting.
Zh: 嗯，有点意思。

En: It's your own fault if you're ill, got it.
Zh: 如果你病了，那只能怪你自己，明白了。

En: Does the book say anything else?
Zh: 这本书还说了些别的什么吗？

En: Various paranatural books still litter the shelf.
Zh: 书架上还散落着各种各样的超自然书籍。

En: The book features chapters on topics such as: how to find magnesium (it even lists plants you can harvest magnesium from); how to continue drinking if you're an alcoholic who has destroyed his liver; and...
Zh: 这本书中的章节主要讲述了以下话题：如何找到镁（甚至还列出了可以提取镁的植物）；如果你是一个肝脏受损的酒鬼，怎样才能继续饮酒；还有…

En: ...there's even a chapter on the ancient Seraise tradition of using duck gall bladder (preservatives) to treat and prevent sexually transmitted diseases. Pre- and post-factum apply. Nothing worth buying.
Zh: ……甚至还有一章讲的是远古萨弗里传统：用鸭子的胆囊（防腐保存）治疗和防止性传播疾病。事前和事后都要使用。没什么值得购买的。

En: This is just mundane garbage. What's even para-natural about this?
Zh: 这些都是世俗的垃圾。到底有什么超自然的啊？

En: Indeed... She mumbles, staring at the book for a moment. "Something about that book does seem to have spoken to you..."
Zh: “确实…”她盯着书看了一会儿，默默嘟囔到。“不知道为什么，那本书似乎很适合你…”

En: The throbbing in your head increases with every passing moment you gaze at this shelf...
Zh: 你盯着那个书架，时间一分一秒的逝去，你的大脑内部越来越悸动…

En: You sense something ominous emanating from the shelf... but it lacks form and substance, and soon enough you lose track of it.
Zh: 你感觉到有什么不祥的东西从书架上升腾出来……但是它还没有形状和实体，很快你就失去了它的踪迹。

En: Suddenly, as if out of nowhere, a small green book becomes apparent. The title of it reads: "Medicinal Purposes of the Pale."
Zh: 突然间，一本绿色的小书凭空冒了出来。标题是：“灰域的医学用途。”

En: What's the pale?
Zh: 灰域是什么？

En: What's this book about?
Zh: 这本书讲的是什么？

En: The book contains very little explanation on the matter -- this knowledge seems to be taken for granted.
Zh: 这本书关于这个问题的解释很少——这些知识就像是理所当然一般。

En: The book contains descriptions of various pseudo-scientific therapies, alternative medicines, and folk remedies involving *the pale* -- also known as *le territoire*.
Zh: 这本书包含了各式各样伪科学治疗方法的说明、替代药品、还有民间偏方，里面都包含了*灰域*——也被称为*疆域*。

En: For example, it recommends vigorously swatting one's naked body with a venik, or hand broom, made from "the leafy twigs of a young birch tree from the near-pale."
Zh: 比如，它推荐用桦木枝，或者“灰域附近幼小桦树的树枝”做成的扫帚，去大力抽打裸露的身体。

En: Sounds painful.
Zh: 听起来很疼。

En: Sounds invigorating.
Zh: 听起来很让人兴奋。

En: It is supposedly invigorating and good for the circulation.
Zh: 据说能让人精力充沛，而且有益于血液循环。

En: It is, and good for the circulation, too.
Zh: 确实可以，而且还有益于血液循环。

En: What else?
Zh: 还有别的吗？

En: I think I've heard enough.
Zh: 我想我已经听够了。

En: It also recommends consuming distilled spirits like vodka or whiskey that have been aged in the pale. Readers are instructed to cover these jars in a shallow hole just inside the pale, and leave them there for 30-60 days, depending on the potency desired.
Zh: 它还推荐饮用蒸馏酒精，比如在灰域中存放的陈年伏特加或者威士忌。书里建议读者把这些罐子盖起来，放进灰域内的一个浅洞里，然后放置30-60天，具体时间取决于想要的功效。

En: And what does this pale-aged liquor do?
Zh: 那这种灰域陈酿有什么用处呢？

En: Among other benefits, it is alleged to restore a damaged liver to perfect health.
Zh: 在所有其他用处里，有一条据说可以让受损的肝脏完全恢复健康。

En: How is that possible?
Zh: 那怎么可能？

En: I should probably get my hands on some of that. What else is in there?
Zh: 也许我也应该弄一点。还有什么吗？

En: I think I've heard enough.
Zh: 我想我已经听够了。

En: Is it any more improbable than anything else that human beings put their faith in?
Zh: 还有什么能比获得人类的信任更不可能的事情吗？

En: Fair enough. What else is in there?
Zh: 有道理。还有别的吗？

En: For general health and well-being, readers are encouraged to take regular strolls through the pale, though a sidebar cautions readers to limit each stroll to less than an hour...
Zh: 如果是为了一般健康和身体康乐，它鼓励读者经常去灰域散步，不过侧边栏提醒读者将每次散步时间限制在一小时以内…

En: These strolls promise to "cleanse the mind of worries and the body of toxins," especially if the perambulator performs this ritual in the nude. (Nudity figures prominently in a number of these prescriptions.)
Zh: 这种散步保证可以“清除精神负担和身体里的毒素”，特别是散步人能够裸体执行某种仪式的话。（在这些处方中，裸体占据着很重要的地位。）

En: Huh. Anything else of note?
Zh: 嗯。还有什么要注意的吗？

En: There's an entire section devoted to cures for men who are struggling to perform their "marital obligations."
Zh: 有一整个章节专门在讲如何治愈那些努力履行*婚姻义务*的男人。

En: Well, I certainly don't need that.
Zh: 呃，那我肯定用不着。

En: I probably need that.
Zh: 我也许用得着。

En: Excuse me, sir! I believe you've been *perusing* that particular volume long enough. If you'd like to continue reading I must insist you buy it.
Zh: “打扰一下，先生！我相信你已经把那本书*精读*够久的了吧。如果你还想看的话，我坚持你应该买下它。”

En: Well, I hope it contains what you're looking for.
Zh: “好吧，希望里面有你要找的东西。”

En: That's all I need to know.
Zh: 我只需要知道这些就够了。

En: You close the book and return it to its place on the shelf.
Zh: 你合上书，把它放回原来的位置。

En: That seems... improbable.
Zh: 那个好像…不太可能吧。

En: Even better if you can find someone else, preferably a large man dressed in nothing but a towel, to thrash you while you're spread naked and helpless on a cool slab.
Zh: 如果能找到其他人就更好了，最好是个只围着一条浴巾的大个子男人，当你赤身裸体又无助地躺在一个冰冷石板上的时候，他可以帮忙抽打你。

En: It's not just *something from the cellar*, I'm doing it for your previous neighbour -- Soona Luukanen-Kilde.
Zh: “它不仅仅是*地窖里的东西*，我是为你之前的邻居做的——苏娜·卢凯伦-吉尔德。”

En: Oh, you mean that girl with those *bizarre* machines? Hmpf! I recall her now... her energetic presence was off, *very* off. She kept rambling something about her memory being lost...
Zh: “哦，你是说有一台*奇怪*机器的那个女孩？呼！我想起她了…她的能量体很不友好，*非常*不友好。她老是漫无边际的说些自己的内存不见了之类的话…”

En: Her *filament* memory.
Zh: 她的*单纤维*内存。

En: Thank you for your help, in any case. You're welcome back here anytime.
Zh: “总之，感谢你的帮助。我这里随时欢迎你过来。”

En: I'm looking for a book about cockatoos.
Zh: “我正在找一本讲小鹦鹉的书。”

En: A book about cockatoos? There should be one upstairs, right next to the shelf of biographies.
Zh: “讲小鹦鹉的书？楼上应该有，就在传记书架的旁边。”

En: I was told that there's a huge fridge in the building's cellar. Can you lead me there?
Zh: “有人跟我说这栋建筑的地窖里有一台巨大的冰箱。你能带我过去吗？”

En: A fridge? She fidgets with her pendant. "No, I don't know anything about a fridge. Aren't you interested in books?" She nods at the bookshelves.
Zh: “一台冰箱？”她把玩着吊坠。“不，我不知道冰箱的事。难道你对书没兴趣吗？”她朝旁边的书架点点头。

En: For whatever reason she's lying to you about the fridge. Seems like you have to find your own way to the cellar.
Zh: 不管出于什么原因，她在冰箱的事上对你撒了谎。看来你得自己想办法下到地窖去。

En: That's all I can say for now, book peddler. [Leave.]
Zh: “我暂时只能说这么多，书贩子！”[离开。]

En: Farewell for now, book peddler! [Leave.]
Zh: “暂时告退了，书贩子！”[离开。]

En: What is this insolence? She misunderstood.
Zh: 这么傲慢无礼是为什么？她误解了。

En: Son, you got destroyed there. What an idiot you are...
Zh: 孩子，你被毁了。你可真是个白痴…

En: No, that's not *me*.
Zh: 不，那不是*我*。

En: Of course. No one puts words in *your* mouth.
Zh: 当然。没人把话塞到*你*的嘴里。

En: Doesn't feel like it's thriving. Feels ghostly.
Zh: 感觉一点也不舒服，有种幽灵的感觉。

En: Take it easy. You've broken her resistance. Pushing her further will gain nothing.
Zh: 放松点。你攻破了她的抵抗情绪。继续逼她也无济于事。

En: Such wraiths may prove a formidable enemy. Suit up.
Zh: 这种幽灵一定是非常强大的敌人。全副武装起来吧。

En: Psychic arts? Sounds right down our alley.
Zh: 通灵艺术？听起来很对我们的胃口。

En: This definitely softened her up.
Zh: 这样绝对能让她软化一点。

En: Tell her there's a *dead body* involved. That will make it easier.
Zh: 告诉她这跟一具*死尸*有关。这样事情会变得容易得多。

En: Wait, mention the *dead body*! People hate those.
Zh: 等等，提一下那具*死尸*！大家都很讨厌那个的。

En: Technically, that's what Crime Bros would do, yes. Commit crimes.
Zh: 从技术上讲，确实是罪恶兄弟会做的事，没错。犯罪。

En: Perfidy? Oh no, we're afraid the presence is VERY REAL.
Zh: 背信弃义？不，恐怕那个存在确实是真的。

En: Unbelievable darkness and ruin.
Zh: 难以置信的黑暗和废墟。

En: What you *discover*? Probably just some office space. Don't be scared.
Zh: 你*发现*什么了？只是办公空间嘛。没什么好害怕的。

En: Do not act surprised. You know of these things, sire.
Zh: 别装出一副这么惊讶的样子。您了解这些东西的，大人。

En: What you *discover*? Probably just office space -- and the damn fridge. Don't be scared.
Zh: 你*发现*什么了？只是办公空间——还有那台该死的冰箱。没什么好害怕的。

En: What you *discover*? A demonic refrigerator! Perhaps the fridge will be the source of the curse...
Zh: 你*发现*什么了？一台有魔力的冰箱！也许它就是诅咒的源头…

En: Hello again, esteemed officer. And welcome to Crime, Romance, and Biographies of Famous People.
Zh: “又见面了，尊敬的警官。欢迎来到犯罪、浪漫和名人传记。”

En: Hello again, esteemed officer, she keeps reciting like a robot. "And welcome to Crime, Romance, and Biographies of Famous People."
Zh: “又见面了，尊敬的警官，”她像个机器人一样不停背诵着，“欢迎来到犯罪、浪漫和名人传记。”

En: Hello again, esteemed officer. And welcome to Crime, Romance, and Biographies of Famous People. Her stare is cold and harsh but the line remains the same.
Zh: “又见面了，尊敬的警官。欢迎来到犯罪、浪漫和名人传记。”她的目光冰冷而严厉，不过台词一直没变。

En: She's breathing heavily, trying to calm herself. Slowly, she regains composure -- but she still can't get a word out.
Zh: 她重重喘息着，想要冷静下来。渐渐地，她恢复了往日的镇静——不过还是一个字也说不出来。

En: *This economy* is a mysterious force, like cosmic weather. Mysterious and harsh.
Zh: *经济*是一种神秘的力量，就像宇宙的天气。神秘而又严酷。

En: A part of hers too, clearly.
Zh: 显然，这也是她的一部分。

En: Yeah, actually it's super all right for kids to chew their hands off. Forget I said anything.
Zh: “是啊，其实让孩子们把手指头咬掉也挺好的。忘了我说过的话吧。”

En: All of a sudden she exhales sharply. Her shoulders slump down. "Oh no..." she mutters. "Hold on, I need to invite her inside and apologize. She must be freezing out there..."
Zh: 突然间，她的呼吸变得急促，肩膀也塌了下来。“不…”她嘟囔着。“等等，我需要邀请她进来，给她道个歉。她一定在外面冻坏了吧…”

En: This is a person coming to terms with a new reality. One where they are *wrong*. It's not easy.
Zh: 这个人与一种新的现实妥协了。在那个现实里，她*犯了错*。这不是件容易的事。

En: She's looking for one -- but there simply aren't any good arguments for being an asshole.
Zh: 她正在寻找一个好借口——但就是没有借口能为她的混账行为开脱。

En: Just nod compassionately.
Zh: 只是同情地点点头。

En: Yes. Sometimes it's better to let people come to these conclusions themselves.
Zh: 是的。有时候让人们自己得出结论会比较好。

En: ...my husband is completely different. Of course.
Zh: …我的丈夫完全不同。绝对是这样没错。

En: Super silent. Almost inaudibly so.
Zh: 非常静默。几乎听不见似的。

En: Of course. Yes. This economy.
Zh: “当然了。没错。经济。”

En: Exactly. She agrees wholeheartedly.
Zh: “没错。”她全心全意地表示赞同。

En: I'm sorry.“ She catches herself. I'm sure you do understand."
Zh: 她突然意识到自己失礼了。“抱歉，我肯定你能理解。”

En: I assure you, I of all people understand the importance of *education*. She will be back in school the moment the store takes off.
Zh: “我向你保证，我是所有人中非常了解*教育*重要性的那一种。只要书店一走上正轨，她马上就会回到学校。”

En: And hell freezes over? Never mind, it's not a good topic to get into.
Zh: 等到海枯石烂吗？没关系，反正这也不是值得深入的好话题。

En: The woman looks aloof, her features much softer. Occasionally she glances at her daughter's silhouette.
Zh: 女人很冷漠，不过面容柔和了许多。她还会时不时地瞥向女儿的身影。

En: ...you look like one.
Zh: …你看起来就像是。

En: Go ahead then. Rock her world, he thinks. I'll compose some notes.
Zh: 那就去吧。撼动她的世界，他这样想到。我来谱点曲子。

En: Here we go!
Zh: 要开始了！

En: You don't have anything of substance to tell her until you've found that *Entity* of hers.
Zh: 在找到她说的那个*存在*之前，你没有什么实质性的东西可以告诉她。

En: The narrative she's built herself -- it does need tearing down.
Zh: 她自己编造的故事高楼——确实需要拆除。

En: She's squeezing on the pendant too tight. A drop of blood in her palm...
Zh: 她全力握紧吊坠，一滴血在掌心浮现……

En: Just don't say you don't have *any* answer yet. The uncertainty is killing her.
Zh: 只要别说你没找到*任何*答案。这种不确定性让她备受煎熬。

En: To hell with it -- perchance you ought to just lie, sire?
Zh: 让它见鬼去吧——或许您应该撒谎，大人？

En: I don't understand. If you're not sure it's her, then where *is* the source of the Doom? How did she explain the *curse*? She looks perplexed.
Zh: 她看起来很困惑。“我不明白。如果你不确定是她，那诅咒的源头到底*是什么*？她怎么解释那个*诅咒*？”

En: This is *a lot* for her to parse.
Zh: 对于她来说，话里有*很多*东西需要解析。

En: There is another Entity, more malignant, pulling the strings in Martinaise. Perhaps in my travels I will cross paths with it.
Zh: “还有一个更加恶毒的存在，正在幕后操纵整个马丁内斯。也许在今后的旅途中，我会与它相遇。”

En: A *third order* presence, yes... She lets go of the pendant.
Zh: “一个*三阶*的存在，没错…”她松开吊坠。

En: A great, dark relief washes over her.
Zh: 一种巨大而又深邃的安慰笼罩着她。

En: I've heard of these *tri-actors*. In certain occult literature that's too dark to dwell on for too long -- and definitely not in the presence of my daughter. She gestures for you to be silent.
Zh: “我听说过这些*三阶邪灵*。在某些神秘文献中，它实在是太过黑暗，绝对不可以细说太久——而且肯定不能当着我女儿的面讲。”她示意你不要说话。

En: I understand everything, sir. Thank you for descending into the maelström. I will keep fort up here -- strengthen the wards, do my best. And if you happen upon the Third Entity in your travels...
Zh: “我能理解，先生。感谢你愿意堕入混沌。我会在这里坚守阵地——加强守护，尽我一切所能。如果你在旅途中遇到三阶存在……”

En: May the Lord be with you. She performs an x-shaped cross on her chest.
Zh: 她在胸口画了一个X型的十字。“愿神明保佑你。”

En: Well, this has been absolutely *educational*. If we happen on the *Third Presence* in our travels we will certainly come back to tell you.
Zh: “呃，这绝对是很有*教育意义的*。如果我们在旅途中遇到*三阶存在*，一定会回来告诉你的。”

En: He turns to you. "Should we get out of here -- before the *vortex* collapses?"
Zh: 他转向你。“我们该离开这里了吧——乘着*争论中心*还没坍塌之前？”

En: Yes, the venture continues. In other waters. Darker waters.
Zh: 是的，冒险还要继续。在其他的水域。更黑暗的水域。

En: Wow. Total psychic collapse between you two right now.
Zh: 哇哦。现在你们俩的精神都崩溃了。

En: There was never any other way this could have gone. She's just too far gone -- into her own mind.
Zh: 确实没什么别的办法。她实在是太固执己见了。

En: I am sorry we had to disappoint you, ma'am. He turns to you. "Can we go now?"
Zh: “抱歉，我们只能让你失望了，夫人。”他转向你。“现在我们可以走了吧？”

En: She so badly wants this to be over, she would have believed anything.
Zh: 她非常想结束这一切，所以她什么都愿意相信。

En: It will only last a day or two, a week tops. Then her mood will sour and she will feel *the curse* again.
Zh: 这样只会持续一两天，最多一星期。然后她的情绪会变坏，又会开始感觉到*诅咒*的存在。

En: All right then -- all's well that ends well. The lieutenant turns to you. "Should we return to our *ordinary* lives?"
Zh: “那好吧——结果好一切都好。”警督转向你。“我们是不是该回归*正常*生活了？”

En: You've come this far -- you know how to end it. There is an entity *behind* the entity.
Zh: 你已经走了这么远——你知道该如何结束这一切。这个存在*背后*，还会有另一个存在。

En: Yes. The system must be *cleansed* of immigrant labour. (Proceed.)
Zh: “是的。这个体系必须*清除*移民劳工。”（继续。）

En: Cleansed? What do you... She looks down at the pendant in her trembling hands, she collects herself.
Zh: “清除？你这是…”她低头看着颤抖手中的吊坠，然后镇定下来。

En: It's okay. You don't have to. I was wrong to trust another person. I will manage this psychic calamity as I always have -- alone. On my own...
Zh: “没事的。你没必要这样。相信别人是我的错。我会处理好这次的幽灵灾难，就像往常一样——孤身一人。独自战斗…”

En: As she has to -- every day.
Zh: 她必须如此——日复一日。

En: Maybe you were wrong. She's endured for at least four decades in this world.
Zh: 也许你错了。她在这个世界上至少忍受了40年。

En:
Thank you for nothing. Please, do buy some books -- or be on your way,“ she concludes, tucking the pendant away in her blouse.	她把吊坠塞进上衣口袋，总结道：“感谢你什么都没做。请随便买几本书——或者自己该干嘛就干嘛去吧！”
0x010000060001AB0F	Dialogue Text	BOOKSTORE / PLAISANCE	Half Light	Anger and loneliness keep this woman moving through the waking hours. Your little words are no match for it.	愤怒和孤独让这个女人能在清醒时继续前行。你那些小打小闹根本比不过她。
0x010000060001AB19	Dialogue Text	BOOKSTORE / PLAISANCE	Shivers	The shop around you feels ancient suddenly, damp and saturated by the coastal air. The books are rotting, a great cold lives here. And there, too -- 1200 metres away, on the urban coast. The dark shape of a church is reflected on the water, calling.	突然间，你感觉这家商店变得陈旧起来，在海岸空气的影响下变得潮湿而沁润。书籍在腐烂，空气异常冰冷。那边也一样——1200米开外的城市海岸。教堂的黑暗阴影映照在水面上，召唤着你。
0x0100001F000004D3	Dialogue Text	BOOKSTORE / PLAISANCE	You	It doesn't matter. Now, tell me something else..."
Zh: “没关系的。跟我说说别的吧…”

En: Hmpf! She seems disappointed, like a kid who's been told no. "And what else can I do for you?"
Zh: “呼！”她好像很失望，就像一个被别人说不行的孩子。“还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Sounds familiar.
Zh: 听起来很耳熟。

En: Don't mention it.
Zh: 别提了。

En: She nudges her glasses, anxious and tense.
Zh: 她轻轻推了推眼镜，焦虑又紧张。

En: She calls that customer service?!
Zh: 她把这个叫客户服务？！

En: If you're really interested in cracking this mystery, then we should go back to the furnace. Maybe you could yell into it or something, make some noise? You'll think of something, you always do.
Zh: “如果你真的坚持要破解这个谜题，那我们应该回到壁炉那里去。也许你可以朝里面大喊什么的，制造点噪音？你会想到办法的，你总能想到。”

En: Despite his cool the lieutenant's interested in solving this mystery, too.
Zh: 尽管一副冷静的样子，警督也很想破解这个谜题。

En: You ssshould get back to the furnace, make some nnnoise.
Zh: 您应该回到壁炉那里去，制造些噪音。

En: You mean the chimney that's hidden behind the metal safety curtains? I knocked, but couldn't get in...
Zh: “你是说隐藏在防火幕背后的烟囱？我敲过门，但是进不去…”

En: You mean the chimney that's hidden behind the metal safety curtains?
Zh: “你是说隐藏在金属防火幕背后的烟囱？”

En: Yes, I've heard that she's barricaded herself inside... It's connected to the central furnace in the basement, you should go and find it. God knows what she's doing there!
Zh: “是的，我听说她把自己封锁在里面了…它跟地下室的中央采暖系统是相连的，你应该去看看。天知道她在那里做什么！”

En: Ma'am -- what he's saying is true. We found an entroponetic anomaly in the Small Pinewood Church down the coast. I don't mean to be an alarmist -- and more research *is* needed -- but... it's not looking good.
Zh: “夫人——他说的是真的。我们在海岸下边的小松木教堂里发现了灰域异常。我不是有意要危言耸听——而且还*需要*进一步的研究——不过……看起来不太妙。”

En: Maybe it's best to tell her the truth after all. Here we go...
Zh: 也许还是告诉她实话的好。来吧…

En: She remembers. Good.
Zh: 她记得。很好。

En: Especially now that Annette is settling in at school again. She's finally making friends in this place! She looks at her daughter, quietly studying in the corner of the shop. "No, we can't leave Martinaise. We can't."
Zh: “特别是现在安妮特又开始上学了。她总算是在这个地方交到朋友了！”她看着正在书店角落安静学习的女儿。“不，我们不能离开马丁内斯。不可以。”

En: You're *right*, officer. I mustn't lose faith -- especially now that Annette is finally settling in at school and making friends. She looks at her daughter, quietly studying in the corner of the shop. "No, we can't just leave!"
Zh: “你说的*没错*，警官。我不能失去信心——特别是现在安妮特又开始上学，还交到了朋友！”她看着正在书店角落安静学习的女儿。“不，我们不能就这么离开！”

En: Besides, didn't I have some Seolite hope catchers around here somewhere...? I must find them; everything will be alright if I can just find them.
Zh: “再说，我这周围好像有些西奥的希望捕手…？我必须找到它们；如果我能找到它们的话，一切都会没事的。”

En: I've heard of these *tri-actors*. In certain occult literature that's too dark to dwell on for too long -- and definitely not in public. She gestures for you to be silent.
Zh: “我听说过这些*三阶邪灵*。在某些神秘文献中，它实在是太过黑暗，绝对不可以细说太久——而且肯定不能公开讲。”她示意你保持沉默。

En: What do you mean the *actual* source? She clutches her pendant anxiously. "Are you talking about the... *Third Presence*?"
Zh: “你说*真正的*源头是什么意思？”她紧张地握住自己的吊坠。“你说的是…*第三存在*吗？”

En: Yes, the Presence, the Entity, the malicious Energy -- however you may want to call it...
Zh: “是的，那个存在，那个实体，邪恶能量——随便你想怎么称呼…”

En: No, not presence -- *Absence*.
Zh: 不，不是存在——是*不存在*。

En: No, not the Presence... (Close your eyes.) "The Third *Absence*."
Zh: “不，不是存在——”（闭上双眼。）“是第三*不存在*。”

En: *Absence...!* she gasps. Then, after collecting herself: "I imagine things must be rather bleak for you to return to me. Tell me, what have you found?"
Zh: “*不存在*…！”她喘了一口气。等到恢复镇定之后：“既然你会回到我这里来，我能想象得到事情一定杳无希望了。告诉我，你发现了什么？”

En: She Who Has Many Names... she nods solemnly. "I imagine things must be rather bleak for you to return to me. Tell me, what have you found?"
Zh: “她有很多个名字…”她郑重地点点头。“既然你会回到我这里来，我能想象得到事情一定杳无希望了。告诉我，你发现了什么？”

En: My investigation has led me to discover a two-millimetre entroponetic hole in reality. That's the source of doom -- both in the commercial area *and* in Martinaise.
Zh: “我的调查让我发现现实中有一个2毫米的灰域小洞。这就是诅咒的源头——这片商业区*和*马丁内斯的厄运源头。”

En: What do you mean the *actual* source? She clutches her pendant, clearly irritated. "Are you here to lecture me about *economics* again?!"
Zh: “你说*真正的*源头是什么意思？”她紧张地握住自己的吊坠。“你不会又打算给我上一堂*经济学*的课吧？！”

En: What do you mean the *actual* source? She clutches her pendant, clearly irritated. "Earlier you told me there was no curse!"
Zh: “你说*真正的*源头是什么意思？”她紧张地握住自己的吊坠。“之前你说根本没有诅咒！”

En: She wasn't expecting this -- at least not the word *entroponetic*.
Zh: 她没有预料到这个——至少没有预料到*灰域*这个词。

En: Not for her, not for him, not for Revachol -- not for Elysium.
Zh: 不是为了她，不是为了他，也不是为了瑞瓦肖——或者是极乐世界。

En: She's too afraid to even say the word.
Zh: 她害怕地连那个词都说不出口。

En: Tell her the truth -- it's out of her hands.
Zh: 把真相告诉她——这不是她所能控制的。

En: Whatever you do, don't take away her hope -- the only weapon against *total absence*.
Zh: 不管你做什么，不要带走她的希望——这是对抗*整个不存在*的唯一武器。

En: Be welcome -- and *please* take responsibility for the energy you bring into this space.
Zh: “欢迎——*请*务必为你带进这个地方的能量负责。”

En: The girl who used to stand outside said that this place is cursed.
Zh: “外面站的那个小姑娘说这个地方被诅咒了。”

En: The girl outside mentioned that this place is cursed.
Zh: “外面那个小姑娘说这个地方被诅咒了。”

En: *Cursed*? Who said that, Annette? She blinks. "I will have a word with her... This place is not cursed, it has a robustly *magnetic* energy. Good for commercial activity. My business is *thriving*, sir!"
Zh: “*被诅咒*？谁说的，安妮特吗？”她眨眨眼。“看来我要跟她谈谈了…这里没有被诅咒，只是拥有比较强大的*磁力*能量。很有利于商业活动。我也因此而*生意兴隆*，先生！”

En: The lieutenant shoots you a sideways glance. "And I am his private partner, John... Shao. Together we run the pre-eminent spousal surveillance firm in Revachol."
Zh: 警督斜了你一眼。“我是他的私人搭档，约翰…肖。我们在瑞瓦肖开了一家卓越的配偶监视公司。”

En: What nonsense are you two going on about? This is not relevant at all. And besides, her lip curls in disgust, "*my* husband would never do such a thing. We are proper people."
Zh: “你们俩在说什么鬼话呢？完全风马牛不相及啊。再说了，”她厌恶地撇弃嘴唇，“*我的*丈夫肯定不会做这种事。我们都是很正派的人。”

En: You shouldn't be so trusting of your husband. Men are filthy, treacherous pigs.
Zh: “你不应该盲目相信自己丈夫的。男人都是肮脏又叛逆的大猪蹄子。”

En: Mind your manners, John. This isn't the great Shao Empire, where you can talk about sex stuff so openly!
Zh: “注意礼貌，约翰。这里可不是伟大的尚恩帝国，容不得你这么公开地讨论两性关系的话题。”

En: Alright, enough nonsense. The point is that we sometimes take on *unusual* cases.
Zh: “好吧，不说废话了。问题是我们有时候会接手一些*不寻常*的案子。”

En: That's right. When not spying on the love affairs of the ultra-rich, we solve unusual mysteries by the lorry-load.
Zh: “没错。我们没有侦查那些超级大富豪的不正当男女关系时，还会解决很多不寻常的神秘案件。”

En: Our sincere apologies, ma'am. No more nonsense. We're offering to assist you with your troubles, *pro bono*. Despite his apologies, there's a twinge of amusement in the lieutenant's eye.
Zh: “向您致以最诚挚的歉意，夫人。我们不会再说胡话了。这次来我们是想帮你*无偿*解决问题的。”虽然嘴里说着抱歉，警督的眼神里却隐含着消遣的意味。

En: Shao is what the Seolite Empire was called in the Occident, back when far less was known about the people. It was a barbarian Other, ever so mystical, on whom you could project any kind of exotic fantasy. The legend of it persists and a lot of people still think the 'Shao' is a thing.
Zh: 以前大家对其他人种知之甚少的时候，奥西登国家把西奥帝国称之为尚恩帝国。那是一个原生而又充满神秘的国度，你可以把所有对异域的幻想投射到它身上。关于它的传说一直持续着，很多人仍然以为‘尚恩’是个真实存在的地方。

En: Some penetrating sarcasm from your colleague there.
Zh: 你那位同事的讽刺还真是有够尖锐的。

En: The presence is real in this building. I have felt it. It lurks. It hungers. But I can bring peace.
Zh: “这栋建筑里的存在是真实的。我能感觉到。它潜伏着，渴望着。但是我可以带来平和。”

En: This so-called *curse* is nothing more than a simple case of collective delusion.
Zh: “这个所谓的*诅咒*，不过是集体妄想的简单案例罢了。”

En: No, no, don't mock the spirits like that! They don't like it... it only makes them angrier. Leave it be.
Zh: “不不不，别这样嘲弄幽灵！它们不喜欢的…这样只会让它们更生气。还是随它们去的好。”

En: Look, ma'am, I really need to get in there. I'm a compulsive detective. I can't sleep until I've solved the mystery.
Zh: “听着，夫人，我真的需要进去。我是个有强迫症的警探，要是不把谜题解开，我连觉都睡不着。”

En: Convince her to let you investigate the Doomed Commercial Area.
Zh: 说服她让你调查诅咒的商业区。

En: Gave smart-ass parenting advice.
Zh: 提供一些自作聪明的育儿建议。

En: Said curses aren't real.
Zh: 说诅咒不是真的。

En: Ripped open the curtains.
Zh: 扯开窗帘。

En: Remote Viewers Division.
Zh: 遥视者部队。

En: Helped Annette.
Zh: 帮助了安妮特。

En: On a quest to freeze the body.
Zh: 需要冷冻尸体。

En: On a quest to find the off-site copy.
Zh: 有个寻找场外拷贝的任务。

En: You're the master of psychic arts.
Zh: 你是通灵术大师。

En: Her façade has dropped. Now you see the curiosity behind the fear.
Zh: 她的面纱掉了下来。现在你能看见恐惧背后的好奇心。

En: This is nothing but fear for the success of your business given a shape.
Zh: “其实这不过是你对生意成功的恐惧——它化成了一个具体的形象而已。”

En: Too many failures for it to be a coincidence... they were not careful. I will not take such risks. Leave it be!
Zh: “失败太多，根本不可能只是巧合…它们根本不小心。我不会冒这种风险的，还是随它去吧！”

En: Sometimes it's necessary to resort to extreme measures.
Zh: “有时候采取极端措施是很有必要的。”

En: Dealing with ghosts is simple. You have to show them who's boss.
Zh: “对付幽灵很简单。只要让它们看看谁是老大就行。”

En: It's a *proper* place to live -- one of the most peaceful neighbourhoods east of Jamrock. You may know it for its massive housing projects...
Zh: “那是个很*适合*居住的地方——加姆洛克东部最和平的地方之一。你可能也知道那些个大型的住宅工程…”

En: I had more questions about your family.
Zh: “关于你的家人，我还有些问题。”

En: About your daughter...
Zh: “关于你女儿…”

En: Your daughter is the one standing outside the store, right?
Zh: “商店外面站着的那个是你女儿，对吗？”

En: Who's the little girl standing outside?
Zh: “外面站着的那个小姑娘是谁？”

En: Annette, yes. My daughter. I hope she wasn't slacking off again with her nose in science fiction! Tell me, was she at her post, doing her job like a proper girl?
Zh: “安妮特，没错，是我的女儿。希望她别又一头栽进科幻小说里了！告诉我，她有没有像个乖女孩一样好好完成自己的工作？”

En: Depends. How much do you pay the kid?
Zh: “看情况吧。你付给那孩子多少钱？”
