En: Good sir, what does a young child do with money anyway? No, I save it for her, as a fund. She's securing her financial future out there.
Zh: “先生，那么小的孩子要钱做什么？没，我帮她把钱存起来了，当做一笔资金，这样才能保障她未来的经济来源。”

En: In some more developed countries this sort of thing is two felonies. Child labour and slavery.
Zh: “在有些更发达的国家，这类事情可是犯了两重重罪。雇佣童工，还有非法奴役。”

En: Those countries will realise they've raised a lazy and spoiled generation. Her tone is decisive, not at all angry at the insinuation. "Are we done with the jokes now?"
Zh: “那些国家迟早会意识到——是他们养育出了懒散而又被宠坏的一代。”她的口气很坚定，一点也没有为这种暗示感到生气。“所以现在笑话讲完了吗？”

En: Is she an only child?
Zh: “你只有这一个孩子吗？”

En: Yes, I'm afraid so. A real treat she is. It would be nice if she had... She pauses for a second. "No, we couldn't have afforded more children really. Not in this economy."
Zh: “是的，恐怕是的。她是个讨人喜欢的小孩，也许可以更好一点，如果她能…”她停顿了几秒。“不，我们真的养不起更多小孩了。反正这种经济状况不行。”

En: Ten. She's certainly very polite and helpful.
Zh: “10分。她真的很有礼貌，也是个好帮手。”

En: I get she's a trooper, alright. She looks like she can take it, but it's actually wearing her down. You know she's been biting her nails?
Zh: “我明白，她是个小骑士。她看起来似乎能承受，实际上却是身心俱疲。你知道她一直在啃指甲吗？”

En: All this pressure has made her really anxious. You know she's been chewing her nails?
Zh: “这些压力让她真的很焦虑。你知道她一直在啃指甲吗？”

En: God, ugh, I've told her not to do that. It's such a disgusting habit. Her voice is firm. "She'll get over it. Anxiety is a part of life."
Zh: “天啦，我都告诉过她不要这么做了。真是个讨人厌的习惯。”她的声音很坚定。“她会熬过去的。焦虑是生活的一部分。”

En: Denial is the way she copes with criticism.
Zh: 面对批评，她的处理方式就是否认。

En: You're right, the curse is worse than ever -- that's why you need to let me in there to investigate.
Zh: “你说得对，诅咒比以前要厉害了——所以你需要放我进去调查。”

En: The store clerk is still breathing heavily, trying to calm herself. "Welcome to Crime, Romance, and Biographies of Famous People." She is regaining her composure. "My name is Plaisance."
Zh: 店主还喘着粗气，想要让自己冷静下来。“欢迎来到犯罪、浪漫和名人传记。”她正在恢复慢慢冷静。“我是普莱桑斯。”

En: Welcome to Crime, Romance, and Biographies of Famous People. My name is Plaisance. The clerk extends a greeting.
Zh: “欢迎来到犯罪、浪漫和名人传记。我是普莱桑斯。”店员向你们发出问候。

En: She has fine-tuned her voice to find the most welcoming approach for attracting new customers. It doesn't work.
Zh: 她调整着自己的语气，想要找出最热情的方法来吸引新顾客。不过没什么用。

En: Her face looks powdery and painted from all the make-up.
Zh: 她脸上的粉很厚，好像所有化妆品都抹了一遍。

En: What if I want to buy a book?
Zh: “如果我想买书呢？”

En: So what types of books do you have?
Zh: “你这儿都有些什么书呢？”

En: Everything is on the shelves, take a look yourself. She nudges her glasses. "The shelves compel you, don't they?"
Zh: “所有书都在架子上，请自己慢慢看。”她轻轻推了推眼镜。“这些书架对你很有压迫感，不是吗？”

En: She's attempting to mentally direct you towards the shelves. She only wants you to buy the goods, she doesn't care about books.
Zh: 她试图从精神上把你的注意力引向书架。她只想让你掏钱买东西，根本不在乎那些书。

En: No thanks. [Leave.]
Zh: “不了，谢谢。”[离开。]

En: Do you even know what books you have for sale?
Zh: “你到底知不知道自己卖的是什么书？”

En: She scoffs. "Truth be told, not really. My sister brings in most of the goods. I'm sure it's all very literary stuff, with *well-written prose*..."
Zh: 她讪笑到。“老实说，不太知道。大部分商品都是我姐姐弄来的。我能肯定都是些很文学的东西，*文笔优美的散文*…”

En: But you don't learn about the *important things* in life from fabricated stories.
Zh: “但是你无法从那些虚构的故事里学到生活中*最重要的事情*。”

En: Where do you learn about the *important* things then?
Zh: “那你又是从哪里学习那些*最重要*的事情呢？”

En: Wait, shouldn't a storekeeper know about her wares?
Zh: “等等，店主难道不应该了解自己的商品吗？”

En: I provide a valuable service. As long as people buy books, I'm fine. She wiggles her bony fingers at you.
Zh: “我提供的是有价值的服务。只要大家能来买书，我就没问题。”她向你挥舞着瘦骨嶙峋的手指。

En: The truth is available if you just know where to look. And you have to open and free your mind to *understand*. She nods at you, cryptically.
Zh: “如果你知道去哪找，就一定能找到真相。而且，你必须敞开心扉，解放自己的思想去*理解*。”她神秘地朝你点点头。

En: God, I've told her not to do that. It's disgusting. And I told *you* to mind your own business. Her voice is firm. "Clearly you have no idea how hard it is to raise a girl in this economy."
Zh: “天啦，我早就告诉过她不要这么做了。恶心死了。而且我也告诉过*你*，只要管好你自己的事就行。”她的声音很坚定。“显然你不知道养育一个女儿到底有多难，特别是在现在这种经济环境下。”

En: And your husband's also involved with the bookstore?
Zh: “你丈夫也在书店帮忙吗？”

En: A glimmer of sadness blinks through the well-crafted exterior.
Zh: 在精心打造的外表下，你读到一丝转瞬即逝的悲伤。

En: We're quite busy people, you know, my husband and I. Quite busy. Her voice wavers a bit. "Children are a lot of work. You don't look like a father, so I don't expect you to understand..."
Zh: “我们都挺忙的，你知道的，我丈夫和我，非常忙。”她的声音稍微有些颤抖…“养孩子很费心的。你看起来不像是个父亲，所以我也不指望你能理解…”

En: He made the initial investment. Since then he's been what you might call a *silent partner*.
Zh: “最初的投资来自于他。在那之后，你可以称他是*静默合伙人*。”

En: Okay, I'll take another look then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，那我再看看。”（总结。）

En: Alright, I'll take a look then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，我随便看看。”（总结。）

En: She smiles and nods, seemingly relieved.
Zh: 她微笑着点点头，好像松了一口气。

En: What in god's name is she talking about?
Zh: 她到底在说些什么啊？

En: Sir, I am well-educated in the commercial and esoteric arts. I know what to do and what to avoid. She nudges her spectacles.
Zh: 她推了推自己的眼镜。“先生，我接受过商业和专业艺术的良好教育。我知道该做什么，不该做什么。”

En: A dead what? Her eyes widen with shock at the mention. "Oh my god, a victim of the curse? The curse has started killing people? It's *killing* now?!" She grabs her head with both hands.
Zh: “什么死了？”她一听到这事，立即惊恐地睁大了双眼。“天啦，诅咒的受害者吗？诅咒开始杀人了？现在就*杀人*了？！”她用双手抱住了头。

En: Most of the buildings are empty at the moment! Her smile is wide. "It's a great opportunity to get ahead of the crowds. Better times ahead, for sure."
Zh: “现在大部分建筑都是空的！”她的笑容更灿烂了。“这是个超越大多数人的好机会。未来的日子一定会更好，这一点毋庸置疑。”

En: I wasn't kidding when I said we're para-psychologically done! The store proprietor is breathing heavily. "You clearly don't care about the consequences. What gives you the right?"
Zh: “当我说我们在超心理上已经没什么话好说的时候，我可并不是在开玩笑！”店主喘着粗气。“你显然就不关心后果。谁给了你这种权利？”

En: I wasn't kidding when I said we're para-psychologically done! You clearly don't care about the consequences. What gives you the right?
Zh: “当我说我们在超心理上已经没什么话好说的时候，我可并不是在开玩笑！很显然，你根本就不关心后果。谁给了你这种权利？”

En: Before we go on... you seem to be well off enough, can you give me some money? I feel there won't be an opportune moment to ask later.
Zh: “在我们继续之前…你好像生活挺富裕的，能给我点钱吗？我觉得以后再找不到机会问你了。”

En: Sir! Don't be ridiculous. I certainly will not give you money. She gives you a disapproving look. "I would be doing you a grave psychic disfavour..."
Zh: “先生！别开玩笑了。我肯定不会给你钱啊。”她露出拒绝的神情。“我会给你带来严重精神伤害的…”

En: Ma'am, I am a renowned private investigator, a paragon of law and a specialist in all things criminal.
Zh: “夫人，我是一位著名的私家侦探，是法律的模范，而且还是所有犯罪行为方面的专家。”

En: Ma'am, do you know how me and my partner are known to our colleagues back at the station?
Zh: “夫人，你知道我和我的搭档以前在警局同事面前，是以什么出名的吗？”

En: Once we've caught our client's spouse *in flagrante delicto*, we blackmail them and pocket the money ourselves, because that's what private investigators do. Now, I see that *you* are a married woman...
Zh: “有一次，我们把客户的配偶*抓了个现行*，然后勒索他们，把钱装进了自己的口袋，因为私家侦探都是这么做的。我看*你*好像也是个已婚人士…”

En: This is preposterous, a waste of my time and of no help to anyone. She looks displeased.
Zh: “太荒谬了，这只是在浪费我的时间，对所有人都没有好处。”她看起来不太高兴。

En: It's not good to talk about the curse, not in detail. The negativism... She shivers. "It's dangerous. Talking about the void wraiths angers them!"
Zh: “这么详尽地谈论诅咒是不好的。反噬…”她颤抖着。“这样很危险。讨论虚空幽灵会让它们生气的！”

En: Most certainly not! I don't want anyone who's not familiar with the psychic arts to get involved in this mess. Stay away. Leave the spirits be, so they can return to their slumber.
Zh: “肯定不行啊！我不想让任何不熟悉通灵艺术的人卷入这场混乱。离远一点。别管那些幽灵，这样它们就能继续沉睡。”

En: Slither up to her, you silver-tongued fiend. Show her what world-class perfidy looks like!
Zh: 偷偷缠绕到她身上去，巧言令色的恶魔。让她看看什么叫做世界级的不诚实！

En: Time to fire up the old lie machine!
Zh: 是时候启动古老的谎言机了！

En: Okay, I'm willing to listen. We are set on the path... there's little else to do. She shrugs, apparently dejected.
Zh: 她耸耸肩，显然很沮丧。“好吧，我愿意听听。我们已经上了同一条船…也没什么其他事可以做。”

En: Do you take me for a complete fool? She's getting irritated. "Leave it be and just go look at the books."
Zh: “你把我完全当傻子看吗？”她火冒三丈。“算了，你看书去吧。”

En: And others have to suffer terrible consequences just so you can sleep at night? I think not!
Zh: “那其他人就活该承受可怕的后果，就为了你晚上能睡个好觉？我可不这么觉得！”

En: Wow. *Void wraiths*. You have new words.
Zh: 哇哦。*虚空幽灵*。你学到新词了。

En: You think Dick Mullen could solve this? No way, time for a real detective... (Flex them biceps.) "...to shine."
Zh: “你觉得迪克·马伦就可以解决吗？不可能的，是时候让一个真正的警探…”（展示自己的肱二头肌。）“…闪亮登场了。”

En: She gives you a curious look -- you don't really understand what to make of it.
Zh: 她露出一个好奇的表情——你真的不明白到底是怎么一回事。

En: She's into your masculine posturing! Your lean -- uh... husky and chubby brawn -- is pure animal magnetism.
Zh: 她被你的男子气概打动了！你的——-呃…强壮而又圆润的肌肉——散发着纯粹的异性吸引力。

En: Wink at her.
Zh: 朝她眨眨眼。

En: Do not wink at her.
Zh: 别对她眨眼。

En: Oh for the love of... no! She rolls her eyes, although a vague blush nearly cracks through her façade. "Go look at books instead of this nonsense."
Zh: “哦，为了…不！”她揉了揉自己的眼睛，不过脸上还是隐隐泛起一阵红晕。“别说这些胡话了，你还是看书去吧。”

En: I am the law.
Zh: “我就是法律。”

En: I don't know. Nothing really. I don't have any right, I'm sorry.
Zh: “我不知道。其实真的没什么。我没有任何权利，抱歉。”

En: I'm a rebel -- I live by my own rules.
Zh: “我是个反叛者——我以自己的规则为生。”

En: We are truly private in our partnership.
Zh: “我们的合作关系是真正意义上私人性质的。”

En: But wait, there's more!
Zh: “等一下，还不止这些呢！”

En: How can I believe you after all that foolishness? She looks somewhat irritated.
Zh: “你做出这种蠢事之后，还让我怎么相信你？”她看起来有点生气。

En: It's a good offer, ma'am. I urge you to accept our help, it may be the only chance to save your business!
Zh: “这是个好提议，夫人。我强烈要求你接受我们的帮助，这可能是你挽救生意的唯一机会了！”

En: What better way, than to ask us --
Zh: “除了找我们，你还能有什么更好的办法——”

En: So, are you the owner of this store?
Zh: “所以，你就是这家书店的主人？”

En: I am. The proudest owner of our little shop of culture. Her voice, high-pitched, sounds familiar -- you've talked to her before, through the doorbell.
Zh: “没错。我是这间小文化商店最骄傲的主人。”她的声音有些尖锐，听起来很熟悉——你之前通过门铃跟她说过话。

En: I am. The proudest owner of our little shop of culture. Her voice is high-pitched, as if to give it more penetration.
Zh: “没错。我是这间小文化商店最骄傲的主人。”她的声音有些尖锐，好像这样能更有穿透力一些。

En: You... broke down... the back door? She looks very sad, resigned even. "The wards... the door... it's all gone now. Dark psychic energy, leeching onto my shop..."
Zh: “你…砸碎了…后门？”她看起来很悲伤，甚至有点听天由命的意味。“守护…那扇门…现在全没了。黑暗的心灵能量，会蚕食我的商店…”

En: I don't know what I was thinking, barging in like that...
Zh: “我也不知道自己在想什么，居然那样闯进来…”

En: The woman before you scans the store, her shoulders rigid and tense. Every now and then she nudges her glasses.
Zh: 你面前的女人扫视着这间商店，肩膀僵硬而紧张。时不时地，她还会轻推鼻梁上的眼镜。

En: I sensed the psychic emanations from afar. The Sleeper Beyond calls out.
Zh: “我从远处感觉到超自然波动。远方的沉睡者在呼唤。”

En: How does this curse manifest itself?
Zh: “这个诅咒有什么表现？”

En: Oh, this? She holds the pendant in her palm. Its ochre heart glistens under the lights. "No. It's a special *Himean amulet* blessed by desert pygmy shamans with a *spell of compulsion*. It's to compel people to buy books..."
Zh: “哦，这个吗？”她把吊坠握在手心。赭色的心在灯光下闪闪发亮。“不是。这是个特殊的*希米恩护身符*，沙漠侏儒萨满在上面施加了*强制咒语*，让它能强迫人买书…”

En: Is your pendant part of the wards as well? (Point at her necklace.)
Zh: “你的吊坠也是守护机制的一部分吗？”（指着她的项链。）

En: Would you like me to take the case? I could investigate, see if the curse is real. (Conclude.)
Zh: “你想让我接下这个案子吗？我可以调查的，看看诅咒是否真实。”（总结。）

En: I don't care about the curse, I just need to get to the fridge. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我不关心诅咒，我只是需要那台电冰箱。”（总结。）

En: I don't think the curse is real, I just want you to let me in there. (Conclude.)
Zh: “我觉得诅咒不是真的，我只想让你放我进去。”（总结。）

En: Seriously, I need to get to the fridge in the cellar -- it's a police matter. I have a *dead body* to take care of.
Zh: “说真的，我需要用地窖里的那台冰箱——这是警方事务。我这里有一具*尸体*要处理。”

En: The dark sarcophagus... (Pause dramatically.)
Zh: “黑暗石棺…”（戏剧性地停顿一下。）

En: Yes, yes? How was it?
Zh: “是的，是的？它怎么样了？”

En: Hold on, I actually haven't done anything yet. I just wanted to ask a few questions.
Zh: “别着急，其实我什么都还没做呢。我只想问点问题。”

En: It was a charnel house of failed business enterprises, leeching life energy from this bookstore.
Zh: “这是一间失败企业的骸骨教堂，它正在从这间书店吸取生命能量。”

En: Honestly -- a dump. Nothing to see there, just heaps of garbage. Someone should let the sunshine in.
Zh: “老实说——这是一个垃圾场。没什么好看的，只有成堆的垃圾。这里需要来点阳光。”

En: But... what else did you find? Did anything survive? No. Of course not. Have you located the... Entity?
Zh: “但是…你还找到什么了？有什么东西还活着吗？不，当然不会有了。你有没有找到…存在？”

En: Goodness, you were already doing good browsing the shelves. Why'd you stop? She fiddles her pendant, then waves her bony fingers directly at you: "Don't you feel compelled? Go, go -- get back there. The books await you."
Zh: “天啦，你已经仔细浏览过所有书架了。为什么还不肯停下来呢？”她把玩着吊坠，然后直直地朝着你挥舞那瘦骨嶙峋的手指：“难道你不觉得很有压力吗？去吧，去吧——回去吧。那些书在等着你。”

En: Then why are you talking to me? Everything is on the shelves to browse. Don't you feel *compelled* to buy anything? She fiddles with her pendant, then waves her bony fingers directly at you: "See those shelves there, go, be drawn."
Zh: “那你为什么要跟我说话？所有东西都在书架上啊。难道你不觉得*应该*买点什么吗？”她把玩着吊坠，然后直直地朝着你挥舞那瘦骨嶙峋的手指：“看见那边那些书架了吗，去吧，被吸引过去吧。”

En: Quietly, she mutters to herself: "He isn't doing what he's designed to do... isn't the spell working..."
Zh: 她静静地自言自语到：“他没有做设计好的事…难道咒语不起作用了吗…”

En: What spell?
Zh: “什么咒语？”

En: Huh? She flinches at your remark. "Nothing. No one's cast any spells of compulsion to draw in customers. What a silly idea!"
Zh: “哈？”她被你的话吓了一跳。“没什么。没人施加什么强制咒语来吸引顾客。这主意真是太蠢了！”

En: Don't expect my help from now on. The back door is locked and I threw away the key. I won't trifle with such forces. She fiddles with her pendant.
Zh: “从今以后，别指望我再帮什么忙了。后面上了锁，钥匙也被我丢了。我不会戏弄这种力量的。”她摆弄着自己的吊坠。

En: Do look at the books, though. The books compel you. You *may* be able to make up for this by buying a lot of books. I hope you're a voracious reader!
Zh: “不过，你看看那些书。它们在强迫你。作为补偿，*也许*你可以买很多很多的书。希望你是个贪婪的读者！”

En: Wonderful! Did you talk to her?
Zh: “太棒了！你跟她聊过了嘛？”

En: My precious! Her dedication brings joy to my heart. She's immensely satisfied with the answer. "If you have children, I hope they turn out as great as my Annette."
Zh: “我的宝贝！她的奉献让我心生喜悦。”她对这个答案非常满意。“如果你有孩子的话，希望他们都能变得像我的安妮特一样好。”

En: Yes, of course.
Zh: “是啊，当然。”

En: No, she was definitely slacking off.
Zh: “不，她绝对是松懈了。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Not really.
Zh: “不算是。”

En: Why not? Was she not friendly enough? Were you not compelled to talk to her?
Zh: “为什么不呢？难道她不够友好？难道你不觉得应该跟她说话吗？”

En: Great! On a scale of one to ten, how compelled were you to buy books after talking with her?
Zh: “太棒了！跟她说完话后，你买书的欲望有多迫切？满分10分能打几分？”

En: Her opinion of her daughter depends on how well she lured you into the store.
Zh: 她对女儿的看法完全取决于她如何把你引诱进这间书店。

En: The way you're handling her strikes me as wrong.
Zh: “我觉得你对待她的方式是错误的。”

En: Mind your own business, sir. Her posture becomes very rigid. "In *our society*, people don't get to tell each other how to raise their children. It's none of your or anyone's business."
Zh: “管好你自己的事吧，先生。”她的态度变得十分粗鲁。“在*我们的社会*，大家是不会告诉别人应该怎样抚养自己的小孩。这不关你事，也不关其他人的事。”

En: Annette is quite the trooper. She's a great value-add.
Zh: “安妮特是个很棒的小骑士，是个很棒的附加值。”

En: Five. I felt some interest.
Zh: “5分。我稍微有点兴趣。”

En: One. Not impressed.
Zh: “1分。反正没有打动我。”

En: I'm not going to grade a human being. I don't do that.
Zh: “我是不会给人类评分的，绝对不会这么做。”

En: I guess it's better than nothing. She shrugs. "She'll have to try harder next time."
Zh: “我猜总比没有的强。”她耸耸肩。“下次她应该再努力一点。”

En: I knew it. She sighs and shakes her head. "Shameful behaviour. She's incorrigible."
Zh: “我就知道。”她叹了口气，摇摇头。“真丢脸。她简直无可救药。”

En: Come now, it's not personal, it's about proper sales practices and market research. She crosses her arms. "I expect an answer."
Zh: “好了，这并不是针对谁，其实是跟正确的销售实践和市场调查有关。”她抱起胳膊。“我期待你们的回答。”

En: Yes, what about? Hrm, I sure hope she has increased her productivity by now.
Zh: “是的，怎么样？嗯，我真希望现在她已经提高了自己的生产力。”

En: Yes? What now? She frowns. "That lazy girl up to no good again? I must have a serious talk with her."
Zh: “是的？现在怎么样？”她皱起眉头。“那个懒丫头又没干好事了？我真应该跟她好好谈谈。”

En: Oh, yes? How's my precious doing? She's a good girl.
Zh: “哦，是吗？我的宝贝表现的怎么样？她是个好姑娘。”

En: That *is* true -- and obviously the will of the market -- but maybe make an exception for your daughter?
Zh: “这*的确*是真的--而且显然这是市场的意志——也许可以让你的女儿成为一个例外？”

En: There, she returns with a nod. "I don't know what to say to you. My husband, he tries to teach me business lessons. I have... what my mother called a *dull mind*. All this stress..." She stops, but her mouth keeps moving.
Zh: “这个嘛，”她点头回应。“我不知道该跟你说什么。我的丈夫，他试着教授我商业课程。至于我…我妈一直叫我*死脑筋*。这种压力…”她停了下来，但是嘴唇还在蠕动。

En: I don't think she can do anything about it.
Zh: “我觉得她对此无能为力。”

En: Okay. Forget it. (None of your business.)
Zh: “好吧。算了吧。”（跟你无关。）

En: Come on, ma'am. It's obvious she can't do anything about it. You're placing an unnecessary burden on a young child.
Zh: “拜托，夫人。显然她根本做不了什么。你在一个年幼孩子的身上施加了不必要的负担。”

En: Is this husband Annette's father?
Zh: “这个丈夫是安妮特的父亲吗？”

En: Yes. My husband is a successful entrepreneur East of the river. If only he were more *involved* in the business we're running up *here*. She says gloomily. "No matter, soon we'll both be off for Grand Couron!"
Zh: “是的。我的丈夫是河东的一个成功企业家。如果他能多*参与*一点我们在*这里*经营的生意就好了。”她有些沮丧。“不过无所谓，很快我们都会去大科戎了！”

En: Wait. Grand Couron? What's that?
Zh: “等等。大科戎？那是什么？”

En: She told me she doesn't go to school anymore.
Zh: “她告诉我说自己没有去学校了。”

En: Yes? Oh, I hope I can make it up to her. She's my precious kid.
Zh: “是吗？哎，希望我可以好好补偿她。她可是我的宝贝女儿。”

En: What you're doing is wrong. Even I know that, and I usually don't know anything.
Zh: “你做错了。这一点连我也看得出来，而我一般总是不清不楚的。”

En: Oh, she exclaims with some irritation. "Shameful girl, what is she up to this time?!"
Zh: “哦，”她有些生气地嚷嚷着。“丢脸的丫头，这次又干什么好事了？！”

En: No, I must've been mistaken. She's right outside, very attentive, yes.
Zh: “不，我一定是搞错了。她就在外面，非常有礼貌，是的。”

En: This is actually none of my business, let's talk about something else.
Zh: “其实这件事真的与我无关，我们还是聊聊别的吧。”

En: Okay.... Let's change the subject.
Zh: “好吧…我们换个话题。”

En: I'm here to dismantle the free market and abolish child labour.
Zh: “我来是为了摧毁自由市场，废除童工制度的。”

En: I don't know, I just walked right in here instead.
Zh: “我不知道，我就直接这么走进来了。”

En: I'm not interested in talking to a kid.
Zh: “我对小孩的话题不感兴趣。”

En: I was afraid she'd call me a f****t like all the kids around here.
Zh: “恐怕她会像这里的所有孩子一样叫我混账玩意。”

En: Sir! Watch the language. She crosses her arms. "My child would never do such a thing."
Zh: “先生！请注意你的用词。”她抱起双臂。“我的小孩绝对不会做出这种事的。”

En: She lightens up. "Oh yes, okay, that makes sense. It's working then... yes. Do browse our books, sir!"
Zh: 她整张脸都亮了起来。“是的，没错，这样才说得通嘛。那就是有用了…没错。请看看我们的书吧，先生！”

En: What's wrong with her? She shakes her head. "Can't she do anything right?"
Zh: “她出什么问题了？”她摇摇头。“难道她就不能把事做好吗？”

En: She rolls her eyes. "You must be kidding, there's nothing like that happening."
Zh: 她翻了翻眼睛。“你一定是在开玩笑吧，从来没有发生过这种事。”

En: Oh. I guess I was mistaken.
Zh: “哦，我猜一定是我搞错了。”

En: Indeed. Her voice is decisive and sharp as a knife. "Are we done with the jokes now?"
Zh: “确实。”她的声音十分果断，像刀一样尖锐。“所以现在笑话讲完了吗？”

En: Slap the cuffs on her!
Zh: 给她戴上手铐！

En: Such criminal behaviour would not happen in more developed countries.
Zh: 那些更发达的国家不会发生这类犯罪行为。

En: I formally reprimand you for your corrupt activities.
Zh: “我在此正式谴责你的堕落行为。”

En: She raises an eyebrow. "Oh, of course, officer. Good work. Are we done with the jokes now?"
Zh: 她扬起眉毛。“哦，当然了，警官。干得漂亮。所以现在笑话讲完了吗？”

En: I know you're trying to be nice, but she really needs to try a bit harder. No one gets anywhere by being average. I'm not raising a financially negligent woman.
Zh: “我知道你也是一片好心，但她真的需要再努力一点。平庸是无法获得任何成就的。我可不会抚出养一个在经济上马马虎虎的女人。”

En: I understand... you're now feeling guilty about the harsh judgement, but there's no need. Annette needs to get her act together and stop being a lazy slouch, or she'll end up in financial oblivion.
Zh: “我理解……你对这个刺耳的评论感到内疚，但是没这个必要。安妮特需要振作起来，不能再这么懒懒散散，不然最后会背上债务负担的。”

En: As a young girl should be. With the proper attitude she'll have a bright financial future.
Zh: “小女孩就应该这样。如果态度正确，她未来的财务状况就能一片光明。”

En: She can, if she has enough *willpower*. This is what's called *growing pains*. Life isn't easy, life doesn't give breaks.
Zh: “如果拥有足够的*意志力*，她绝对可以的。这就是所谓的*成长的烦恼*。生活并不简单，从来不会给人喘息的机会。”

En: She stands stiff and severe, silently fuming. Ten or so seconds pass without change.
Zh: 她僵硬又严厉地站着，默默生着气。十秒左右过去了，没有任何变化。

En: It sounds like both the husband and the mother treat her the way she treats Annette.
Zh: 听上去，她在用丈夫和父母对待她的方式来对待安妮特。

En: You're like Annette to your husband. And your mother.
Zh: “对于你的丈夫和母亲来说，你就像安妮特一样。”

En: Oh... She nudges her glasses nervously. "Well. My mother was horrible, of course, absolutely *perverse* energies around that person, but my husband..." She shakes her head.
Zh: “哦…”她紧张地推了推眼镜。“好吧。我的母亲确实很可怕，周身都是*一意孤行*的能量，但是我的丈夫…”她摇摇头。

En: Alright. I had something else in mind. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧。我有些别的想法。”（总结。）

En: The woman looks aloof, her features much softer. Occasionally she glances at her daughter's silhouette.
Zh: 女人看向远方，面容柔和了许多，时不时还会瞥向女儿的身影。

En: Yes, we've had quite enough fun here, right. The lieutenant taps his foot.
Zh: “是的，我们在这里玩的很开心。”警督用脚拍打着地面。

En: Your head feels light and dizzy... a cold breeze caresses your flesh.
Zh: 你感觉头晕目眩…一阵冷风爱抚着你的肉体。

En: Shudder, let it pass.
Zh: 颤抖，让它过去。

En: What's going on?
Zh: 出什么事了？

En: Monstrous buildings loom over Grand Couron. A cold wind howls in the empty halls of homes built upon delusions.
Zh: 巨大的建筑在大科戎上方若隐若现。一阵寒风从那些建立在妄想之上的空荡大堂里呼啸而过。

En: Why?
Zh: 为什么？

En: There is no life in Grand Couron. No one can afford to live there.
Zh: 大科戎没有生命。那里没人住的起。

En: Why not?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: I get Annette's a trooper... she looks like she can take it, but it's actually wearing her down. You know she's been biting her nails?
Zh: “我知道安妮特是个小骑士……她看起来似乎能承受，实际上却是身心俱疲。你知道她一直在啃指甲吗？”

En: All the pressure has made Annette really anxious. You know she's been chewing her nails?
Zh: “那些压力让安妮特变得非常焦虑。你知道她在咬指甲吗？”

En: She's been too busy, helping me here, so she's studied at home this trimester. This is a temporary solution, of course. Plaisance nods.
Zh: 普莱桑斯点点头。“她在这里帮我，一直都很忙，所以这学期她在家自学。当然了，这只是临时的解决方案。”

En: I don't want to say all that.
Zh: 我不想说这些。

En: Yes, you do.
Zh: 是的，你想。

En: You're right. What could go wrong?
Zh: 你是对的。能出什么问题呢？

En: No. It's really stupid.
Zh: 不行，真的太蠢了。

En: Oh my god, will you stop with the  incessant yammering? It's too much. Her palm goes to her face. "If you wanted the key to the back door you could have just asked for it."
Zh: “我的天，你能不能别在叽叽喳喳的了？真的过头了。”她用手掌捂住脸庞。“如果你想要后门钥匙，可以直接开口要啊。”

En: Enough funny business. Will you just give us the key already?
Zh: “那就别搞这些花里胡哨的了。你能把钥匙给我吗？”

En: Absolutely not!
Zh: “绝对不行！”

En: Fine, I'll ask for it. Can we have the key?
Zh: “好吧。那我就问了。能把钥匙给我吗？”

En: But I don't want to ask for it, asking is beneath me.
Zh: “但是我不想问啊，开口问对我来说太低级了。”

En: Oh? Well good for you, because you're not going to get it anyway!
Zh: “哦？那很好啊，反正我也不会给你！”

En: Not this nonsense again. I already said *no*!
Zh: “别再说废话了。我已经说过*不可以*了！”

En: I detected psychic emanations from within and was compelled to investigate.
Zh: “我从里面侦查到超自然波动，必须强制调查。”

En: Yes, the venture continues. In other waters. Darker waters.
Zh: 是的，冒险还要继续。在其他的水域。更黑暗的水域。

En: What have you found? She seems anxious. "And also, do buy the books. There may be *teachings* in them."
Zh: “你发现什么了？”她好像有点焦虑。“还有，买些书吧。说不定能从里面发现什么*教诲*呢。”

En: You've hurt yourself now! I told you not to mess around back there. What are you even trying to accomplish, you fool?!
Zh: “你这是在伤害自己！我告诉过你不要在后面乱来。你这个蠢货，到底想干什么？！”

En: Absolutely not. She is mortified. "It's more dangerous than I ever imagined. I definitely can't let you in now, we have to contain it."
Zh: “绝对不可以。”她有些窘迫。“它比我想象的还要危险。现在我绝对不会放你进去了，我们必须控制它。”

En: Enough of this mumbo-jumbo, I have a decomposing body rotting away as we speak. Just let me in!
Zh: “别再说些晦涩难懂的话了，就在我们说话的时候，还有一具尸体正在腐烂呢。快放我进去！”

En: I had a few more questions about the curse...
Zh: “关于这个诅咒，我还有些问题…”

En: Okay, but please, only a few questions. She peers at the curtains. "You wouldn't want to disturb the spirits..."
Zh: “好吧，但是只可以问几个问题。”她凝视着窗帘。“你可不想打扰到那些幽灵…”

En: Why didn't you just tell me right away it's the curse?
Zh: “你为什么没立马告诉我是诅咒？”

En: There are numerous spells cast throughout the store. I had the books anointed with a different inducement spell, for example. She nods. "It's guaranteed to boost sales *fifteen percent*!"
Zh: “这间商店施加了很多咒语。比如说，我在书上就有很多不同的诱导咒语。”她点点头。“它保证可以让销售额增加*15个百分点*！”

En: *Extreme violence*. The wards only make them invite their ghost-families. And the Semenese want that because they're in league with the ghosts.
Zh: “*极端暴力*。守护让它们只能邀请自己的幽灵家族成员。塞美尼人也希望如此，因为他们跟幽灵是勾结在一起的。”

En: What does that even mean?
Zh: “那又是什么意思？”

En: My liege! You know what this case calls for? A para-detective!
Zh: 大人！您知道这个案子需要什么吗？超能警探！

En: You're right... She is mortified. "It's worse than I imagined. We definitely have to contain it now!"
Zh: “你说得对…”她有些窘迫。“它比我想象的还要糟糕。现在我们必须控制住它！”

En: Yessss, this is your chance! This is your chance! Only a *para-detective* can solve this case.
Zh: 没错，时机到了！您的机会来了！只有*超能警探*才能解决这种案子。

En: You're alive and well! Don't keep me waiting now. What's in there? She's looking for the right expression. "In that dark sarcophagus?"
Zh: “你还活得好好的！别让我干等了。里面有什么？”她在寻找正确的表达方式。“那个黑暗石棺里有什么？”

En: Oh. Her disappointment is obvious. "What about?"
Zh: “哦。”她显然非常失望。“怎么样？”

En: Tell her how *ghasssstly* it was! You know it's what she wantssss to hear...
Zh: 告诉她到底有多*可怕*！您知道她希望听到什么……

En: I knew it! A tremor runs through her. "Oh, such horrors that have been thrust upon us..." She shakes her head.
Zh: “我就知道！”她浑身颤抖着。“这就是强加在我们身上的恐怖…”她摇摇头。

En: I have returned from the void. A para-detective from a long line of para-detectives.
Zh: “我从虚空中归来。一个来自超能警探世家的超能警探。”

En: Ma'am, I came here to help. I've handled *paranatural* situations before.
Zh: “夫人，我是来帮忙的。我以前也处理过这种*超自然*状况。”

En: Are you sure? She looks sceptical. "Don't think I haven't seen *charlatans* before..."
Zh: “你确定吗？”她看起来很怀疑。“别以为我之前没见过*江湖骗子*……”

En: You're no para-detective. You look nothing like one -- and you're clearly a drinker. Pardon me for being so blunt, but...
Zh: “你不是超能警探。你看起来根本不像——而且你明显就是个酒鬼。请原谅我这么直接，不过…”

En: You see, it's necessary to drink the *spirits* in order to contact the *void*.
Zh: “你也看见了，为了与*虚空*联系，*喝点酒*是很有必要的。”

En: I used to live on the shadowy isles... the wild wisemen taught me their rituals.
Zh: “我曾经住在幽暗神秘的小岛上…野蛮的智者教会我他们的仪式。”

En: I admit, I've had my share of drinks, but only because the spectral realm is para-psychologically taxing.
Zh: “我承认，我确实喝酒，不过只是因为幽灵国度在超心理方面实在是让人筋疲力尽。”

En: I admit, I've had my share of drinks, but only because *mediation* is so draining.
Zh: “我承认，我确实喝酒，不过只是因为*调解*实在是让人筋疲力尽。”

En: Al Gul hails from beyond the veil, ma'am. Al Gul guides the way.
Zh: “阿尔古在异界向你致敬，夫人。阿尔古会引导我们的道路。”

En: Wait, what if I don't want to lie...
Zh: 等等，如果我不想撒谎呢…

En: You're not *lying* -- you're giving her peace of mind. The meanssss are thus jusssstified.
Zh: 您没有*撒谎*——这是让她的心灵得到平静。所以，这些手段都是正当的。

En: I know the Remote Viewers Division, they have taught me perception beyond this world.
Zh: “我知道遥视者部队，他们教会我超越这个世界认知的感知能力。”

En: The lieutenant keeps his usual stony calm. He silently picks out his notebook.
Zh: 警督维持着一贯的冷酷和镇静。他默默掏出自己的笔记本。

En: Just ask my partner, Kim. He'll vouch for me.
Zh: “问问我的搭档就好了，金。他会为我担保的。”

En: Oh, uh... the lieutenant mumbles in minor confusion. He has not been listening closely enough. You've put him on the spot.
Zh: 警督听的不够认真。“哦，呃…”他有些困惑地低吟着。你让他有些为难。

En: Certainly so, ma'am. I can assure you my partner is eminent in this *particular* field.
Zh: “当然了，夫人。我可以保证，我的搭档是这个*特殊*领域的名人。”

En: I am the Void-Revenant. I have the powers to de-bad all the bad energies.
Zh: “我是虚空亡魂。我拥有消除所有坏能量的能力。”

En: I should have realized... a pattern lies within the fabric. The hand of fate guides us, our meeting couldn't have been mere chance.
Zh: “我应该注意到的……冥冥之中一切皆有定数。命运之手指引着我们，我们的相遇绝对不仅仅是偶然。”

En: She shudders. "I'm not sure I can trust your claims -- honestly, you look like a bit of a drinker. I'm sorry for being so blunt, but..."
Zh: 她耸耸肩。“我不确定能否相信你的主张——老实说，你看起来有点像酒鬼。很抱歉这么直白，但是…”

En: No, it simply can't be! She looks at you with some puzzlement. "How can I trust you? You're clearly a heavy drinker, don't get me wrong..."
Zh: “不，不可能的！”她略呆困惑地看着你。“我怎么能相信你？你明显就是个老酒鬼，别误会…”

En: How do you... *know* all this?
Zh: “你是怎么…*知道*这些的？”

En: Your wards brought me here in the first place. The Semenese blood also runs through me.
Zh: “一开始，是你的守护把我引到这里的。我身上也流淌着塞美尼人的血液。”

En: Oh my, I should have known! You do have that *wild-man* look. Our meeting couldn't have been mere chance!
Zh: “天啦，我就知道！你确实有几分*野人*的样子。我们的相遇绝对不是偶然的！”

En: You're part Semenese?! Oh, it means our meeting couldn't have been mere chance... the hand of fate guides us.
Zh: “你有塞美尼人的血统？！这就意味着我们的相遇绝对不是偶然…命运之手在引导着我们。”

En: If you promise, good officer... then. She shuffles nervously. "You might be our last hope. Do you swear it?"
Zh: 她紧张地扭动着。“如果你能保证的话，警官…那好吧。”她停顿了一下。“也许你是我们最后的希望。你能发誓吗？”

En: But... I am not the only one at risk, I have to think of my daughter. You are certain you can help us, keep us safe? I can't allow any *collateral damage* to hit us.
Zh: “但是…有风险的不止我一个，我还要考虑自己的女儿。你确定能帮到我们，保护我们的安全吗？我可不能允许任何*连带伤害*发生。”

En: No problem whatsoever, your family is safe, the phantoms are no match for me.
Zh: “绝对没问题，你的家人会很安全的，幽灵根本赢不了我。”

En: I don't want to say that either.\n
Zh: 我也不想这么说。

En: Wait... That's it? I don't want to say that!
Zh: 等等…就这样？我不想这么说！

En: Stop being such a fussy prude! You can't convince her without liessssss.
Zh: 别这么挑挑剔剔，扭扭捏捏的了！要是不撒谎的话，您是无法说服她的。

En: Okay, I guess I'll give it a try: "Crime Bros." (Nod at Kim.) "We're called *crime bros*, my partner and I."
Zh: 好吧，我猜我可以试试：“罪恶兄弟。”（朝金点点头。）“别人都叫我的搭档和我，*罪恶兄弟*。”

En: No! It's just as stupid.
Zh: 不行！这样实在是太蠢了。

En: No, it's really stupid.
Zh: 不行，真的太蠢了。

En: Sir, I really don't have time for your jokes. The situation is very serious.
Zh: “先生，我真没时间听你那些笑话。现在的情况非常严峻。”

En: Blech! You deny your own Revacholian blood for the sake of a lie!
Zh: 见鬼！为了撒谎，你居然否认自己的瑞瓦肖血统吗！

En: Hey now, hey. Hey! Hey. You need the booze to focus, alright?
Zh: 喂，喂喂喂！你需要酒精才能集中精神，不是吗？“

En: Perhaps you truly *are* the one to deliver this woman from the doom...
Zh: 也许你真的*就是*能把这个女人从厄运中解救出来的人…

En: (A hand on your heart.) "On my honour."
Zh: （将一只手放在心脏的位置。）“这是我的荣耀。”

En: Actually, I'm not feeling the vibe anymore. I just wanted to get to the fridge...
Zh: “其实，我再也感觉不到那种波动了。我只想去冰箱那里…”

En: Actually, I'm not really feeling the vibe anymore... The psychic force has left me.
Zh: “其实，我再也感觉不到那种波动了…那股超自然力量已经离开我了。”

En: Come now! Her eyes light up. "Enough with your jokes, no backing down anymore. Do I have your word?"
Zh: “拜托！”她的眼睛亮了起来。“玩笑说够了，现在已经没有退路。你能向我保证吗？”

En: These ideas are all worthless.
Zh: 这些想法一文不值。

En: Thy sweet tongue drips not with honey today... yet, try thou must!
Zh: 您甜蜜的唇舌今天没有滴下蜜汁来…不过，您必须试试！

En: Goddamnit, you're completely unhelpful.
Zh: “见鬼，你真是一点用也没有。”

En: 'Tis a poor craftsman who blames his tools!
Zh: 真是个可怜的手艺人，居然责怪自己的工具！

En: Nothing... I'm just terribly clumsy sometimes, sorry. (Rub your aching forehead.)
Zh: “没什么…有时候我真的很笨拙，抱歉。”（揉揉隐隐作痛的前额。）

En: I thought it was obvious: I tried to smash through the door by using pure physical force.
Zh: “我想已经很明显了：我试过用纯粹的物理力量砸破这扇门。”

En: I'm trying to find a way to the fridge downstairs -- it's an urgent police matter.
Zh: “我在想办法到楼下冰箱那里去——这是警方的紧急事件。”

En: You're only making things worse! Stop it. Be compelled to look at the *books*. She fiddles with her pendant. "*Go to them*!"
Zh: “你只会让事情变得更糟。强迫自己去看看那些*书*吧。”她摆弄着自己的吊坠。“*快去看看*！”

En: Look, I've got this decomposing body I *really* need to put in a fridge down there.
Zh: “是这样的，我这里有具腐烂的尸体，*真的*需要放到下面的冰箱里。”

En: You fool! You've gone and unleashed unimaginable evil, all for the sake of a refrigerator!
Zh: “你这个白痴！你释放了难以想象的邪恶力量，而这一切就是为了一台该死的冰箱！”

En: I suppose... it's all over now? Her shoulders slump, as if a heavy burden lies on them. "I guess there's no escape."
Zh: “我猜…现在已经结束了？”她的肩膀塌了下来，好像上面压着什么沉重的负担。“我想已经无路可逃了。”

En: It was the only way to get this investigation going.
Zh: “这是唯一能让调查继续的方法。”

En: Well, I've looked around... I can tell you what I saw.
Zh: “呃，我看了一圈…我能告诉你到底看见了什么。”

En: You... broke down the back door? She looks sad and confused. "Why? I gave you the key."
Zh: “你…把后门撞坏了？”她看起来悲伤又困惑。“为什么？我不是给你钥匙了吗。”

En: I hope you can find the solution quickly. The wards are broken, the passage has been opened... She shudders. "Now tell me, what's back there?"
Zh: “希望你能尽快找到解决办法。守护一破，通道就会被打开…”她颤抖着。“现在告诉我，那里有什么？”

En: Real men don't need keys.
Zh: “真男人是不需要钥匙的。”

En: I'm not sure, I don't know what I was thinking.
Zh: “我不确定，我不知道自己在想什么。”

En: The door was giving me shit.
Zh: “那扇门让我很不舒服。”

En: Never mind, I had other questions.
Zh: “没关系，我还有其他问题。”

En: Oh my god, things have gotten out of hand! She unloads immediately: "I thought the psychic stuff was bad, but the crime... it's even worse. I haven't been able to come to work for *three days*, the shop has been locked. No sales, gang-bangers running around, shooting everyone. I know I shouldn't have bought a shop in this *ghetto*."
Zh: “天啦，情况已经失控了！”她瞬间卸下了负担：“我以为灵魂之类的东西已经很糟糕了，但是犯罪…比这个还要糟糕。我已经*三天*没来上班，商店一直是关着的。没有生意，黑帮分子到处游荡，朝所有人胡乱开枪。我就知道不该在这种*贫民区*买下一间店铺。”

En: In the meantime, do buy the books. As many as you can. They won't be here for much longer. Determination shines in her eyes. "Is there anything else I can help you with?"
Zh: “同时，买些书吧。尽量多买些。它们不会在这里停留太久的。”她的眼睛里闪烁出坚毅的目光。“还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: A golden pendant hangs around the woman's neck, in the shape of what looks like a tiny fish head trapped in amber.
Zh: 女人的脖子上挂着一只金色吊坠，形状像是一只困在琥珀里的小鱼头。

En: A curious pendant you're wearing. (Narrow your eyes mysteriously.)
Zh: “你戴的吊坠还挺奇怪的。”（故弄玄虚地眯起眼睛。）

En: Oh yes, helps to have an anchor in these times. She clutches the pendant and narrows her eyes as well.
Zh: “是啊，这种时候有个依靠还是有帮助的。”她握紧吊坠，也跟着眯起双眼。

En: One has to earn one's success, even if one is a police officer. Handouts are nothing but manipulation, all they do is make you dependent.
Zh: “成功是自己争取来的，就算警察也是一样。施舍物不过是一种操纵手段，它们只会让你变得更依赖别人。”

En: Certainly there are good things to be said about dependence.
Zh: 当然了，依赖也有好的一面。

En: It would forge ties between us working people -- good practice for fighting our common enemy.
Zh: “它可以铸造劳动人民之间的联系——这是对抗共同敌人的好方法。”

En: You misunderstand me. I'm a powerful feudal lord. I demand tribute. This is about traditions.
Zh: “你误解我了。我是个强大的封建领主。我要求供奉。这跟传统有关。”

En: Damn, you're right. What kind of a business relationship would that kick-start?
Zh: “见鬼，你是对的。这会促成哪门子的商业关系啊？”

En: Excuse me, I don't even know why I said that. A lapse of professionalism that does not represent my values.
Zh: “请原谅，我甚至都不明白自己在说些什么。一次专业精神上的失误，不能代表我的价值观念。”

En: Now hey there. She raises her finger. "Sounds like someone isn't taking responsibility for the energy they bring into this space..."
Zh: “现在看来。”她竖起手指。“某人好像不想为自己带到这里的能量负责啊……”

En: Now hey there. She raises her finger. "Sounds like someone isn't taking responsibility for the energy they bring into this space..."
Zh: “现在看来。”她竖起手指。“某人好像不想为自己带到这里的能量负责啊……”

En: Exactly. In this kind of a business relationship I could come and critique your work any time. I could demand things from you, limiting your creative freedom.
Zh: “没错。在这种商业关系中，我可以随时批判你的工作。我可以向你提出要求，限制你的创造自由。”

En: Fighting, enemy... my philosophy is everyone just getting along.
Zh: “战斗，敌人…我的哲学就是每个人都能和睦相处。”

En: Tribute, power... these are not the traditions we're used to in this part of the world.
Zh: “供奉，力量…这些不是我们这个地区的传统。”

En: We wouldn't want that.
Zh: “我们可不想那样。”

En: Oh my. Of course. She looks at you and adds, "What a man."
Zh: “天啦，当然了。”她看着你，然后补充到，“真是条汉子。”

En: She's energetically impressed with you and your values. This is sincere. She's the kind of person who believes in telling a man that he's *quite the man* while he's being very manly.
Zh: 她被你和你的价值观打动了，这是非常真挚的。她是这样一种人：她相信当一个男人表现地很有男子气概时，就应该告诉他*你还真是个爷们*。

En: It's always nice to see you here. You bring good energy.
Zh: “能在这里看见你真的很开心。你带来了很好的能量。”

En: Hey, I'm the one who got shot here. In the leg... *and* in the shoulder!
Zh: “喂，我才是这里中枪的人。腿上一颗…*还有*肩膀也有一颗。”

En: Uh... I'm the one who got shot here, ma'am.
Zh: “呃…我才是这里中枪的人，夫人。”

En: Say nothing, stare at her.
Zh: 什么也不说，只是盯着她。

En: And you... oh my god, just look at you! It looks like even the *police* can't take care of all this. Someone should do something about this. She rubs her pendant between two fingers, thinking.
Zh: “还有你…我的天啦，看看你！感觉就连*警察*也没办法处理这些问题。有人必须做点什么了。”她把吊坠夹在两只手指之间摩挲着，若有所思。

En: Oh my god, even the *police* can't take care of all this. Just look at you! You're a wreck. Someone should do something about this. She rubs her pendant between two fingers, thinking.
Zh: “天啦，就连*警察*也没办法处理这些问题。看看你！都快成残废了。有人应该做点什么。”她把吊坠夹在两只手指之间摩挲着，若有所思。

En: Oh my god, even the *police* can't take care of all this... just look at that limp! Someone should do something about this. She rubs her pendant between two fingers, thinking.
Zh: “天啦，就连*警察*也没办法处理这些问题…看看你这跛脚。有人应该做点什么。”她把吊坠夹在两只手指之间摩挲着，若有所思。

En: Maybe... I should close the bookstore and open a *psychic studio*! Yes! She nods to herself. "It makes sense. Crime is the sixth element, you see. The darkest element."
Zh: “也许…我应该关掉这家书店，开一间*灵媒工作室*！没错！”她点点头。“确实说得通。你也看见了，犯罪就是第六种元素——黑暗元素。”

En: I could teach people to protect themselves against bad energies and to fight crime.
Zh: “我可以教会人们保护自己，抵抗坏能量，同时也能打击犯罪。”

En: (Nod.) "Sounds like a plan. Life must go on, after all."
Zh: （点头。）“听起来不错。毕竟生活总要继续。”

En: Honestly, I'm not sure what to think of it.
Zh: “老实说，我不确定该怎么想。”

En: A 'psychic studio'? This is even worse than a bookstore in Martinaise...
Zh: “一间‘灵媒工作室’？这比马丁内斯开书店更糟糕……”

En: No need to be modest, good officer. You've convinced me -- it is a great idea! Thank you for the inspiration.
Zh: “不用这么谦虚，我的好警官。你已经说服了我——这是个很棒的主意！谢谢你给我的灵感。”

En: No, no... you're right, officer! You've convinced me -- it is a great idea! Thank you for the inspiration.
Zh: “不，不…你是对的，警官！你已经说服了我——这是个很棒的主意！谢谢你给我的灵感。”

En: Exactly. It's a brilliant idea. Thank you, officer! I'm going to start drawing up business plans right away.
Zh: “没错，这主意真的很棒。谢谢你，警官！我马上就开始起草商业计划。”

En: Yes, what Martinaise really needs is more vigilantes roaming the streets.
Zh: “是的，马丁内斯的街道上真的需要更多治安维持者。”

En: You *have to* let me to the cellar. There's something I desperately need from there.
Zh: “你*必须*让我进地窖。那里有我非常需要的东西。”

En: Who, what? I don't understand what you're talking about. I can't, in good conscience, risk releasing the *bane of doom* upon all because you need something from the cellar.
Zh: “谁，什么？我不明白你在说什么。凭良心讲，我不能只凭你需要地窖里的某些东西，就冒险把*末日灾厄*全部放出来。”

En: In fact, she pauses to think, "It's odd how many of these troubles started with her departure. How do I know she's not behind the curse?! We definitely have to contain it now."
Zh: “其实，”她停下来想了一会儿，“很多麻烦是从她离开之后开始的，这很古怪。我怎么知道诅咒不是她施放的？！我们现在必须控制住它。”

En: Uh, this Dick Mullen book fell apart before I got to the end. Do you have another copy? (Show her the item.)
Zh: “呃，这本迪克·马伦的书坏了，我都还没看完。你这里还有其他副本吗？”（把东西拿给她看。）

En: Oh, I'm very sorry, sir. Some of these old paperbacks just don't have the durability. I would be happy to sell you another one, but unfortunately it's the last copy we had in stock.
Zh: “哦，非常抱歉，先生。有些老旧的平装书耐久性不是很好。我也很想再卖给你一本，不过很可惜，我们的库存里只有这最后一本了。”

En: Have you ever thought about a sale? Maybe this could lure in some customers.
Zh: “你有没有想过搞促销？也许可以吸引一些顾客。”

En: Discount my wares?! I can see, sir, that you don't value books very highly.
Zh: “给我的商品打折？！我明白了，先生，你并不怎么重视这些书的价值。”

En: That's right, and all you have to do is give us the key to the back door.
Zh: “没错，你只需要把后门钥匙交给我们就行。”

En: Is *that* what you've been after this whole time? You want the key to the back door?
Zh: “难道一直以来，你才是*这件事*的幕后主使吗？”

En: Yes! The key! Can we have it?
Zh: “没错！钥匙！能交给我们吗？”

En: Attaboy.
Zh: 好样的。

En: Alright -- Take two!
Zh: 好了——再来一次！

En: You know, maybe this time you should just be upfront with her?
Zh: 您知道吗，也许这次应该对她坦率一点？

En: Why are you bothering with this tiresome woman -- just open the curtains while she's distracted!
Zh: 为什么还要为这个麻烦的女人费心——乘她不注意的时候打开窗帘就好了啊！

En: I can assure you, he is... a police officer. Very... knowledgeable.
Zh: “我可以向你保证，他是个…警察。非常的…聪明。”

En: Yessss, this is your chance! Only a *remote viewer* can solve this case.
Zh: 没错，这是您的机会！只有*遥视者*可以解决这个案件。

En: Why are you so uptight about those curtains? I just want to know what's on the other side.
Zh: “你为什么这么紧张那些窗帘？我只想知道另一边有什么。”

En: I already told you, it's just a storage room for employees! I don't understand why it's so important to you...
Zh: “我已经告诉过你了，只是个雇员用的储藏室！我不明白它为什么对你那么重要…”

En: If it's just a storage room then why does it have a Semenese ward protecting it?
Zh: “如果只是一间储藏室，为什么会有塞美尼守护在保护它？”

En: If it's just a storage room then why do you have that strange trinket on the curtains?
Zh: “如果只是一间储藏室，为什么窗帘上会有些奇怪的小装饰品？”

En: It's not just a storage room, I already looked... There's another door behind the curtains.
Zh: “它不只是一个储藏室，我已经看过了…窗帘背后还有一扇门。”

En: It's just for decoration! She wavers under your gaze, mouth pressed into a tight-lipped smile... then something breaks:
Zh: “那只是装饰！”在你的注视下，她有些动摇，嘴唇拼命挤出一丝浅浅的微笑…然后某种东西破裂了：

En: Okay... fine! It's because this place is *cursed*, just like everyone said! They don't call it the Doomed Commercial Area for nothing!
Zh: “好吧…好吧！因为这个地方被*诅咒*了，就像大家说的一样！大家把这里叫做诅咒商业区不是毫无理由的！”

En: She's so tense it's a miracle she hasn't snapped in half yet.
Zh: 她真的很紧张，现在还没失控真是奇迹。

En: Okay... fine! It's because this place is *cursed*, just like Annette said! They don't call it the Doomed Commercial Area for nothing!
Zh: “好吧…好吧！因为这个地方被*诅咒*了，就像安妮特说的那样！大家把这里叫做诅咒商业区不是毫无理由的！”

En: She recites it like it's a poem, or like she's playing a role she's grown tired of.
Zh: 她像是在背诵一首诗歌，又像是在扮演一个早已厌倦的角色。

En: *What-did-you-do?!* she demands in a single breath, hand clasped around the pendant. "You *looked?*"
Zh: “*你做了什么？！*”她用手紧紧握住吊坠，一口气问到。“你*看过*了？”

En: Yes, when you weren't paying attention.
Zh: “是的，乘你没注意的时候。”

En: It's not like I ripped the curtains open or anything, it was just a little peek...
Zh: “我也没有把窗帘撕开什么的，只是稍微瞥了一眼…”

En: No, you misunderstood me -- I didn't 'look' with my 'eyes', I used my inner vision to 'get a read' on the Darkness... *metaphysically*.
Zh: “不，你误会我了——我不是用自己的‘眼睛’去‘看’的，我用的是内心视觉，‘读取’了黑暗中的信息…是*超自然*的。”

En: The Darkness... She closes her eyes and nods. "It must be real then... This place is cursed, just like Annette said. They don't call it the Doomed Commercial Area just for nothing!"
Zh: “黑暗…”她闭上双眼，点了点头。“那一定是真的了…这个地方被诅咒了，就像安妮特说的那样。大家把这里叫做诅咒商业区不是毫无理由的！”

En: If it's just a storage room then it wouldn't hurt if I just peeked inside...
Zh: “如果只是个储藏室，那我稍微看看里面应该也没什么大不了的…”

En: No! She wavers under your gaze, mouth pressed into a tight-lipped frown... then she breaks down:
Zh: “不！”在你的注视下，她有些动摇，嘴唇拼命挤出一丝浅浅的微笑…然后她崩溃了：

En: Why are you still talking about books? Are you trying to put a spell on me?
Zh: “你为什么还在聊书的事？你是想对我施咒吗？”

En: A spell! As if! She snorts. "This place doesn't need any spells or hoo-doo mumbo-jumbo, this place has *wonderful* energy!" She wavers under your gaze, mouth pressed into a tight-lipped smile... then something breaks:
Zh: “咒语！说的好像真的一样！”她哼了一声。“这个地方不需要咒语，或者什么神秘魔力，这个地方的能量*非常好*！”在你的注视下，她有些动摇，嘴唇拼命挤出一丝浅浅的微笑…然后她崩溃了：

En: If it's just a storage room then it wouldn't hurt if I just peeked inside... It's not like it's *cursed*, right?
Zh: “如果只是个储藏室，那我稍微看看里面应该也没什么大不了的…又不是说它被*诅咒*了什么的，对吧？”

En: If it's just a storage room then why are you so tense?
Zh: “如果只是个储藏室，那你为什么这么紧张？”

En: Just let it go, officer! Go buy some god damn books, you're supposed to be... *drawn* to the books!
Zh: “放过它吧，警官！买几本好书，你应该…被那些书*吸引*的！”

En: I'm not tense, I'm just... She wavers under your gaze, mouth pressed into a tight-lipped frown... then something breaks:
Zh: “我不紧张，我只是…”在你的注视下，她有些动摇，嘴唇拼命挤出一丝浅浅的微笑…然后她崩溃了：

En: You *looked*, you let the spirits in! she shouts, her face getting red. "We are all doomed now, this place is cursed, just like Annette said. They don't call it the Doomed Commercial Area just for nothing!"
Zh: “你*看过*了，你把那些幽灵放进来了！”她大喊着，脸涨得通红。“现在我们都得完蛋，这个地方被诅咒了，就像安妮特说的那样。大家把这里叫做诅咒商业区不是毫无理由的！”

En: You *looked*, you let the spirits in! Her face is getting red. "We are all doomed now, this place is cursed, just like everyone said. They don't call it the Doomed Commercial Area just for nothing!"
Zh: “你*看过*了，你把那些幽灵放进来了！”她大喊着，脸涨得通红。“现在我们都得完蛋，这个地方被诅咒了，就像安妮特说的那样。大家把这里叫做诅咒商业区不是毫无理由的！”

En: And you saw a *door*? She closes her eyes. "It must be real then... This place is cursed, just like everyone said. They don't call it the Doomed Commercial Area just for nothing!"
Zh: “你看见了*一扇门*？”她闭上双眼。“那一定是真的了…这个地方被诅咒了，就像安妮特说的那样。大家把这里叫做诅咒商业区不是毫无理由的！”

En: I'm not sure whether I want to know what else the spirits have told you in your *visions*... She shudders and starts mumbling something to her pendant.
Zh: “我不确定自己想不想知道——你*看见*的那些幽灵还跟你说了什么…”她颤抖着，开始对脖子上的吊坠自言自语。

En: Are you happy now, officer? Happy that you've *ruined* everything? She closes her eyes and starts mumbling something to her pendant.
Zh: “现在你高兴了吧，警官？因为*毁掉*一切而幸灾乐祸？”她闭上双眼，开始对脖子上的吊坠自言自语。

En: Host of Hosts, she prays, "guard me and my honest business venture from the curse that lurks behind the curtains..."
Zh: “万主之主，”她祈祷着，“保护我和我诚信的企业，不受潜伏在窗帘之后的诅咒侵袭…”

En: It's not just a storage room, I already saw that... There's another door behind the curtains.
Zh: “它不只是个储藏室，我已经看见了…窗帘背后还有一扇门。”

En: Of course it's not just a storage room! she suddenly shouts, her face getting red. "But what does it matter now, you already let the spirits in... We are all doomed now."
Zh: “它当然不只是个储藏室了！”她突然大喊起来，脸也涨得通红。“但是现在都不重要了，你已经把幽灵放了进来…我们全都得完蛋。”

En: It's just like everyone said, this place is cursed. They don't call it the Doomed Commercial Area just for nothing. She closes her eyes, visibly defeated, and starts mumbling something to her pendant.
Zh: “就像大家说的一样，这个地方被诅咒了。他们把这里叫诅咒商业区可不是毫无理由的。”她闭上双眼，显然有些挫败，接着开始对着吊坠嘟囔起来。

En: It's just like Annette said, this place is cursed. They don't call it the Doomed Commercial Area just for nothing. She closes her eyes, visibly defeated, and starts mumbling something to her pendant.
Zh: “就像安妮特说的，这个地方被诅咒了。他们把这里叫诅咒商业区可不是毫无理由的。”她闭上双眼，显然有些挫败，接着开始对着吊坠嘟囔起来。

En: Plaisance, I have something to tell you... I've found the actual source of doom.
Zh: “普莱桑斯，我有些话要告诉你…我找到了厄运真正的源头。”

En: What do you mean the *actual* source? She clutches her pendant anxiously. "I thought this issue was resolved!"
Zh: “你说*真正的*源头是什么意思？”她紧张地握住自己的吊坠。“我还以为这件事已经解决了！”

En: Yes? What news do you bring? Her fingers grasp tightly at her pendant.
Zh: “怎么样？你带来什么消息了吗？”她的手指紧紧攥住了吊坠。

En: I'm afraid all I can tell you is this -- you must leave Martinaise immediately. It's too dangerous to stay.
Zh: “恐怕我不能告诉你——你肯定会马上离开马丁内斯的。待在这里实在是太危险了。”

En: Leave? She blinks twice, mulling over your words. Then: "Officer, I must *insist* that you tell me what you've uncovered! I can't just leave everything on a whim..."
Zh: “离开？”她眨了两下眼，仔细琢磨着你的话，然后：“警官，我*坚持*你把发现的一切都告诉我！我不能因为一时兴起把一切都抛诸脑后……”

En: A... a what?
Zh: “一个…什么？”

En: A tiny hole... in reality. It may be connected with pale, an origin point of sorts. It would explain why historically so many things have ended in failure here in Martinaise.
Zh: “现实里…有一个小洞。它也许跟灰域是相连的，类似某种源点。这就解释了马丁内斯的历史上为什么有那么多事最后都以失败告终。”

En: But... but... *that's* not in any of the ancient texts! How am I supposed to protect my bookstore from *that*?!
Zh: “但是…但是…远古文献里没有任何*那方面*的记载啊！我怎么能保护书店不受*那个*的影响呢？！”

En: You can protect it with hope, by refusing to give up. That's what people have done in the past -- by building a church, a place of worship around this thing.
Zh: “你可以拒绝放弃，用希望去守护它。过去人们都是这么做的——在这个东西附近建造了一座教堂，一个礼拜的场所。”

En: You can't protect it -- not against *pale*. Close up the shop and try to get as far away from this thing as possible.
Zh: “你保护不了它——无法抵抗*灰域*。关掉书店，尽可能离这个东西远一点吧。”

En: You'll have to find your own answer. I've spent too long on this quest as it is.
Zh: “你需要自己找出答案。我已经在这件事上浪费掉太多太多时间了。”

En: Close... the shop? But it's all I have! No, there *must* be a way...
Zh: “关掉…商店？但我只有它了！不，*肯定*还有其他办法的…”

En: You're *right*, officer. I can't just leave everything -- after all this work! This little bookstore has become the *cornerstore* of our neighbourhood -- I can't just jump the ship and leave.
Zh: “你是*对的*，警官。我不能就这么放下一切——特别是付出这么多心血之后！这间小书店已经变成我们街区的*街角小店*——我不能就这么跳上船离开。”

En: Yes, you've certainly done your part. I've come so far... I can't give up now...
Zh: “是的，你已经完成了自己的工作。我已经走了这么远…不能现在放弃…”

En: Thank you for your help, in any case. You're welcome back here anytime.
Zh: “不管怎么说，还是谢谢你的帮助。我这里随时欢迎你的到来。”

En: I need to know what *secrets* lie behind the door... It's part of a *spiritual quest*.
Zh: “我需要知道这扇门背后隐藏着什么*秘密*…这是*精神探索*的一部分。”

En: No? The storekeeper has crossed her arms.
Zh: “不行？”书店店主抱起双臂。

En: Crime bros. (Nod at Kim.) "We're called *crime bros*, my partner and I."
Zh: “罪恶兄弟。”（对着金点头。）“大家都把我和我的搭档叫做，*罪恶兄弟*。”

En: You're called... *what*? She peers at you over her glasses. "What is this, a joke, mister policeman? Do you commit *crimes* together or what?"
Zh: “你叫…*什么*？”她透过眼镜盯着你。“这是干什么，讲笑话吗，警官先生？你们一起*犯罪*吗，还是什么？”

En: The lieutenant looks at you, waiting to see where this is going.
Zh: 警督看着你，等着看接下来的发展。

En: You've got this. Just go with it, this is *elegant*.
Zh: 您可以的。就这样继续吧，这就叫*优雅*。

En: He turns away, but not before you can see a small hint of a smile -- he's struggling to not crack up.
Zh: 他转过身，但你还是看见一丝微笑的痕迹——他努力不让自己大笑出来。

En: Uh... Sorry, this came out wrong again.
Zh: “呃…抱歉，好像又错了。”

En: No, not like this, we're called crime bros because we like to *solve* crimes... as brothers in arms!
Zh: “不，不是这样的，别人叫我们罪恶兄弟，是因为我们喜欢*破获*犯罪案件…并肩作战！”

En: Desert pygmy shamans...? That sounds like a rather questionable way to describe a group of people.
Zh: 沙漠侏儒萨满…？用这种方式描述一个群体，听起来似乎有些可疑。

En: Didn't... didn't that curtain just *move*?!
Zh: 那个…那个窗帘刚刚好像是*动了*？！

En: Strange... I feel unwanted too. What does it mean?
Zh: “奇怪…我也感觉到很多余。这是什么意思？”

En: Truly so? Her eyes narrow, as she tries to get a read on your energy. "Perhaps the dark energies are leeching off you? You shouldn't stay in the store too long, it may be dangerous."
Zh: “真是这样吗？”她眯起眼睛，像是在尝试阅读你的能量。“也许黑暗能量在蚕食你？你不应该在商店里待得太久，也许会有危险。”

En: Thank you, sir. A timid sigh of relief, followed by a cautious smile. "There's one more thing I haven't told you about yet... *the Entity*."
Zh: “谢谢你，先生。”她胆怯地叹了一口气，接着露出了谨慎的笑容。“还有一件事我没有告诉你…那个*存在*。”

En: The Entity?
Zh: “存在？”

En: Of course, the Entity... (Close your eyes.) "For I have sensed its presence."
Zh: “当然了，存在…”（闭上双眼。）“因为我已经感觉到它的存在。”

En: You have? She gasps. "The Entity takes the form of a woman, a witch probably. I've suspected that she must be connected to the curse ever since I first saw her. Did you know that she lives inside the *chimney*?"
Zh: “你有吗？”她喘息到。“存在是一个女人的形象，也许是女巫。我怀疑当我第一次看见她时，她就已经跟诅咒有关系了。你知不知道她住在*烟囱*里？”

En: Yes, a malignant *Entity* that lives inside the chimney... it takes the form of a woman. A witch, most likely. She or *it* must be connected to the curse somehow.
Zh: “是的，一个住在烟囱里的恶毒*存在*…是一个女人的形象。很有可能是女巫。她，或者是*它*跟诅咒有着某种关联。”

En: Chimneys aren't big enough for that.
Zh: “烟囱怕是不够大吧。”

En: Chimney... the passage between heaven and hell, of course...
Zh: “当然了，烟囱…是天堂与地狱之间的通道…”

En: No, this chimney is part of the building's central furnace and it's enormous. She has barricaded herself behind some metal security curtains... God knows what she's doing there.
Zh: “不，烟囱只是这栋建筑采暖系统的一部分，而且它非常庞大。她把自己封锁在金属防火幕背后…天知道她在那里做了什么。”

En: Some unnatural magic, I assume. She shivers. "You should go find *the Entity* and ask what happened to all the companies in the building. What is the source of this curse? Here's the key to the warded door behind the curtains, take it."
Zh: “我猜，应该是些非自然的魔法吧。”她颤抖着。“你应该找到*存在*，问问这栋建筑里的公司都怎么样了。还有诅咒的源头是什么？这是窗帘后那扇门的钥匙，给你。”

En: Yes, that chimney is part of the building's central furnace and it's enormous. She has barricaded herself behind some metal security curtains... God knows what she's doing there.
Zh: “没错，烟囱只是这栋建筑采暖系统的一部分，而且它非常庞大。她把自己封锁在金属防火幕背后…天知道她在那里做了什么。”

En: Oh and... please, do return to me after you've looked around. I'm quite... *anxious* to know what she has to say about the curse. What you *discover* in there...
Zh: “哦，对了…你看过之后，一定要记得回来找我。我很…*着急*，想知道她怎么说那个诅咒。还有你在那里*发现*了什么…”

En: The Entity? No, I don't think so.
Zh: “存在？不，我不这么觉得。”

En: Of course, the Entity... (Close your eyes.) "I didn't see her, but I sensed her presence."
Zh: “当然了，存在…”（闭上双眼。）“我没看见她，但我感觉到她的存在。”

En: You did? She gasps. "Then it has be true... I've suspected that this woman-shaped energy must be connected to the curse ever since I first saw her. Did you see that she lives inside the *chimney*?"
Zh: “你有吗？”她喘息到。“那一定是真的……我怀疑当我第一次看见她时，她就已经跟诅咒有关系了。你没看见她住在*烟囱*里吗？”

En: No, this chimney is part of the building's central furnace and it's enormous. She has barricaded herself behind some metal security curtains... God knows what she's doing there.
Zh: “不，烟囱只是这栋建筑采暖系统的一部分，而且它非常庞大。她把自己封锁在金属防火幕背后…天知道她在那里做了什么。”

En: Yes, the malignant *Entity* that lives inside the chimney... It takes the shape of a woman, a witch probably. I've suspected that she knows more about the curse than she lets on ever since I first saw her.
Zh: “没错，恶毒的*存在*住在烟囱里…它是个女人的形象，可能是个女巫。我一直很怀疑，当我第一次看见她的时候，她知道的绝对比假装出来的要多的多。”

En: Yes, that chimney is part of the building's central furnace and it's enormous. She has barricaded herself behind some metal security curtains... God knows what she's doing there.
Zh: “没错，烟囱只是这栋建筑采暖系统的一部分，而且它非常庞大。她把自己封锁在金属防火幕背后…天知道她在那里做了什么。”

En: But... before we go on... did you encounter *the malignant Entity?* Her eyes narrow, as she whispers the name.
Zh: “但是…在我们继续之前…你有没有遇到那个*恶毒的存在*？”她眯起眼睛，低声说出了那个名字。

En: Chimneys aren't big enough for anyone to live there.
Zh: “烟囱不够大吧，谁也没办法生活在里面。”

En: Chimney... the passage between heaven and hell, of course. It all makes sense now!
Zh: “当然了，烟囱…是天堂与地狱之间的通道。现在一切都说得通了！”

En: Some unnatural magic, I assume. She shivers. "You should go find the *Entity* and ask what happened to all the companies in the building. And do return to me after you've talked to it. I'm quite *anxious* to know what she has to say about the curse."
Zh: “我猜，应该是些非自然的魔法吧。”她颤抖着。“你应该找到*存在*，问问这栋建筑里的公司都怎么样了。跟它聊完之后记得回来找我。我*急着*想知道她怎么看那个诅咒。”

En: Yes, I've heard that it's enormous. She has barricaded herself behind some metal security curtains upstairs... God knows what she's doing there.
Zh: “是的，我听说它很庞大。她把自己封锁在楼上的金属防火幕背后…天知道她在那里做了什么。”

En: You mean the chimney that's part of the central furnace? I heard *voices* coming from there...
Zh: “你是说烟囱是中央壁炉的一部分？我在里面听见*声音*了…”

En: You mean the chimney that's part of the central furnace?
Zh: “你是说烟囱是采暖系统的一部分？”

En: The curse... is so much worse than you could imagine. It's a *disease*, eating at the very foundation. A shiver runs through the woman, as she looks around the dimly lit store.
Zh: 女人看着灯光昏暗的商店，浑身止不住地颤抖。“那个诅咒……比你想象的还要糟糕。它是一种*疾病*，蚕食着所有根基。”

En: It's the curse of *financial distress*. Her voice drops to a whisper. "Of *ruin* -- and bankruptcy." She peers at the curtains again...
Zh: 她的声音变成一阵低语：“是*财务困境*的诅咒。是*毁灭*——是破产。”她又盯住了窗帘…

En: Okay. I'm a little confused. What does that mean?
Zh: “好吧。我有点困惑。那是什么意思？”

En: Ah! Annette mentioned that the previous tenants have experienced some financial troubles.
Zh: “啊！安妮特提过前任租客遇到了一些财务问题。”

En: Wait, that's it? I was hoping for something more... *paranatural*.
Zh: “等等，就这些？我还希望是些更…*超自然*的东西呢。”

En: But officer, there's nothing *natural* about entire companies declaring bankruptcy! I'm talking about *caco-daemons* feeding off bad business practices and disappointing income statements!
Zh: “但是警官，所有公司宣布破产根本没有*自然*可言！我说的可是*邪魔*，专门以不良商业行为和令人失望的损益表为食的恶魔！”

En: There's something *wrong* with this building, I can tell you. Ever since I arrived, I've sensed an eerie lingering presence -- as if I was *unwanted* here.
Zh: “我可以告诉你，这栋建筑有些*不对劲*。自从我到了这里，就感觉到一种可怕的存在——就好像我在这里是*多余的*。”

En: It's not just that, officer, we're dealing with something *supra-natural* here. It's the *caco-daemons* feeding off bad business practices and disappointing income statements!
Zh: “不只是那样的，警官，我们对付的可是*超自然*力量。它是一个*邪魔*，专门以不良商业行为和令人失望的损益表为食的恶魔！”

En: Everyone knows that all the previous companies in this building have sooner or later declared bankruptcy. And their *malicious spirits* are still here, feeding off bad business practices and disappointing income statements!
Zh: “所有人都知道这栋建筑之前的公司前前后后都宣告破产了。而且那些*恶毒幽灵*还停留在这里，专门以不良商业行为和令人失望的损益表为食！”

En: Have you sought help from anyone?
Zh: “你有向谁寻求过帮助吗？”

En: Yes, I've contacted numerous parapsychologists and even a pair of Semenese mediators -- they provided me with the wards. She nods at the strange cage-like trinket on the curtains.
Zh: “有的，我联系过很多通灵者，甚至还有一对塞美尼灵媒——他们帮我做了这些守护。”她朝着窗帘上那个像笼子一样的奇怪装饰点点头。

En: The wards help to keep the doom at bay and protect us against the darkness that lies further in the building. Even though now I fear... it's not enough.
Zh: “守护能够帮助困住厄运，保护我们不受隐藏在这栋建筑里的黑暗力量伤害。只是现在，我恐怕…还是不够。”

En: What about the wards on the back door? Are these Semenese as well?
Zh: “那后门上的守护呢？也是塞美尼的吗？”

En: Yes, the Semenese are very crafty and their wards are extremely powerful. Lesser wards simply won't do here.
Zh: “是的，塞美尼人很狡诈，他们的守护非常强大。稍微弱点的根本就没用。”

En: And what's behind that door if I may ask?
Zh: “如果可以的话，我能问下门后有什么吗？”

En: Failure... so much more failure! She turns away from you, shaking her head.
Zh: “失败…更多的失败！”她背过身去，摇摇头。

En: I heard that the Union has squeezed out all the local business to fund their strike... How is your store still up and running?
Zh: “我听说工会压榨所有本地企业来支援他们罢工…为什么你的商店还在正常营运？”

En: She fingers the pendant around her neck, thinking. "It must be these very spells and wards of protection that keep me safe from all the ill and wicked deeds."
Zh: 她用手摆弄着脖子上的吊坠，若有所思。“一定是那些咒语和守护保护我不受那些邪恶和不道德行为的伤害。”

En: Most certainly not! I'm not letting you open the gates of hell. In fact, I don't want *anyone* who isn't familiar with the psychic arts involved in this mess. Stay away. Leave the spirits be, so they can return to their slumber.
Zh: “绝对不行！我是不会让你打开那扇地狱之门的。其实，我不想让*任何*不熟悉通灵艺术的人卷入这场混乱。请离远一点。别管那些幽灵，这样它们就能继续沉睡。”

En: Doesn't seem like the spell is working... There are no customers around except you.
Zh: 看起来咒语没有生效…周围除了你没有其他客人。

En: Honestly, it seems like this pendant thing is a scam. You could be doing so much more.
Zh: “老实说，这个吊坠像是个骗局。你本来还有更多事可以做。”

En: There are hardly any customers in the store. Do you think it's really working?
Zh: “店里基本没什么客人。你真觉得它有用吗？”

En: I'm only trying to help... What about some neon signs and blinking lights?
Zh: “我只是想帮忙…不如来点霓虹灯，再来点闪光灯怎么样？”

En: Sir, this is a bookstore, not a stripclub. No one would take us seriously if we put up some horrendous neon lights.
Zh: “先生，这是家书店，不是脱衣舞俱乐部。如果真我们搞些霓虹灯的话，没人会把我们当回事的……”

En: You could try to be friendlier with the customers.
Zh: “你可以试着对客人友好一点。”

En: Maybe you should try to *reconceptualize* your business... Martinaise doesn't look like the best place for a bookshop.
Zh: “也许你应该试试*重构*自己的事业…马丁内斯不像最适合开书店的地方。”

En: She stands silent for a second, before exhaling sharply. "You're right. A bookstore wasn't even my original plan, I actually wanted to sell esoteric paraphernalia to help to balance out this neighbourhood's dark energies."
Zh: 她沉默地站了一两秒，然后重重地吐出一口气。“你说得对。书店甚至都不是我最初的计划，其实我想卖些只有内行才懂的随身用具，帮助平衡一下这个街区的黑暗能量。”

En: My husband suggested otherwise. He said it's less... stupid. More appropriate. Cultured and all that.
Zh: “我的丈夫不这么认为。他说这没那么…蠢，更合适一点。而且还比较文雅之类的。”

En: Besides, this would only tempt the phantoms of doom... They can sense the desperation, you know.
Zh: “再说了，这样只会更加吸引厄运幽灵…它们可以感觉到绝望，你知道的。”

En: My voice is trained to perfection by true sales professionals, good sir! I paid good money for those courses.
Zh: “我的声音经过真正的销售专家培训，堪称完美，先生！我为那些课程花了不少钱。”

En: Great idea. What happened?
Zh: “这主意很棒啊。出什么事了？”

En: I'm not sure that a trinket shop is a better idea for business.
Zh: “我不确定小饰品商店这个主意会不会更好。”

En: Well, my husband would definitely agree with you. Her mouth has become a single tight line. "He's the one who suggested selling books. Less... *stupid*, he said. More *cultured*."
Zh: “嗯，我丈夫肯定会赞同你的意见。”她的嘴唇变成一条紧绷的直线。“他是建议我卖书的人。他说这样没那么…*愚蠢*。更有*文化*。”

En: But being *cultured* doesn't offer any protection against the curse, does it? She looks around the dusty store. "Maybe I shouldn't have listened to him after all..."
Zh: “但是*有文化*也不能抵抗诅咒啊，不是吗？”她环视着这个肮脏的店铺。“也许我根本就不该听他的……”

En: Too much money, even... She looks around the dusty store. "Perhaps I should have bought some wards instead."
Zh: “太多钱了，甚至……”她环视着这个肮脏的店铺。“也许我应该买点守护的。”

En: I talked to the *Entity* you told me about. Her name is Neha and she's a novelty dicemaker.
Zh: “我跟你口中的那个*存在*聊过了。她的名字叫妮亚，是一个骰子匠人。”

En: A novelty *dicemaker*? Her eyes widen. "Well, spit it out! Why does she need the dice -- for some kind of *sorcery*? Sometimes they use the ankle bones of sheep..."
Zh: “一个*骰子匠人*？”她睁大双眼。“呃，痛快说吧！她为什么需要骰子——是为了进行某种*巫术*吗？有时候他们会用绵羊的踝骨…”

En: She's not a sorceress -- or some 'malicious Entity'. She's a businesswoman, like you.
Zh: “她不是巫师——或者什么‘邪恶存在’。她是个女商人，跟你一样。”

En: No, ma'am. I have felt her aura. She is not the one to blame for this curse.
Zh: “不，夫人。我感觉过她的光环。她不是诅咒的罪魁祸首。”

En: She may be involved, but I don't have a way to question the malignant Entity further.
Zh: “她也许是有干系，但是我没能进一步质问那个邪恶存在。”

En: I don't understand. If it's not her, then where *is* the source of the Doom? How did she explain the *curse*? She looks perplexed.
Zh: 她看起来有些困惑。“我不明白。如果不是她，那厄运的源头又是*什么*？她是怎么解释这个*诅咒*的？”

En: Before you say anything, ask yourself. Is the woman really able to *withstand* the truth?
Zh: 在你开口之前，先问问自己。这个女人真的能够*承受*真相吗？

En: She says there is no curse, because there are too many inconsistencies.
Zh: “她说没有诅咒，因为实在有太多矛盾的地方了。”

En: She said it's just capitalism. Bankruptcy is a quirk of our economic system.
Zh: “她说那不过是资本主义。破产只是我们经济系统的一种怪癖。”

En: Wait, *WHAT*? Her entire face goes white as a skull. "The entire economic system is cursed?!"
Zh: “等等，*什么*？”她整张脸变得像骷髅一样惨白。“那整个经济系统都被诅咒了？！”

En: Too many *inconsistencies*? What kind of talk is that? The woman scoffs at you with an audible snort.
Zh: 你听见女人轻蔑地哼了一声，像是在嘲笑你。“太多*矛盾*的地方？这又是什么鬼话？”

En: I understand what's going on here. You went in there, rummaged around -- and accomplished nothing. And now you're telling me these *intellectual* things to cover up for it.
Zh: “我明白是怎么回事了。你进去，四处乱翻了一遍——但是一事无成。现在你又跟我说些*聪明话*，想掩盖这个事实。”

En: It's true. We live in a precarious world, nothing ever turns out right. You need to get used to chaos. (Proceed.)
Zh: “这是真的。我们住在一个岌岌可危的世界，没什么永远是对的。你得习惯混沌。”（继续。）

En: This woman's not healthy enough to withstand this level of stress.
Zh: 这个女人不够健康，应该承受不住这种级别的压力。

En: What I meant was... I found the source of the doom. Forget about the economy thing. (Back off.)
Zh: “我的意思是…我找到了厄运的源头。忘了那些经济的话题吧。”（放弃。）

En: Precarious world? Economy? She looks down at the pendant in her trembling hands...
Zh: “岌岌可危？经济？”她低头看向吊坠，手不住的颤抖……

En: Honestly, I don't have an answer yet. There are still leads to be followed, like that strange radiocomputer-thing...
Zh: “老实说，我也没找到答案。还有很多线索要跟，比如说那台像无线电脑一样的怪东西…”

En: (Lie.) "The source is in the taxidermist's shop. He became involved in arts darker than taxidermy and brought the void spirits down upon this place."
Zh: （撒谎。）“源头就在动物标本剥制师的店里。他卷入了比标本剥制术更黑暗的艺术里，把虚空幽灵引到了这里。”

En: Oh! How horrid. I *knew* something wasn't right about that place...
Zh: “哦！真是太可怕了！我就*知道*这个地方有些不对劲…”

En: Yes. I performed the ancient Semenese dispelling rites. This commercial area is doomed no more.
Zh: “是的。我做了远古塞美尼人的驱散仪式。这片商业区已经没有诅咒了。”

En: Yeah, I de-spooked it. The curse is definitely gone.
Zh: “没错，我驱逐了它。诅咒已经不在了。”

En: Yes. I utilized the secret methods of the Remote Viewers Division.
Zh: “是的。我用了遥视者部队的秘密方法。”

En: Yes. I called upon the spirit of *Guillaume le Million* to banish the curse in a flash of disco brilliance.
Zh: “是的。我召唤了*纪尧姆·列米利翁*的灵魂，在一阵迪斯科光芒之中，彻底驱除了诅咒。”

En: Guillaume le Million? The singer who made that awful face? Well... I *do* see the resemblance... She looks at you with sudden admiration.
Zh: “纪尧姆·列米利翁？那个做鬼脸的歌手？呃…我*确实*看到了一些相似之处…”她突然有些羡慕地看着你。

En: All that matters is that the energies are retreating. I can already *feel* the curse lifting! It will be a long time until we're *fully* free of it, of course. But still...
Zh: “重要的是，能量正在消退。我已经能*感觉*到诅咒被解除了！经过这么长时间，我们总算是可以*完全*解放了。不过…”

En: I understand that you can't divulge official secrets, officer. But know that I appreciate the efforts of your department. She looks at you with sudden admiration.
Zh: 女人点点头。“我能理解，你不能泄露官方机密，警官。但是我真的很感激你们部队的努力。”她突然有些羡慕地看着你。

En: What does that mean? Are you certain... Oh, but who am I to question your methods? She looks at you with sudden admiration.
Zh: “那是什么意思？你确定…哦，不过怎么轮得到我质疑你的方法呢？”她突然有些羡慕地看着你。

En: Tell me: Did you find a way to *break* the curse?
Zh: “告诉我：你找到*破解*诅咒的方法了吗？”

En: Thank you, officer. Truly. You've brought a better psychoemanation to this humble bookstore and that's no small achievement.
Zh: “谢谢你，警官。真心的。你给这间卑微的书店带来了更好的心灵能量，这可不是件小成就。”

En: Of course. I trust my Semenese wards and charms kept you safe while you performed the ancient rites... She looks at you with sudden admiration.
Zh: “当然。在你进行远古仪式的时候，我相信我的塞美尼守护和护身符能够保护你的安全…”她突然有些羡慕地看着你。

En: As I said, she's a novelty dicemaker. Her business has been up and running for a long time now.
Zh: “我说过了，她是一个新奇骰子匠人。她的生意已经持续很长一段时间了。”

En: First, *you're* one of those inconsistencies.
Zh: “首先，*你*就是其中一个矛盾。”

En: You're telling me that you went in there and didn't find out *anything* about the curse?! She looks disappointed.
Zh: “你是说你进去了半天，结果没发现*任何*跟诅咒有关的事？！”她看起来有些失望。

En: Of course, I should have known better than to put my hopes on a man like you... You're no Semenese ghostwhisperer, you're an alcoholic!
Zh: “当然，我早知道不该把希望寄托在你这样的男人身上…你不是塞美尼鬼语者，你只是个酒鬼！”

En: The investigation's over. I don't want you going in there anymore, you're only angering the psychic weather in here. Now, if you're not going to *buy* anything... She turns to face the door, waiting for potential customers.
Zh: “调查结束了。我不想让你再进去，你只会激怒这里的幽灵。现在，如果你打算*买*东西的话…”她转向门口，等待着潜在的顾客。

En: I am not doing as well as it looks like I am... She grasps her pendant. "I only have my Semenese wards to thank for the protection they provide."
Zh: “我做得没有看上去那么好…”她抓住自己的吊坠。“我只能感谢塞美尼守护的保护。”

En: Yes, because her business consists of the *psychic leech* that's been feeding the curse! Red blotches appear on her skin.
Zh: “是的，因为她的生意使用了*通灵血咒*，一直在供养诅咒！”她的皮肤上冒出红色的疹斑。

En: Good luck persuading her. Some people can rationalize *anything*.
Zh: 要想说服她，只能祝你好运了。有些人可以将*任何事情*合理化。

En: You found a way to fix our entire economic system...? She peers at you suspiciously. "Or are you talking about the building?"
Zh: “你找到修复整个经济系统的办法了吗…？”她怀疑地看着你。“还是你在说建筑的事？”

En: I see that you've fallen for her *manipulation*, detective. The investigation is over. I just hope that you haven't made anything *worse* by going in there.
Zh: “我能看出，你已经被她*操纵*了，警探。调查已经结束。希望你这次行动没有让情况变得*更糟糕*。”

En: I'm afraid that only world revolution can help you. (Proceed.)
Zh: “恐怕只有世界革命才能帮到你了。”（继续。）

En: Another revolution? She looks down at the pendant in her trembling hands, she collects herself.
Zh: “另一场革命？”她低头看着吊坠，手微微颤抖，接着又恢复了镇定。

En: I'm afraid that I can only identify the *source*, but you have to call some other experts to lift the curse.
Zh: “恐怕我只能确定*源头*，如果要驱散诅咒，只能找其他专家了。”

En: Oh, I see... She looks at her pendant, then back to you. "I guess it's time to visit Boogie Street again."
Zh: “哦，我明白了…”她看着吊坠，然后又看了看你。“我猜是时候再去一趟布吉街了。”

En: I'm sure that I'll find someone who can help me get rid of the doom that still remains in this house... Things are *improving*, I can feel it. She nods.
Zh: “我肯定能找到人，帮我驱除徘徊在这间房子里的诅咒…不过情况确实*有所好转*，我能感觉到。”她点了点头。

En: I am in no position to give out personal opinions.
Zh: “我没有资格提什么私人意见。”

En: Please, do tell! Did anything survive? Have you located the... Entity?
Zh: “请说吧！有什么东西活下来了吗？你有没有发现…那个存在？”

En: What are you talking about? It's just a door.
Zh: “你在说什么呢？只是一扇门而已。”

En: Just a door?! This place is cursed, detective! They don't call it the Doomed Commercial Area for nothing -- just look at the sheer amount of companies that have failed in this house!
Zh: “只是一扇门？这个地方被诅咒了，警探！大家把这里叫做诅咒商业区可不是毫无来由的——看看在这里倒闭的公司有多少！”

En: Host Almighty, she prays, "guard me and my honest business venture from the curse that lurks behind the door, blessed be your name..."
Zh: “全能的神啊，”她祈祷到，“保护我和我诚信的企业，不受潜伏在窗帘之后的诅咒侵袭，称颂你的姓名……”

En: I hope you're happy now, happy that you've *ruined* everything... Now that you've broken the door the curse is coming to get *me* as well. She closes her eyes and starts mumbling something to her pendant.
Zh: “你现在高兴了吧，因为你*毁掉*了一切…你打破了那扇门，现在诅咒也会冲着*我*来了。”她闭上双眼，开始对着自己的吊坠念念有词。

En: Cursed? But I thought the curse wasn't real.
Zh: “诅咒？但我以为诅咒不是真的啊。”

En: Oh, sorry, I didn't know about the curse.
Zh: “哦，抱歉，我不知道诅咒的事。”

En: Cursed? But curses aren't real, ma'am.
Zh: “诅咒？但是诅咒不是真的啊，夫人。”

En: Of course you didn't, you're nothing but a policeman. She shrugs, visibly dejected.
Zh: “你当然不是了，你只不过是个警察而已。”她耸耸肩，显然很沮丧。

En: Of course you would think that, you're nothing but a policeman. She shrugs, visibly dejected.
Zh: “你当然会这么想，你只不过是个警察而已。”她耸耸肩，显然很沮丧。

En: Her façade has dropped. Now you see the curiosity behind the fear.
Zh: 她的面纱掉了下来。现在你能看见恐惧背后的好奇心。

En: But... before we go on, tell me... did you encounter *the malignant Entity?* Her eyes narrow, as she whispers the name.
Zh: “但是…在我们继续之前…你有没有遇到那个*恶毒的存在*？”她眯起眼睛，低声说出了那个名字。

En: Of course you did, you're nothing but a policeman. She shrugs, visibly dejected.
Zh: “你当然是了，你只不过是个警察而已。”她耸耸肩，显然很沮丧。

En: Alright, you know what? I'm willing to let you investigate the Doomed Commercial Area. We are set on the path... there's little else to do.
Zh: “好吧，你知道吗？我愿意让你去调查那个诅咒商业区。我们已经上了同一条船…也没什么其他事可以做。”

En: Never mind, I had other questions.
Zh: “没关系，我还有其他问题。”

En: Flip through the pages.
Zh: 翻阅书页。

En: Madam, what's this book?
Zh: “夫人，这是什么书？”

En: A highly educational primer. It's exceptional for the young mind... and obviously, very popular.
Zh: “一本非常有教育意义的初级读物。对于年轻的小脑瓜来说有些超常了…而且很显然，十分受欢迎。”

En: It's exactly like a primer -- a word on every page relating to a letter of the alphabet.
Zh: 确实很像是初级读物——每一页上都有一个单词，跟字母表上的字母相对应。

En: Oh wow, that's cool. I want to become smarter.
Zh: “哇哦，太酷了吧，我想变得更聪明点。”

En: You're a grown man, though. It's a bit too late for that... this is a book for small children.
Zh: “不过，你已经是个成年人了。所以现在可能有点太迟了…这本书是给小孩子看的。”

En: Uh... you have kids? She shudders uncomfortably. "Okay, I guess. Enjoy it."
Zh: “呃…你有孩子吗？”她不自在地颤抖着。“好吧，我猜。好好享受吧。”

En: I want to buy this Primer.
Zh: 我想买这本初级读物。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: You see a colourful primer. The title reads: "A Primer for Small Kids."
Zh: 你看见一个彩色的初级读本。标题是：“儿童识字入门。”

En: I'm not sure what you're planning to get from this. It's meant for babies. Literal babies.
Zh: 我不确定你打算从里面得到些什么。这是给小婴儿看的——字面意义上的，小婴儿。

En: Steal the Primer.
Zh: 偷走初级读物。

En: Angry storekeeper stare-down.
Zh: 愤怒的店主盯的你不敢看她。

En: You peek at the storekeep, waiting for her to be distracted. When she's not looking, you deftly swipe the small booklet off the shelf and stuff it in your pocket.
Zh: 你偷偷盯着店主，等待她分心。当她没看到时，你熟练地把那本小册子从书架上取了下来，然后塞进自己的口袋。

En: Slowly, you move your hand towards the small book. Before you even can reach it, you're interrupted:
Zh: 你慢慢把手伸向那本小书。不过还没碰到，你就被打断了：

En: You now proudly own a primer book for small children which, to be honest, barely cost anything.
Zh: 现在，你骄傲地拥有了一本给小孩子看的初级读物，而且说实话，它根本花不了几个钱。

En: Sir, please, no touching the merchandise.
Zh: “先生，请不要乱碰商品。”

En: The lieutenant coughs sharply, then whispers: "Let's not. Defrauding small business owners is not our *thing*."
Zh: 警督用力咳了几声，然后低语到：“还是别了吧。诈骗小商家可不是我们的*菜*。”

En: Okay. Cool.
Zh: “行吧。”

En: The shelves are littered with romantic tales from across time and space.
Zh: 书架上散落着来自各个时空的浪漫小说。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Storekeep, what's on this shelf?
Zh: “店主，这个书架上有什么？”

En: Does anything more real, more *you* stand out?
Zh: 还有什么更真实，更*像你*的东西吗？

En: Your interest in tales of real woe is well known.
Zh: 你对现实悲剧故事的兴趣是众所周知的。

En: Look through the display of books.
Zh: 浏览陈列的书籍。

En: I want buy the 'Sixteen Days of Coldest April' book.
Zh: “我想买这本*四月最冷的那16天*。”

En: You'd have to buy the book to be sure. But the book cover is a wall of concrete buildings, with a black-and-white rainbow covering them. It's written by someone from a country where even the rainbows are black-and-white. So it does seem like it'll be a downer.
Zh: 你得买下这本书才能知道它究竟讲了些什么。书的封面是一堵混凝土建筑的墙，涂有黑白彩虹状图案。这本书的作者想必来自一个连彩虹都只有黑白两色的国家。估计读了会很沮丧。

En: Oh, such wonderful, beautiful stories. Stories of love! She sighs wistfully. "You should definitely buy these."
Zh: “哦，那是个美妙又精彩的故事，关于爱情的故事！”她激动地叹息到。“你绝对应该买下它。”

En: To be honest, I'm looking for something more *real*.
Zh: “老实说，我在找一些更*真实*的东西。”

En: No-no, sir, you don't want that. That's not proper romance... romance is about overcoming adversity and finding that *single millionaire* who wants to take care of you until the end of days.
Zh: “不，先生，你不想的。那不是真正的浪漫…浪漫说的应该是克服逆境，找到一位愿意照顾你直到天荒地老的*单身百万富翁*。”

En: Someone thought this was a romance story, but it's actually a thoroughly excruciating work of psychological realism.
Zh: 有人觉得这只是个浪漫故事，其实是一部折磨人心的心理现实主义作品。

En: You focus on the overwhelming mass of colourful novels to find anything of note, but it only ends up with your eyes aching from the strain.
Zh: 你把注意力集中在眼前大量色彩斑斓的小说上，想找到些真正值得注意的东西，最后只换来一双因为疲惫而刺痛的眼睛。

En: Huh, it's in the wrong shelf... Annette's been moving things around again, has she! That girl... She shakes her head. "No matter, I hope you enjoy... this."
Zh: “啊，这本放错书架了…安妮特老是喜欢把东西到处乱放！那个丫头…”她摇摇头。“不要紧的，希望你能喜欢…这个。”

En: As you focus on the overwhelming mass of colourful novels, a discrepancy catches your eye -- one of the books is not like the others.
Zh: 当你集中在眼前大量色彩斑斓的小说上时，一个不和谐的东西抓住了你的眼球——有一本书跟其他的不太一样。

En: On the cover of the book there's a wall of concrete buildings, with a black-and-white rainbow over them. It's written by someone from a country where even rainbows are black and white.
Zh: 书的封面是一栋混凝土建筑的高墙，上方跨越着一道黑白彩虹。这本书的作者来自于一个连彩虹都是黑白色的国度。

En: They also tend to feature chisel-jawed men in dark suits. It's all such... glitzy garbage.
Zh: 他们还倾向于刻画一些身着黑色西装、面容如雕刻般完美的男人。全都是些…浮夸的垃圾。

En: Being a rough, *hard-boiled* detective, you can only be pleased by something more real, more *authentic*.
Zh: 作为一个粗旷的*硬汉*警探，你只会被更加真实，更加*可信*的东西打动。

En: You see a foamy mass of pinks and reds... colourful covers filled with risqué imagery. These books are most oft adorned by women in skimpy clothes, thighs revealed... a few roses and sometimes even some pistols.
Zh: 你看见一大团粉色和红色…彩色的封面上充斥着不雅的意象。这些书大多装饰着衣着清凉，大腿裸露的女性…配上一些玫瑰花，有时甚至还有手枪。

En: Is there anything here that calls out to me?
Zh: 这里有什么在呼唤我吗？

En: I think I'd enjoy reading a few of these.
Zh: 我觉得自己应该喜欢看这些书吧。

En: No, you wouldn't. Being a rough, *hard-boiled* detective, you hope for something more real... more *authentic*.
Zh: 不，你不会的。作为一个粗旷的*硬汉*警探，你只会被更加真实，更加*可信*的东西打动。

En: A grey book has lost itself between all the romance. The title reads: 'Sixteen Days of Coldest April.'
Zh: 一本灰色的书被遗忘在了这片浪漫的海洋。标题是：“四月最冷的那16天。”

En: Why is it out of place?
Zh: 为什么它的位置放错了？

En: What's this all about?
Zh: 这是怎么回事？

En: Help, I don't want to do this...
Zh: 救命，我不想这样做…

En: Turn away, this is a bad idea.
Zh: 转身离开，这是个坏主意。
